## Session 519 — Backend LOW-severity cleanup batch — close 6 items (L-2, L-8, L-12, L-14, L-91, L-131) (2026-04-28)

**6 backend LOW-severity items closed in one PROMPT.md batch.** Four parallel build subagents on file-disjoint subsystems (Core / Materializer / Search-Links / GCal), four pipelined reviewers. All four reviewers returned PASS-on-correctness. One reviewer (L-91) added a 6-line clarifying comment inside `truncate_str` explaining the `nth(max_chars)` mechanic; one reviewer (L-131) added two regression-guard tests (`join_calendar_path_extends_existing_base_path`, `join_calendar_path_rejects_cannot_be_a_base_url`). Each subagent ran in its own `git worktree` so concurrent `cargo build` did not contend on the target dir — the previous session's "stash chaos" pattern was avoided cleanly.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **LOW findings:** 101 → 95 (6 items resolved).
- **Top-level open count (FEAT/MAINT/PERF/PUB/UX tracker):** 41 → 41 (LOW items live in the appended Backend Code Review block, not the top tracker).
- **Previously-resolved counter:** 608+ → 614+ across 486 sessions (latest session 519).

**Items closed (6):**

| Item | Subsystem / files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| L-2 | Core (`src-tauri/src/lib.rs`) | New module-level `fn log_or_zero(r: Result<i64, sqlx::Error>, ctx: &str) -> i64` near the top of `lib.rs`; emits `tracing::warn!(error = %e, ctx, "boot count query failed; treating as 0")` and returns `0`. Replaced four `.unwrap_or(0)` boot-count call sites with `log_or_zero(...)` using stable ctx tags `"fts_blocks_count"`, `"fts_indexable_block_count"`, `"block_tag_refs_count"`, `"btr_indexable_block_count"`. Unrelated `lines.iter().position(...).unwrap_or(0)` (markdown-template trim helper) preserved. New `#[cfg(test)] mod log_or_zero_tests` covers Ok-passthrough + `Err(sqlx::Error::PoolTimedOut) → 0`. |
| L-8 | Core (`src-tauri/src/db.rs`) | Added `sqlx::query("PRAGMA optimize").execute(&pool).await?;` inside the legacy `init_pool` fixture, immediately after `sqlx::migrate!("./migrations").run(&pool).await?;` — byte-similar to the production `init_pools` invocation that already runs the same statement. New `init_pool_runs_pragma_optimize` test seats the regression. |
| L-12 | Materializer (`src-tauri/src/materializer/coordinator.rs`) | `enqueue_foreground` no longer reads `tx.capacity()` *before* `send().await` (TOCTOU on the mpsc snapshot). New flow: `tx.try_send(task)` first; on `TrySendError::Full(task)` increment `fg_full_waits` exactly once and fall back to `tx.send(task).await`; `TrySendError::Closed` propagates as `AppError::Channel`. The `fg_full_waits` metric now correlates 1:1 with real wait events. Public signature unchanged. New test `enqueue_foreground_does_not_bump_fg_full_waits_when_capacity_available` covers the happy path; the optional "fill-channel-and-demonstrate-real-wait" test was skipped (inherently racy with a live consumer). |
| L-14 | Materializer (`src-tauri/src/materializer/handlers.rs`) | Promoted three misroute log lines from `warn` to `error` and changed three return values from `Ok(())` to `Err(AppError::Validation(_))` so the consumer's existing `fg_errors` / `bg_errors` counters fire on misroute: (i) fg catch-all `_ =>` arm in `handle_foreground_task`; (ii) `MaterializeTask::ApplyOp` arm in `handle_background_task`; (iii) both branches of the `MaterializeTask::BatchApplyOps` arm (populated + empty-batch). Original tracing fields (`?task`, `op_type`/`device_id`/`seq`, `batch_size`) preserved. **`debug_assert!` removed** in favor of plain `Err` + `error!` log — avoids the `#[should_panic]` ceremony in tests asserting the `Err` contract directly. Three existing tests updated (`handle_fg_unexpected`, `handle_fg_unexpected_reindex`, `handle_bg_unexpected_apply`); two new tests added (`handle_fg_rebuild_fts_index_returns_validation_err`, `handle_bg_unexpected_batch_apply_returns_validation_err`). |
| L-91 | Search & Links (`src-tauri/src/link_metadata/html_parser.rs`) | `truncate_str` body replaced with `s.char_indices().nth(max_chars).map(|(i,_)| &s[..i]).unwrap_or(s)`. The function now matches its name — char count, not byte count — so CJK / emoji input is no longer over-truncated by the 3:1 (or 4:1) byte-to-char ratio. Visibility bumped from `fn` to `pub(super)` to match the existing sibling-test convention (`extract_origin`, `extract_domain`, `resolve_url`). Reviewer added a 6-line inline comment inside the body explaining the `nth(max_chars)` mechanic (returns the byte index of the (max_chars+1)th char). Eight new `assert_eq!`-based tests cover ASCII shorter/equal/longer, CJK satisfied/exceeded, mixed ASCII + 4-byte SMP emoji, empty string, and `max_chars=0`. Pre-existing `parse_title_truncates_to_500_chars` / `parse_description_truncates_to_300_chars` continued to pass unchanged (their data was ASCII-only — bytes == chars). The unrelated `parse_title_chained_entity_known_limitation` test was untouched. |
| L-131 | GCal (`src-tauri/src/gcal_push/api.rs`) | New private `fn join_calendar_path(base_url: &str, calendar_id: &str, suffix: &[&str]) -> Result<String, AppError>` builds calendar / event URLs via `url::Url::parse` + `path_segments_mut().push(...)`, percent-encoding `calendar_id` and `event_id` against the path-segment encode set (`/`, `?`, `#` → `%2F`/`%3F`/`%23`). Five raw `format!(...)` URL constructions in `delete_calendar`, `insert_event`, `patch_event`, `delete_event`, `get_event` collapsed to helper calls; `create_dedicated_calendar` (the `/calendars` POST without an id) intentionally untouched. **No new dependencies** — `url = "2"` is already a direct dep. Existing wiremock tests continued to pass unchanged (`TEST_CAL_ID = "cal_ABCDEF"` is ASCII-safe, encoded path is byte-identical). Five new tests added (3 helper unit tests + 2 wiremock e2e); reviewer added two more (`join_calendar_path_extends_existing_base_path` pinning that the production `/calendar/v3` base path is preserved, `join_calendar_path_rejects_cannot_be_a_base_url` exercising the `mailto:`-style error branch). |

**Files touched (this session's batch — 8 modified):**

- Backend Core (2): `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, `src-tauri/src/db.rs`
- Backend Materializer (3): `src-tauri/src/materializer/{coordinator,handlers,tests}.rs`
- Backend Search & Links (2): `src-tauri/src/link_metadata/{html_parser,tests}.rs`
- Backend GCal (1): `src-tauri/src/gcal_push/api.rs`
- Docs (2): `REVIEW-LATER.md` (6 items removed; LOW count 101 → 95; previously-resolved counter bumped), `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry)

**Verification:** `prek run --all-files` → all hooks PASS (vitest 8500+, cargo nextest 3032 tests, biome / tsc / clippy / fmt / lychee / sqruff / cargo deny / cargo machete / sqlx prepare check / tauri-command-sanitize). Targeted runs during build/review:

- L-2 / L-8: `cargo nextest run -E 'package(agaric) and (test(/log_or_zero/) or test(/init_pool/))'` → 24 passed.
- L-12 / L-14: `cargo nextest run -E 'package(agaric) and (test(/handle_fg/) or test(/handle_bg/) or test(/enqueue_foreground/) or test(/fg_full/))'` → 12 passed; full materializer slice 161/161 passed.
- L-91: `cargo nextest run -E 'package(agaric) and test(/truncate/)'` → 31 passed.
- L-131: `cargo nextest run -E 'package(agaric) and (test(/calendar/) or test(/event/))'` → 103 passed.

Full-suite: `cargo nextest run --no-fail-fast` post-merge → **3032/3032 passed, 2 skipped** (the 2 skipped are pre-existing `#[ignore]` tests).

**Process notes:**

- **Worktree-per-subagent paid off.** Four `git worktree add --detach` checkouts under `/tmp/agaric-wt-{core,mat,search,gcal}` gave each parallel build subagent its own `target/` directory; no cross-batch stash chaos like session 518's "five subagents stashing each other's work" scenario. Cold-compile cost was ~5 min per worktree but ran in parallel; total wall-clock build time ≈ slowest subagent (5m32s) rather than 4× sequential.
- **Reviewer-applied micro-fixes preserved by file-copy merge.** Both reviewer additions (L-91 inline comment, L-131 two extra tests) landed in their respective worktrees during review. The orchestrator merged via `cp $WORKTREE/<file> $MAIN/<file>` after verifying clean git status in each worktree — simpler and more auditable than `git apply` / cherry-pick across worktrees with a clean main tree, especially when changes are file-disjoint.
- **`debug_assert!` removed in L-14.** The original recommendation in REVIEW-LATER suggested either `Err` + `error!` log OR `debug_assert!(false, ...)`. The builder went with the simpler `Err`+`error!` path because adding `debug_assert!(false, ...)` to a code path that tests *intentionally* exercise (to assert the misroute Err contract) would have forced every such test to use `#[should_panic]`. The rationale comment is in place at `handlers.rs:93-96` / `:925-926`. The misroute is still observable through the `error!` log and the consumer's `fg_errors` / `bg_errors` counters.
- **Pre-existing `cargo clippy --all-targets` failure noted but out-of-scope.** Two reviewers flagged `src-tauri/src/sync_files.rs:1561` (`(i % 256) as u8` — `clippy::cast_possible_truncation`) when running clippy with `--all-targets`. The prek hook at `prek.toml:327` runs `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` *without* `--all-targets`, so test-code lints don't block the commit and this pre-existing warning has shipped through several sessions. Filing as informational; not part of this batch.
- **Stash management — no new stashes.** The 4 stale stashes from session 518 are still present; not touched in this commit (per session 518's recommendation, they should be reviewed and dropped together rather than piecemeal).
- **Worktree cleanup.** All four `/tmp/agaric-wt-*` worktrees removed via `git worktree remove` after the merge; the main repo returned to a single-worktree state.

## Session 518 — All 5 LOW backend cleanup batches closed (MAINT-148..152) (2026-04-27)

**Five backend LOW-cleanup batches closed in one PROMPT.md run.** Five parallel build subagents on file-disjoint subsystems (materializer/cache/pagination/backlink, sync stack, MCP, GCal, misc features), five pipelined reviewers, two reviewer-applied inline fixes (cache/tags.rs migration missed by builder; MAINT-151(g) `_emitter` removal lost during stash chaos). The session was significantly more chaotic than session 517: subagents repeatedly stashed each other's work for build isolation, leading to multiple overlapping stashes and one nearly-lost file (mcp/dispatch.rs was deleted mid-session by MAINT-151's stash dance, recreated by MAINT-150's reapply). The orchestrator verified the final on-disk state matches each builder's intent before committing.

- All five LOW-priority backend cleanup batches now closed — the next-priority MAINT items are the larger architectural ones (MAINT-118, 119, 120, 122, 124, 125, 127, 128, 129, 130, 131, 142).

- Backend cache (8): `cache/{tags,pages,page_id,agenda,projected_agenda,block_tag_refs,block_links,mod}.rs`

**Verification:** `prek run --all-files` → all 34 hooks PASS (vitest 8546+, cargo nextest 3000+, biome / tsc / clippy / fmt / lychee / sqruff / cargo deny / cargo machete / sqlx prepare check / tauri-command-sanitize). One `cargo fmt` round-trip needed before final pass (5 long-line struct fields in `sync_protocol/types.rs::SyncMessage` enum got reflowed to single-line form, plus `peer_refs.rs` and `pairing.rs` doc-comment whitespace). The merged build was buildable (`cargo check --lib` passing) for most of the session despite parallel-batch chaos.

**Process notes:**

- **Working-tree chaos was the dominant story.** Five concurrent build subagents repeatedly stashed each other's in-flight files for build isolation, then `git stash pop`'d their own stashes — but those operations happened in interleaved windows where the stash content captured *other* batches' work too. Three subagents reported their edits were silently reverted at some point and had to be re-applied. `mcp/dispatch.rs` (a NEW file from MAINT-150) was inadvertently deleted by MAINT-151's stash-recovery dance and re-created by MAINT-150's later reapply. MAINT-151's sub-item (g) (`_emitter` removal in `classify_date_err` + `push_date`) was *entirely lost* during a stash recovery; the reviewer caught it and applied the inline fix. The orchestrator verified the final on-disk state via direct file reads + `cargo check --lib` before committing — all 5 batches' intent was preserved end-to-end despite the noise.
- **TODO for next session: parallel reviewers must NOT stash shared in-flight changes.** PROMPT.md should be updated to forbid `git stash` for build isolation in subagents. Better alternatives:
  1. Use `cargo nextest -E 'test(/regex/)'` filter syntax (the "package(agaric) and test(/foo/)" form) to scope tests within a multi-batch working tree.
  2. Use `cargo check --lib --quiet -p agaric` against a single binary if test compilation is broken from parallel work.
  3. Trust the orchestrator's full-prek run after merge — subagents don't need a green full build to verify their own subsystem changes.
- **Reviewer work was high-value.** Two of the five reviewers caught real defects: (i) MAINT-148's `cache/tags.rs` was missed by the builder for the `rebuild_with_timing` migration; reviewer applied the one-line fix. (ii) MAINT-151's `_emitter` removal was lost during stash chaos; reviewer re-applied the full removal across 5 sites in `connector.rs`. Without per-batch reviewers, both regressions would have shipped as silent inconsistencies.
- **MAINT-148(d) lifetime-constraint outcome.** The builder's first attempt at a fully generic sort helper (parameterized by both fetcher closure AND comparator) hit Rust lifetime issues with the borrowed `&[&str]` inside the future. The pragmatic resolution — keep per-column fetchers as discrete async fns and only generic-ify the comparator pass — is the correct trade-off: the comparator was the actual duplication anyway.
- **MAINT-149(a) latent bugfix.** While extracting `consume_binary_data`, the builder noticed it had no `size_bytes == 0` handling. Tracing the sender path showed `send_binary_chunked` *always* emits at least one frame (even for empty data, it sends a single empty `Binary` frame). The pre-fix `consume_binary_data`'s `while received < size_bytes` loop never iterates when `size_bytes == 0`, leaving the empty frame in the receive buffer to corrupt the next message. The fix drains exactly one empty frame when `size_bytes == 0`. Latent bug; whether anyone has hit it in practice depends on whether 0-byte attachments coincide with the unknown-attachment-id consume path.
- **MAINT-150(d) defer reasoning.** The schemars approach (compile-time link from `ListPagesArgs` struct to its JSON schema) would introduce a new dependency requiring user approval per AGENTS.md "Coupled Dependency Updates". Deferred to a future explicit decision; partial fix (2 equivalence tests pinning struct↔schema parity) is the bridging step.
- **MAINT-151(b) cleaner option chosen.** Between "Transport(String)" variant vs "documented `NO_RESPONSE: u16 = 0` constant", the builder picked the variant — cleaner because logs and `CycleOutcome` no longer print misleading "HTTP 0" for transport failures.
- **MAINT-152(a) macro is non-portable.** The `batch_insert_snapshot_rows!` macro accesses `tx` from the surrounding `apply_snapshot` body via Mixed Site hygiene. This works correctly for the function-local helper but means the macro is not reusable elsewhere. Acceptable given exactly one caller; flagged as a style note by reviewer.
- **Stale stash management** — at session-end the working tree had 4 stashes accumulated from inter-batch isolation:
  1. "More parallel batch work" (cache/tags.rs, snapshot/restore.rs, sync_daemon/{orchestrator,server}.rs)
  2. "Other batch parallel work" (error.rs, gcal_push/{api,connector}.rs, sync_files.rs)
  3. "WIP on main: 68a22b8 ..." (large in-flight dump from earlier in the session)
  4. "in-progress unrelated H-4/H-13" (pre-session 514 stash, kept across sessions)

  All 4 contain stale snapshots — every file they cover has since been re-edited and re-merged into the working tree. Recommend dropping all 4 in a follow-up cleanup; not done in this commit to avoid losing history if any of them happen to have unique content.

## Session 517 — Backend cleanup batch — close 5 disjoint MAINT items (MAINT-133, 136, 140, 141, 147) (2026-04-27)

**5 backend MAINT items closed in one PROMPT.md-style batch.** Five parallel build subagents on file-disjoint subsystems (gcal_push, mcp, snapshot, tag_inheritance, commands), five pipelined reviewers, two minor inline fixes from the reviewers, biome auto-fix + 3 clippy fixes from the orchestrator, single commit. One subagent surfaced a real data-loss bug (MAINT-133 reproduced end-to-end before fixing); another authored a byte-exact equivalence test that pins the SQL semantics (MAINT-141).

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **Top-level open count:** 51 → 46 (5 items resolved).
- **Planned work:** 48 → 43 (FEAT/MAINT/PERF/PUB).
- **Previously-resolved counter:** 598+ → 603+ across 484 sessions (latest session 517).
- **MAINT-151(h)** absorbed by MAINT-140's `kind_display` helper — sub-item removed from MAINT-151's description.
- **MAINT-142** unblocked (its precondition MAINT-141 closed); description updated to note macros file is a sibling that stays in place.

**Items closed (5):**

| Item | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| MAINT-140 | `src-tauri/src/gcal_push/connector.rs` (+88/-50) | `CycleOutcome::HardFailure(String)` → `HardFailure(GcalErrorKind)` enum-variant change. Extracted `kind_display(kind: &GcalErrorKind) -> String` helper at `connector.rs:483-495` and added `impl Display for CycleOutcome` so log lines stay byte-equivalent. The 5 explicit per-variant `HardFailure` constructions inside `classify_cycle_failure` collapsed to a single `Ok(HardFailure(kind.clone()))` after a side-effect-only `match` for emitter events. `classify_date_err`'s `Skipped` reason strings now thread through `kind_display` (closes MAINT-151(h)). One log-string drift: date-level `RateLimited` reason `"rate_limited: {n}ms"` → `"rate_limited: retry after {n}ms"` (now byte-aligned with the cycle path; no test asserts on the old format). 202/202 gcal_push tests still pass. |
| MAINT-136 | `src-tauri/src/mcp/{registry,summarise,tools_ro,tools_rw}.rs` | 15 `pub(crate) const TOOL_<NAME>: &str` constants in `registry.rs:58-74` (9 RO + 6 RW). Choice of constants over `ToolName` enum because every internal call site already matches against `&str` (JSON-RPC `params.name`, `parse_args`, summarise dispatch). ~76 production-side literal `"<tool_name>"` sites collapsed onto the 15 constants. `tools_ro::list_tool_descriptions()` + `tools_rw::list_tool_descriptions()` now exposed `pub(crate)` so tests can iterate without DB-pool setup. Privacy-guard test `privacy_guard_no_summariser_leaks_content_or_value_text` rewritten to iterate the live registry — `assert_ne!(summary, desc.name, ...)` drift detector fires when a tool is registered without a `summarise_<name>` arm. Reviewer empirically verified the drift detector by commenting out a `summarise()` arm; test failed with the expected clear message; reverted. 225/225 mcp tests pass. |
| MAINT-133 | `src-tauri/src/snapshot/{types,create,restore,tests}.rs`, `src-tauri/.sqlx/query-{23fb4df...→a7096c9f...}.json` | **Data-loss bug confirmed and fixed.** Phase 1 wrote a failing round-trip test that seeded a conflict block with `conflict_type='Text'`, ran `create_snapshot` → `apply_snapshot`, and asserted the column survived — assertion failed with `got None, expected Some("Text")`, confirming the bug is reachable end-to-end. Phase 2 added `conflict_type: Option<String>` (with `#[serde(default)]` — required because ciborium hard-errors on missing CBOR keys without it) to `BlockSnapshot`, included it in the SELECT list at `create.rs:21`, bound it in the INSERT at `restore.rs:135-154`, and bumped `SCHEMA_VERSION` 2 → 3. `codec.rs:26` already accepts `version <= SCHEMA_VERSION` so the bump is forward-compatible. Phase 3 added a backward-compat test: hand-crafted a v2-shape CBOR blob (parallel `BlockSnapshotV2` / `SnapshotTablesV2` / `SnapshotDataV2` struct trio matching the pre-fix shape exactly), encoded via the production `ciborium::into_writer` + `zstd::encode_all` path, decoded via real `decode_snapshot`, asserted `conflict_type.is_none()` (defaulted via `serde(default)`). 19 in-test `BlockSnapshot { ... }` ctors gained `conflict_type: None` (all on `is_conflict: 0` blocks — no semantics lost). Reviewer added one stale-comment fix at `tests.rs:2418`. 109 → 111 snapshot tests pass; 241/241 cross-subsystem (snapshot/merge/restore/sync_daemon::snapshot) pass. |
| MAINT-141 | NEW `src-tauri/src/tag_inheritance_macros.rs` (517L), `src-tauri/src/tag_inheritance.rs` (1324L → ~1233L, -91), `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` (+1 module declaration) | New sibling file with `MAX_TAG_INHERITANCE_DEPTH = 100` constant + 6 `#[macro_export]` macros covering 4 conceptual shapes: `tag_inh_descendants_active!()`, `tag_inh_subtree_active!()`, `tag_inh_subtree_unfiltered!()` (documented invariant-#9 exception used by purge-after-soft-delete), `tag_inh_ancestors_walk!(seed_depth)` (parameterized 0/1 for `nearest_ancestor` ranking), `tag_inh_descendant_tags_full!()`, `tag_inh_tagged_descendants_in_subtree!()`. All 11 raw `WITH RECURSIVE` blocks in `tag_inheritance.rs` replaced by 14 macro invocations across 11 logical sites; all 14 literal `depth < 100` bounds eliminated from the file. Built-in `macros_match_pre_maint141_sql_byte_for_byte` test asserts each macro's expansion matches a hardcoded copy of the pre-refactor SQL string after whitespace normalization — makes future drift impossible without flagging itself. Plus 5 other in-file macro tests covering depth-literal-matches-constant, descendant-walks-filter-is_conflict, active-walks-filter-deleted_at, ancestors-walk-arms-differ-only-in-seed-depth, macros-are-cte-bodies-only. Public API unchanged (8 `pub async fn` signatures preserved). `lib.rs` ordering: `tag_inheritance_macros` declared BEFORE `tag_inheritance` so `#[macro_export]` macros are visible at crate root before consumers see them. Zero `.sqlx/` regen needed (file uses runtime `sqlx::query()`, not the compile-time `query!` macro). 23 → 29 tag_inheritance tests; 161/161 materializer + 102/102 agenda still pass. **Reviewer race window incident:** the first reviewer ran during a window where a parallel reviewer had stashed the macros file for build isolation; reviewer reported the file as missing. Re-launched the review with stash-aware preconditions; second reviewer verified everything correctly. The stash mechanism for build isolation needs a different shape going forward (TODO: parallel reviewers should not stash shared files). |
| MAINT-147 | `src-tauri/src/commands/{blocks/crud.rs,blocks/mod.rs,bug_report.rs,compaction.rs,drafts.rs,gcal.rs,link_metadata.rs,properties.rs,sync_cmds.rs}`, NEW `scripts/check-tauri-command-sanitize.mjs` (~200L), `prek.toml` (new hook), `src/lib/bindings.ts` (regenerated downstream) | 9 of 11 sub-items DONE; 2 SKIPPED with sound rationale (the `dispatch_background_for_*` helpers no longer exist post-MAINT-112, replaced by `CommandTx::commit_and_dispatch`). DONE items: (a) `purge_descendants_table!()` macro replacing 10 of 15 uniform `DELETE FROM ... WHERE block_id IN (SELECT id FROM descendants)` blocks (5 variant cases left inline). (b) `find_prev_edit_in_tx(conn: &mut sqlx::SqliteConnection, block_id: &str) -> Result<Option<(String, i64)>, AppError>` extracted; `crud.rs:449` and `drafts.rs:87` delegate. (c) Pre-commit script flags any `#[tauri::command]` with `Result<_, AppError>` return and `?` propagation but no `.map_err(sanitize_internal_error)`. 7-entry allowlist documents pre-existing exemptions (`logging.rs:get_log_dir`, 6× `mcp.rs:*` commands) explicitly forbidding new additions. New `tauri-command-sanitize` hook in `prek.toml` filtered to `^src-tauri/src/commands/.*\\.rs$`. Bonus fix: `bug_report.rs:collect_bug_report_metadata` was unsanitized, now wraps with `.map_err(super::sanitize_internal_error)`. (e) `validate_reserved_property_value(def_row_present, key, value, defaults)` extracted; `set_todo_state_inner` and `set_priority_inner` delegate. Helper takes `bool` (not `def_row`) so each call site keeps its literal-keyed `sqlx::query!` lookup → no `.sqlx/` regen needed. (g) `commands/properties.rs` import normalization: 2 `super::sanitize_internal_error` references → bare `sanitize_internal_error` after `use super::sanitize_internal_error;`. (h) `bug_report.rs` constants referenced by name in docstrings + comments (`MAX_FILE_BYTES`, `MAX_LINE_BYTES`, `MAX_ROLLED_AGE_DAYS`, `RECENT_ERRORS_CAP`). (i) `RedactionContext { home, device_id, gcal_email, peer_device_ids }` struct collapses `redact_line` / `redact_log` 4-positional-arg signatures to a single context arg; 1 production + 11 test call sites updated. (j) `// SAFETY:` comments at both `cast_signed()` sites in `commands/compaction.rs` (constant-bound case + IPC-validated case). (k) Doc comments on 12 undocumented `#[tauri::command]` wrappers in `link_metadata.rs`, `gcal.rs`, `bug_report.rs`, `sync_cmds.rs`. Doc-comment additions propagated through tauri-specta to `src/lib/bindings.ts` — regenerated via `cargo test --lib specta_tests::ts_bindings_up_to_date`. 509/509 commands tests pass; full suite 3003/3003. |

**Files touched (this session's batch — 26 modified + 2 new + 1 .sqlx delete):**

- Backend (15 modified .rs): `src-tauri/src/{commands/{blocks/{crud,mod},bug_report,compaction,drafts,gcal,link_metadata,properties,sync_cmds},gcal_push/connector,lib,mcp/{registry,summarise,tools_ro,tools_rw},snapshot/{create,restore,tests,types},tag_inheritance}.rs`
- New backend file: `src-tauri/src/tag_inheritance_macros.rs` (MAINT-141 macros)
- `.sqlx/` cache: 1 deleted + 1 added (MAINT-133's snapshot SELECT shape change)
- Frontend: `src/lib/bindings.ts` (regenerated downstream of MAINT-147k doc comments)
- Tooling: `prek.toml` (MAINT-147c new hook), `scripts/check-tauri-command-sanitize.mjs` (NEW)
- Docs: `REVIEW-LATER.md` (5 items removed; MAINT-151(h) sub-item removed; MAINT-142 description updated to drop the now-satisfied MAINT-141 dependency), `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry)

**Verification:** `prek run --all-files` → all 34 hooks PASS (vitest 8546 tests, cargo nextest 3003+ tests, biome / tsc / clippy / fmt / lychee / sqruff / cargo deny / cargo machete / sqlx prepare check / new tauri-command-sanitize). Three orchestrator-side fixes were needed after the merge: `cargo fmt` reflowed long string literals in `tag_inheritance_macros.rs`; `cargo clippy` flagged 3 new lints from the merged code (auto-deref `&mut **tx` → `&mut tx` at 2 sites, `defaults.iter().any(|d| *d == value)` → `defaults.contains(&value)` at 1 site) — all fixed in 3 one-line edits. Biome `--write` was needed once for the new sanitize script (regex `[^]` non-portable form → `[\s\S]`, `while ((m = re.exec(src)) !== null)` assignment-in-expression → moved out of condition).

**Process notes:**

- **5-way disjoint backend split worked cleanly.** Each subagent owned a self-contained subsystem (gcal_push/connector.rs, mcp/, snapshot/, tag_inheritance.rs + new macros file, commands/) — no file conflicts despite five subagents writing to the same working tree concurrently. Diff-isolation per subagent was clean.
- **Reviewer race window for MAINT-141.** A parallel reviewer (MAINT-136's) stashed concurrent files including `tag_inheritance_macros.rs` to isolate `cargo nextest run mcp`; that reviewer's `git stash pop` succeeded, but the brief mid-stash window was when MAINT-141's reviewer ran. The reviewer reported the macros file as missing; investigation showed the file existed, was just transiently stashed. Re-launched MAINT-141's review with stash-aware preconditions (`ls -la` of the macros file + `git stash list` count check) at the start. The second reviewer verified everything correctly. **Process improvement:** parallel reviewers should not stash shared in-flight changes for build isolation; instead use `cargo nextest -E 'test(/foo/)'` filters or run `cargo build` only against a single binary (`-p agaric --bin agaric`).
- **MAINT-133 was a real bug.** The review note "before shipping a fix, confirm the bug is reachable" was the right framing — the Phase 1 failing test confirmed the data-loss path. Without that confirmation step, we'd have shipped a `SCHEMA_VERSION` bump for a phantom regression. Build subagent also caught the spec's claim about "the decoder tolerates missing fields" was conditional on `#[serde(default)]` — without it, ciborium hard-errors on missing keys, which would have shipped a worse regression than the original bug.
- **MAINT-141's byte-exact equivalence test is the right shape for SQL macro refactors.** Anyone editing a macro body and accidentally diverging from the pre-refactor SQL gets caught loudly by `macros_match_pre_maint141_sql_byte_for_byte`. The literal `100` is duplicated across 7 macro bodies (sqlx requires `&'static str` literals for `concat!`-composed SQL, so we can't embed the constant), but `macro_depth_literal_matches_constant` test guards drift between the constant and the macro literals.
- **`#[serde(default)]` is mandatory for additive struct fields read by ciborium.** A note for the next snapshot extension: ciborium's struct deserializer treats a missing CBOR map key as a hard error unless the field carries `#[serde(default)]`. The pre-existing `todo_state` / `priority` / `due_date` / `scheduled_date` fields all have it; the new `conflict_type` followed the same pattern.
- **The new `tauri-command-sanitize` hook is now part of prek.** Future `#[tauri::command]` wrappers returning `Result<_, AppError>` and using `?` propagation must end with `.map_err(sanitize_internal_error)` — the IPC boundary never leaks bare `AppError::Sqlx` / `AppError::Internal` to the renderer. The 7-entry allowlist documents pre-existing exemptions (mostly `mcp.rs` commands and `logging.rs:get_log_dir`) and explicitly forbids new additions.
- **MAINT-147 sub-items (d) and (f) were stale.** The cited `dispatch_background_for_*` helpers no longer exist; MAINT-112 phase B replaced them with `CommandTx::commit_and_dispatch`. The build subagent correctly noted "the duplication this sub-item targeted has already been fixed" and skipped both. Items in REVIEW-LATER can drift; the SKIP-with-rationale pattern is the right way to handle that.
- **Specta bindings regenerate on doc-comment changes.** MAINT-147(k) added 12 new `///` doc comments on `#[tauri::command]` wrappers; tauri-specta's TypeScript generator picks these up, so `src/lib/bindings.ts` regenerated. This caused an initial `specta_tests::ts_bindings_up_to_date` failure that was resolved by running `cargo test --release --lib specta_tests -- --ignored` to write the new bindings.

## Session 516 — Frontend test-quality cluster — close all 5 remaining TEST items + Windows release fix (2026-04-27)

**Two threads in one session.** First, a Windows release flake that broke 0.1.16's APK upload was diagnosed and fixed (cache post-step on `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2` exiting non-zero with no log output, cascading to skip `android-build-and-release`). Second, a PROMPT.md-style batch closed all 5 remaining frontend test-quality items (TEST-56, TEST-61, TEST-62, TEST-63, TEST-64) — 5 parallel build subagents on disjoint file sets, 5 pipelined reviewers, one merge with prek-driven biome auto-fix.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **Top-level open count:** 56 → 51 (5 items resolved).
- **Frontend test-quality items:** 5 → 0 (entire TEST section removed; the backend TEST-40..51 block had already closed).
- **Previously-resolved counter:** 593+ → 598+ across 483 sessions (latest session 516).

**Items closed (5):**

| Item | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| TEST-56 | `src/hooks/__tests__/{useBlockTags,useBlockDatePicker,useBlockProperties,useBlockSlashCommands,useBlockMultiSelect,useBlockAttachments}.test.ts` | Captured `originalOnNewAction = useUndoStore.getState().onNewAction` once per file (module-top); added `afterEach(() => useUndoStore.setState({...getState(), onNewAction: originalOnNewAction, pages: new Map() }))`. Each file already had per-test `setState({...onNewAction: vi.fn()})` spy installs — those still install the spy for that test; the new `afterEach` guarantees restoration so a future test added between existing sites can rely on the real `onNewAction` semantics again. `pages: new Map()` reset is also part of the restore — closes the within-file leak that the original finding flagged. |
| TEST-61 | 18 sites across 14 hook + lib test files (`useBlock{Collapse,Dn,KeyboardHandlers,Navigation,Zoom,DatePicker,Properties,SlashCommands,MultiSelect,Tags}` + `useDuePanelData{,.helpers}` + `useQuery{Execution,Sorting}` + lib `agenda-{filters,sort}`, `query-result-utils`, `tree-utils`) | Replaced every local `function makeBlock(...)` / `makeFlatBlock` / `makeTagBlock` / `mkBlock` / `mkFlat` factory with `import { makeBlock } from '../../__tests__/fixtures'` + override-bag call sites. The shared `makeBlock` returns a `FlatBlock` with all 14 fields defaulted; every override-bag rewrite preserves the previous test's expected fields (verified field-by-field by both build and review subagents). Special cases: `useBlockTags.makeTagBlock` was also missing `page_id: null` (real defect — the shared factory now sets it explicitly at every call site); `tree-utils.test.ts` keeps thin positional wrappers `mkBlock(id, parentId, position, content)` / `mkFlat(...)` that delegate to `makeBlock({...})` with NO field-level defaults inline. Net 157 lines removed from the touched files. |
| TEST-62 | `src/hooks/__tests__/{useBlockSwipeActions,useBlockTouchLongPress,useDeepLinkRouter}.test.ts` | Replaced 3 hand-rolled `function renderHook<T>(...)` helpers (each ~30 lines: `createRoot` + `createElement` + `act` plus `IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT = true` ceremony) with a single-line `import { renderHook } from '@testing-library/react'`. Deleted unused `createRoot`, `Root`, `createElement` imports plus `beforeEach`'s `IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT` set (the library configures this internally). Removed the misleading "Minimal renderHook helper (matches existing project pattern)" comment in `useDeepLinkRouter.test.ts` — the project pattern is the library import (47 other hook/component test files use it). Test bodies already destructured `{ result, unmount }`, the same shape testing-library returns, so no caller changes were needed. |
| TEST-63 | `src/hooks/__tests__/useBlockCollapse.test.ts`, `src/hooks/__tests__/useBlockDatePicker.test.ts` (combined with TEST-56 fix), `src/lib/__tests__/{bug-report-events,relaunch-app,date-utils}.test.ts` | Five sites where global state (jsdom `window`, `localStorage`, `window.location`, fake timers, zustand store seed) was mutated with the restore inline at the bottom of the test body — moved to `afterEach`. `useBlockCollapse.test.ts`: replaced the custom `mockStorage` object + `Object.defineProperty(globalThis, 'localStorage', ...)` with jsdom's real `localStorage` + `vi.spyOn(Storage.prototype, 'setItem')` (matches `useAgendaPreferences.test.ts:10-13`'s pattern), and `afterEach(() => { localStorage.clear(); vi.restoreAllMocks() })`. `useBlockDatePicker.test.ts`: captured `originalSpaceState = useSpaceStore.getState()` once and added `afterEach(() => useSpaceStore.setState(originalSpaceState))`. `bug-report-events.test.ts`: captured `originalWindow` and restored in `afterEach`. `relaunch-app.test.ts`: captured `originalLocation` and restored via `Object.defineProperty(window, 'location', { configurable: true, writable: true, value: originalLocation })` in `afterEach` (`vi.restoreAllMocks` doesn't reverse `defineProperty` replacements, so the explicit restore was required). `date-utils.test.ts`: moved the `getTodayString` describe's inline `vi.useFakeTimers()` / `vi.useRealTimers()` to `beforeEach` / `afterEach` matching sibling describes. |
| TEST-64 | `src/editor/__tests__/property-picker.test.ts` (vacuous test deleted), `src/hooks/__tests__/useUndoShortcuts.test.ts` (4× `setTimeout(10)` → `await Promise.resolve(); await Promise.resolve()` microtask drain), `src/lib/__tests__/agenda-sort.test.ts` (midnight-race fix: `vi.useFakeTimers()` + `vi.setSystemTime(new Date('2025-01-15T12:00:00'))` — local-time, no `Z`, to be timezone-safe for `groupByDate`'s `getFullYear/getMonth/getDate`), `src/components/__tests__/{ImageResizeToolbar,PairingDialog,BlockInlineControls,BlockPropertyDrawer,Calendar}.test.tsx`, `src/components/ui/__tests__/sonner.test.tsx`, `src/lib/__tests__/{attachment-utils,tauri-mock}.test.ts` | Nine mechanical hygiene fixes: deleted the `property-picker.test.ts:288-299` vacuous "cancellation path" test (tautology — `onSelect` could not fire because no test-body code path invoked `command`); microtask-drain replaced 4 brittle `setTimeout(10)` waits in `useUndoShortcuts.test.ts`; `ImageResizeToolbar.test.tsx`'s 2 `fireEvent.click` sites converted to `userEvent.setup() + user.click(...)` per `src/__tests__/AGENTS.md`; `PairingDialog.test.tsx` `expect(focusSpy).toHaveBeenCalled()` tightened to `toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)`; `sonner.test.tsx`'s `expect(container).toBeTruthy()` (always-truthy tautology) replaced with `expect(container.querySelector('section, ol, ul')).not.toBeNull()`; `attachment-utils.test.ts` strengthened with concrete URL assertion `expect(result).toBe('asset://localhost/%2Fpath%2Fto%2Ffile.png')` matching the `convertFileSrc` mock that uses `encodeURIComponent`; `tauri-mock.test.ts` added daily-page symmetry check `expect(titles.some((t) => /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/.test(t as string))).toBe(true)`; axe `waitFor` raised to `{ timeout: 5000 }` on the 3 plausibly cold-load sites (`BlockInlineControls.test.tsx:535` overflow-button + Tooltip async positioning, `BlockPropertyDrawer.test.tsx:231` drawer-open-with-properties, `Calendar.test.tsx:137`). The reviewer for this batch verified per-file the picked sites really were cold-load and the un-picked sibling axe sites in `BlockInlineControls.test.tsx` (default-state at 591, with-children at 599) are synchronous renders that don't need the bump. |

**Files touched (35 modified test files only — no production code):**

- Hook tests (19): `src/hooks/__tests__/{useBlockAttachments,useBlockCollapse,useBlockDatePicker,useBlockDnD,useBlockKeyboardHandlers,useBlockMultiSelect,useBlockNavigation,useBlockProperties,useBlockSlashCommands,useBlockSwipeActions,useBlockTags,useBlockTouchLongPress,useBlockZoom,useDeepLinkRouter,useDuePanelData,useDuePanelData.helpers,useQueryExecution,useQuerySorting,useUndoShortcuts}.test.ts`
- Lib tests (9): `src/lib/__tests__/{agenda-filters,agenda-sort,attachment-utils,bug-report-events,date-utils,query-result-utils,relaunch-app,tauri-mock,tree-utils}.test.ts`
- Component tests (5): `src/components/__tests__/{BlockInlineControls,BlockPropertyDrawer,Calendar,ImageResizeToolbar,PairingDialog}.test.tsx`
- UI primitive test (1): `src/components/ui/__tests__/sonner.test.tsx`
- Editor test (1): `src/editor/__tests__/property-picker.test.ts`
- Docs (2): `REVIEW-LATER.md` (5 items removed; counts updated; entire `## TEST` section deleted), `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry)

Plus the orchestrator-side workflow fix from earlier in the session (single commit, separate from this batch):

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — `save-if: false` added to both `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2` invocations (desktop matrix + Android job) so cache-save flakes can never block a release. Released as commit `4229115` and recovered the 0.1.16 APK by retagging at the new commit (run 25009988305).

**Verification:** `prek run --all-files` → all 33 hooks PASS (vitest 8546 tests across 318 test files, cargo nextest 3000+ tests, full clippy / fmt / biome / tsc / lychee / sqruff / cargo deny / cargo machete / sqlx prepare check). Biome `--write` was needed once after the merge to apply formatter + import-sort auto-fixes to 7 of the 35 files (long array literals reflowed, import order normalized in `useBlockCollapse.test.ts` and `useBlockDnD.test.ts`); after that, `npx biome check` exits 0 with only 9 pre-existing `suppressions/unused` + cognitive-complexity warnings on files this batch did NOT touch (`SearchablePopover.tsx`, `AttachmentRenderer.tsx`, `JournalPage.tsx`, `JournalPage.test.tsx`, `JournalCalendarDropdown.test.tsx`, `test-setup.ts`).

**Process notes:**

- **5-way subagent split with no file overlap.** TEST-56 + TEST-61 + TEST-63 had heavy file-set overlap (the same hook test files were targets of multiple items). Rather than worktree, the orchestrator partitioned the 35-file work surface into 5 disjoint subsets — A (6 hook files: TEST-56 + TEST-61 + TEST-63 partial), B (9 hook files: TEST-61 + TEST-63 partial), C (4 lib files: TEST-61 + TEST-64 partial), D (3 hook + 3 lib: TEST-62 + TEST-63 partial), E (10 component/editor/lib: TEST-64). All 5 ran in parallel in the main tree without conflicts.
- **Pipelined reviews.** As builders A, C, D, E finished (close together), their reviewers were launched immediately in parallel rather than waiting for all 5 builders. Reviewer B was the only sequential one (B was the longest builder, finishing last). Review wall-clock was bounded by the slowest reviewer (~2-3 min), not 5×reviewer-cost serial.
- **Zero inline reviewer fixes.** All 5 reviewers approved their respective batches without needing to apply fixes. The reviewer for E pre-emptively predicted the biome formatter would flag the `property-picker.test.ts:287` extra blank line — confirmed when prek ran post-merge and biome `--write` reflowed it.
- **9 pre-existing biome warnings surfaced but did not block.** The first prek run failed only on biome formatter errors caused by my long-array literal call sites; the 9 `suppressions/unused` + cognitive-complexity warnings existed on `main` before my changes (verified by stashing the working tree and re-running biome check on `4229115`). They are pre-existing tech debt from a biome bump that retired/renamed `lint/performance/noDelete` (and similar). Biome itself exits 0 on warnings, so the prek hook passes once the formatter errors are fixed.
- **Build subagents reported good observations the prompt didn't ask for.** Subagent C noticed the spec's claim that "the rest of the file already uses `vi.useFakeTimers()`" was wrong for `agenda-sort.test.ts` (true for `agenda-filters.test.ts` only) and added the fake-timer setup fresh, mirroring `agenda-filters.test.ts`'s pattern. Subagent C also flagged that the spec's `'2025-01-15T12:00:00Z'` UTC suffix would break in UTC+13/+14 timezones because `groupByDate` uses local `getFullYear/getMonth/getDate`; switched to `'2025-01-15T12:00:00'` (no `Z`). Both decisions were the right shape, not boilerplate compliance.
- **Reviewer E's site-selection disposition was the main judgment call.** TEST-64's spec line numbers for `BlockInlineControls.test.tsx:519-525` were stale; the closest axe site in the same nested describe was line 535 (overflow button — async Tooltip positioning). Builder E applied the timeout-bump there and left two other axe sites (default-state at 591, with-children at 599) untouched per the spec's "only these 3 sites" constraint. Reviewer E verified those untouched sites are synchronous renders without portals/tooltips, so they are not plausibly cold-load — the picked site was correct.
- **Windows release fix unrelated to this batch but resolved in same session.** The 0.1.16 release (commit `81c4b1f`) lost its Android APK because the Windows job's post-step cache save flaked and the dependency `android-build-and-release` (which `needs:` the desktop matrix) was skipped. Fixed in commit `4229115` with `save-if: false` on both `rust-cache@v2` steps. Old draft + tag deleted (`gh release delete 0.1.16 --cleanup-tag`), retagged at the fix commit, new release run is in flight at run 25009988305 — Windows + Android jobs are now decoupled from cache flakes.

## Session 515 — Tier 1 + Tier 2 batch — close 8 items from the automated-validation exception audit (2026-04-27)

**8 of 10 confirmed findings from the previous session's automated-validation exception audit (MAINT-153..MAINT-162) closed in one PROMPT.md-style batch.** Four parallel build subagents grouped by domain (Rust cast/dead-code; vitest test improvements; Tauri-mock handlers; config + script tweaks), four pipelined review subagents, plus an orchestrator-side fix for one tiny review finding. Two items deferred with explicit rationale: MAINT-159 (its own description says "Land AFTER MAINT-128"), MAINT-162 (M-cost architectural change requiring manual NVDA/VoiceOver testing).

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **Top-level open count:** 64 → 56 (8 items resolved).
- **Planned work:** 56 → 48 (FEAT/MAINT/PERF/PUB).
- **Previously-resolved counter:** 585+ → 593+ across 482 sessions (latest session 515).
- **Items remaining from the audit:** MAINT-159 (defer behind MAINT-128), MAINT-162 (defer pending AT testing).

**Items closed (8):**

| Item | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| MAINT-153 | `knip.json`, `src/lib/config.ts`, `prek.toml` | Deleted the entire `ignoreBinaries` array (13 SPDX license-identifier strings that knip itself reported as unused — they were misuse of the field, which is for CLI executables, not licenses). Removed `src/lib/tauri.ts` from `ignore` (also flagged by knip). Moved `better-npm-audit`, `depcheck`, `license-checker` to `ignoreDependencies` (they're dev-only CLI tools used via prek, like `tailwindcss` / `markdownlint-cli2`). Deleted unused `BugTrackerConfig` type from `src/lib/config.ts:19` (zero references elsewhere). Dropped `--no-exit-code` from the prek `knip` hook so knip is a hard gate again. `npx knip` now reports 0 issues. |
| MAINT-154 | `e2e/helpers.ts` | Replaced the dangerously broad `IGNORED_CONSOLE_ERROR_PATTERNS = [/favicon/, /Failed to load resource/]` with a single tight pattern: `/Failed to load resource:.*\/favicon\.ico/`. The old second pattern silenced every 4xx/5xx chromium error (broken `<img src>`, missing fonts, attachment URLs, mock 404s, backend 4xx in production); the new pattern matches only the literal favicon-404 chromium message. Smoke spec passes 3/3, no previously-silenced 404 surfaced. |
| MAINT-155 | `src-tauri/src/{cache/projected_agenda,commands/agenda,commands/compaction,db,fts/search,hash,materializer/{consumer,coordinator,dispatch,metrics,tests},recovery/boot,recurrence/{compute,parser},snapshot/create,sync_files}.rs` | Replaced 20 of 25 `#[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation/sign_loss/possible_wrap)]` sites with `try_into().expect("invariant: …")` (or saturating `try_from(…).unwrap_or(MAX)` for millis-since-epoch where truncation is acceptable). Each `expect()` documents the invariant AND fails loudly if it's violated, instead of silently wrapping. Kept 5 `#[allow]` sites for `f64 → i64`/`usize` casts (std has no `TryFrom<f64>` for integer types and the prek clippy gate is `-D warnings`); rewrote each comment to explicitly call out "no `TryFrom` in std" + the safety invariant (non-negative, finite, whole numbers, SQLite REAL affinity). The 16 module-level `#![allow(clippy::cast_possible_wrap)]` annotations in `benches/*.rs` were left untouched (deliberately lax for loop indices in non-shipping code, per the REVIEW-LATER scope). Module-level allow on `sync_files.rs` was removed; 21 inline `slice.len() as i64/u64` casts in the test mod were converted individually. |
| MAINT-156 | `src-tauri/src/{gcal_push/connector,mcp/server,sync_daemon/mod}.rs` | Deleted 4 dead `#[allow(dead_code)]` "future feature" helpers: `gcal_push/connector.rs:1182,1221,1226` (`set_fixed_calendar_id`, `call_count`, `remote_event_count` — all "Reserved for future integration tests" with no callers) and `mcp/server.rs:381` (`wrap_tool_result_error` — claimed FEAT-4j wire contract, FEAT-4j unshipped; deleted along with its multi-line FEAT-4j justification block, its only test `wrap_tool_result_error_shape_has_is_error_true`, and a dangling cross-reference in `app_error_to_jsonrpc`'s doc comment). Narrowed `sync_daemon/mod.rs:86` from broad `#[allow(dead_code)]` to `#[cfg_attr(not(test), allow(dead_code))]` — the `handle: Option<JoinHandle<()>>` field IS read in 7 sites in `sync_daemon/tests.rs` (`daemon.handle.is_some()`, `let handle = daemon.handle;` for awaiting shutdown), so the test-conditional suppression is correct. |
| MAINT-157 | `src/components/__tests__/{ConflictListItem,ConflictTypeRenderer}.test.tsx` | Removed all 7 stale `'color-contrast': { enabled: false }` axe overrides. Rationale was "axe-core's static color analysis doesn't account for CSS custom properties (OKLch tokens)" — but axe-core 4.7 (Apr 2023) added CSS-custom-property resolution; this repo runs axe-core 4.11.3. After removal: 54/54 tests still pass — the suppressions were indeed stale, not load-bearing. No real WCAG-AA contrast bug surfaced in the OKLch tokens. |
| MAINT-158 | `src/components/__tests__/{FeatureErrorBoundary,ErrorBoundary}.test.tsx` | Added 16 count + message assertion pairs across 9 sites that previously bare-spied `console.error` without ever asserting the call count. Each `vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation(() => {})` now binds to a named variable, asserts `toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2)` (confirmed empirically: 1 call from React 19's caught-error logging + 1 from `logger.error` chaining to `console.error`), asserts `toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.stringContaining('<expected>'))`, and `mockRestore()`s. `ErrorBoundary.test.tsx` additionally needed `_resetRateLimits()` (internal logger helper, also used by `logger.test.ts`) called in `beforeEach` because the boundary always logs the same `module:message` key — without the reset, the logger's 5/min rate limit suppresses calls 6+ and the deterministic `toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2)` becomes flaky on the 6th throwing test. `useBatchCounts.test.ts:132` was inspected and left unchanged (it already had a named binding, the correct zero-call assertion, and `mockRestore()`). |
| MAINT-160 | `src/lib/tauri-mock/handlers.ts`, `src/lib/__tests__/tauri-mock.test.ts`, `scripts/check-tauri-mock-parity.mjs` | Added 15 mock handlers for the previously-unmocked Tauri commands (5 GCal: `get_gcal_status` returning the disconnected default with the matching Rust default `privacy_mode: 'full'`, `force_gcal_resync`, `disconnect_gcal` — accepts but ignores `deleteCalendar`, `set_gcal_window_days`, `set_gcal_privacy_mode`; 4 MCP RO + 4 MCP RW returning the disabled status / socket-path / set-enabled / disconnect-all shapes; `trash_descendant_counts` with a documented semantic-divergence note vs Rust's cascade-batch JOIN-on-`deleted_at` behaviour AND an `is_conflict` filter to honour AGENTS.md invariant #9; `quick_capture_block` reusing the existing daily-page lookup + `pushOp('create_block', …)` pattern). Emptied `KNOWN_UNMOCKED` from 15 entries to 0; left a comment explaining MAINT-160 cleared it and that re-additions need a justification. Added 17 happy-path tests in `tauri-mock.test.ts` (test count 194 → 211). |
| MAINT-161 | `scripts/check-agents-md-counts.mjs`, `prek.toml`, `AGENTS.md` | Tightened `TOLERANCE` from `0.5` (±50%) to `0.25` (±25%) in the script — the band that was supposed to catch "doc says 100, actual is 1" drift was so wide it let 16.8% drift go silent. Updated AGENTS.md test count claim (single approved one-line edit): `Rust tests (2100+ tests)` → `Rust tests (3000+ tests)`. Vitest `7300+` claim left as-is — actual 8278 is within ±25% (13.4% off). Updated all 6 in-script `±50%` / `>50%` references to `±25%` / `>25%`, plus the prek.toml comment block at L158-163 (originally caught by the review subagent and fixed orchestrator-side). |

**Files touched (33 modified):**

- Rust (15): `src-tauri/src/{cache/projected_agenda,commands/agenda,commands/compaction,db,fts/search,gcal_push/connector,hash,materializer/{consumer,coordinator,dispatch,metrics,tests},mcp/server,recovery/boot,recurrence/{compute,parser},snapshot/create,sync_daemon/mod,sync_files}.rs`
- Frontend tests (5): `src/components/__tests__/{ConflictListItem,ConflictTypeRenderer,ErrorBoundary,FeatureErrorBoundary}.test.tsx`, `src/lib/__tests__/tauri-mock.test.ts`
- Frontend infra (3): `src/lib/{config,tauri-mock/handlers}.ts`, `e2e/helpers.ts`
- Scripts (2): `scripts/{check-agents-md-counts,check-tauri-mock-parity}.mjs`
- Config (3): `knip.json`, `prek.toml`, `AGENTS.md` (single-line edit, user-approved)
- Docs (2): `REVIEW-LATER.md` (8 items removed; counts updated), `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry)

**Verification:** `prek run --all-files` → all 33 hooks PASS (vitest 8500+ tests, cargo nextest 3000+ tests, full clippy / fmt / biome / tsc / lychee / sqruff / cargo deny / cargo machete / sqlx prepare check). The full Playwright e2e suite was not re-run (orchestrator preserved the post-release green baseline; the changes here are test-additions + suppressed-rule-removals + script tweaks, none of which touch e2e flow logic).

**Items deferred with rationale (2):**

- **MAINT-159** (`check-ipc-error-path.mjs` subdir scope) — the item's own description says "Land AFTER MAINT-128 so the new subdirectories from the god-component split are in place". MAINT-128 is unscheduled. Walking subdirs now would surface gaps we'd then have to fill in code that's about to be split anyway.
- **MAINT-162** (ARIA role re-evaluation: `role="listbox"` → `role="grid"`) — M-cost architectural change requiring manual NVDA/VoiceOver verification per component (~6 components × manual AT testing). Not feasible in an automated session. The item's own description says "may be deferred behind MAINT-128 or shipped earlier as a per-file role flip" — preferring per-component work as those files are touched.

**Process notes:**

- **Discovery agents' calibration was systematically off.** The previous session's audit (which produced MAINT-153..162) had four discovery subagents collectively flag "1 SUSPECT out of ~600 exceptions" — adversarial validators inverted the bias and surfaced the real findings. Two-stage discovery + adversarial validation was the right shape for an exception audit; pure single-pass discovery would have shipped a "100% LEGITIMATE" verdict that was demonstrably false.
- **Build agent → reviewer pipelining worked.** Per PROMPT.md, reviewers were launched as builders finished rather than waiting for all builds. The MAINT-160 build → review → fix cycle caught 4 substantive issues (wrong `privacy_mode` default, `disconnect_gcal` argument-handling, comment count drift, missing `is_conflict` filter on the trash mock) and 1 missing-test gap, all addressed in a single fix subagent before merge. Without the review, `privacy_mode: 'titles_only'` would have shipped to e2e tests as a wrong default vs. Rust.
- **`#[allow]` for `f64 → i64` casts is a real std limitation.** The build subagent correctly identified that std lacks `TryFrom<f64>` for integer types and that the prek clippy gate is `-D warnings`, so removing the `#[allow]` would fail the gate. The 5 kept sites had their comments rewritten to be more explicit ("no `TryFrom` in std; the cast is safe because …") — better than the original "documents an invariant the cast doesn't enforce" pattern.
- **The e2e regex change is a no-op in current dev.** `index.html` includes `<link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg">` and `public/favicon.svg` exists, so the favicon 404 doesn't fire. The new tight regex remains correct insurance for the day someone removes the favicon — but it doesn't change observed test behaviour today. This is the right shape: a regex that matches the literal Chromium error string for the genuine non-error noise, not a broad sieve that catches real bugs.
- **One intentional `_resetRateLimits` test→logger-internals coupling.** The `ErrorBoundary.test.tsx` count-assertion approach required clearing the logger's rate-limit map between tests because the boundary always logs the same `module:message` key. The internal `_resetRateLimits` helper has the right semantics (already used by `logger.test.ts`) and is `@internal`-tagged "Exported for testing only". Alternatives (per-test unique error messages, or fewer per-test count assertions) were considered and rejected as worse trade-offs. Documented in `ErrorBoundary.test.tsx`'s `beforeEach` rationale comment.
- **Rust flaky test (`run_file_transfer_initiator_breaks_on_cancel_m47`)** failed first-try in the post-MAINT-155/156 nextest run but passed on retry. Verified pre-existing: 5/5 PASS in isolation. Cast-syntax conversions in `sync_files.rs` test fixtures cannot affect the test's runtime behaviour (`usize → i64/u64` is a syntactic change for non-negative usize, not a value change). The flake is a contention race under full-suite parallelism — orthogonal to this batch.

## Session 514 — Pick up + finish another agent's H-4 + H-13 leftover Rust WIP (2026-04-27)

**Two HIGH backend code-review findings closed by salvaging another agent's abandoned in-progress work.** The previous agent had landed substantial scaffolding for H-4 (`set_todo_state` atomicity), H-13 (op-log immutability triggers), and H-9b (deny-list bug-report redaction) in the working tree, but never completed it — the code didn't compile and had been sitting unchanged across at least 3 of my prior sessions. This session investigated the WIP, finished the missing pieces for H-4, accepted H-13 as already complete, dropped H-9b (incomplete + scope too large for this session — left open in REVIEW-LATER), and added a dedicated H-4 atomicity regression test.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **HIGH findings:** 6 → 4 (H-4 + H-13 removed; severity table at the top of the BCR appendix bumped). Top-priority list pruned from 4 entries to 2.
- **Previously-resolved counter:** 583+ → 585+ across 482 sessions (latest session 514).
- **Top-level open count:** 54 unchanged (H-4 / H-13 were BCR-appendix entries, not main-summary items).
- **H-9b stays open** — left in the BCR appendix at H-9 (parent) / H-9b / H-9c entries; H-9b is still M-cost and needs careful safe-token-set tuning.

**Items closed (2):**

| Item | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| H-4 | `src-tauri/src/commands/blocks/crud.rs` (+85L: new `delete_property_in_tx`), `src-tauri/src/commands/blocks/mod.rs` + `commands/mod.rs` (re-exports), `src-tauri/src/commands/properties.rs` (the previous agent's WIP — `set_todo_state_inner` now opens one `CommandTx::begin_immediate` covering state change + timestamp writes + recurrence sibling creation, snapshots GCal pre-state inside that tx, and routes through `set_property_in_tx` / `delete_property_in_tx` / `handle_recurrence_in_tx`), `src-tauri/src/recurrence/compute.rs` (split `handle_recurrence` into a thin pool-based wrapper + new `handle_recurrence_in_tx(tx, device_id, block_id)` that takes a caller-managed `CommandTx`; bumped `&mut **tx` → `&mut ***tx` and `&mut tx,` → `&mut *tx,` since `tx` is now a `&mut CommandTx` parameter rather than an owned value), `src-tauri/src/recurrence/mod.rs` (re-export `handle_recurrence_in_tx`, drop crate-wide re-export of `handle_recurrence` since no production caller remains), `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/property_cmd_tests.rs` (new test `set_todo_state_atomic_when_recurrence_fails` plants a corrupt `repeat-until` value, asserts the rolled-back tx leaves todo_state unchanged + no completed_at + no recurrence sibling + op_log size unchanged). The `delete_property_in_tx` helper mirrors `delete_property_core` (validate block exists → append DeleteProperty op → clear column for reserved keys / DELETE FROM block_properties for non-reserved) but runs entirely inside the caller's tx and returns the OpRecord for the caller to enqueue. | One commit |
| H-13 | `src-tauri/migrations/0036_op_log_immutability_triggers.sql` (NEW — adds `_op_log_mutation_allowed` sentinel table + BEFORE UPDATE / BEFORE DELETE triggers on `op_log` that ABORT unless the sentinel is present), `src-tauri/src/op_log.rs` (NEW `enable_op_log_mutation_bypass` / `disable_op_log_mutation_bypass` helper pair; existing test `op_log_update_not_blocked_by_schema` rewritten to assert the trigger now fires; 2 new tests `compaction_path_with_bypass_succeeds` + `compaction_bypass_does_not_leak_to_sibling_connection` covering the documented enable → mutate → disable → commit dance and WAL connection-isolation guarantee), `src-tauri/src/snapshot/create.rs` (production compaction path wraps its per-device DELETEs with the bypass helper pair), `src-tauri/src/snapshot/restore.rs` (snapshot RESET path wraps the `DELETE FROM op_log` with the bypass helper pair), `src-tauri/src/dag/tests.rs` + `src-tauri/src/snapshot/tests.rs` + `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/tests.rs` (existing tests that DELETE from op_log directly migrated to use the bypass dance). The previous agent's `temp.` per-connection-scoped sentinel had been rejected by SQLite (triggers cannot reference temp-schema tables); the doc comment in the migration now documents that we achieve equivalent isolation via WAL semantics + transactional discipline (sibling connections never observe the sentinel as present because BEGIN IMMEDIATE serialises writers and the helper requires the sentinel be DELETEd before commit). | Same commit |

**H-9b dropped (left open):** the previous agent had added ~233L of deny-list redaction scaffolding to `src-tauri/src/commands/bug_report.rs` — a `SafeTokenClass` enum with 11 variants, ten compile-time regex matchers, an `allowed_classes_for_field` helper, a `RedactionContext` struct (separate MAINT-147(i) refactor) — but had NOT wired any of it into the existing `redact_line` function. Without the wiring, every component was dead code (15 unused-warning lines on `cargo build --lib`). Wiring it correctly requires careful tuning of the safe-token set + extensive property tests + a decision on `RedactionContext`'s adoption (which is a refactor separable from H-9b). All of that is M+-cost work and properly belongs in a dedicated session, not appended to a salvage commit. The bug_report.rs file was reverted to HEAD; H-9b stays open in the BCR appendix.

**Files touched (12 modified + 1 new migration + 1 new test + 2 .sqlx cache files + 1 doc):**

- `REVIEW-LATER.md` (HIGH count 6→4; H-4 + H-13 detail blocks removed; top-priority list pruned; previously-resolved 583+→585+; sessions 513→514)
- `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry)
- NEW `src-tauri/migrations/0036_op_log_immutability_triggers.sql` (H-13 — triggers + bypass table)
- NEW `src-tauri/.sqlx/query-233e1ab5...json` + `src-tauri/.sqlx/query-fcd0271b...json` (sqlx cache for the new `delete_property_in_tx` query and a related test query)
- `src-tauri/src/commands/blocks/crud.rs` (NEW `delete_property_in_tx` ~85L)
- `src-tauri/src/commands/blocks/mod.rs` + `src-tauri/src/commands/mod.rs` (re-exports)
- `src-tauri/src/commands/properties.rs` (H-4 single-tx `set_todo_state_inner`)
- `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/property_cmd_tests.rs` (H-4 atomicity regression test)
- `src-tauri/src/op_log.rs` (H-13 helper pair + 3 reworked / new tests)
- `src-tauri/src/recurrence/compute.rs` (split into wrapper + `_in_tx`)
- `src-tauri/src/recurrence/mod.rs` (re-export update)
- `src-tauri/src/snapshot/create.rs` + `src-tauri/src/snapshot/restore.rs` (compaction + RESET use bypass)
- `src-tauri/src/dag/tests.rs` + `src-tauri/src/snapshot/tests.rs` + `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/tests.rs` (test sites that DELETE op_log migrated to the bypass dance)

**Verification:** `cargo nextest run` → **2996 tests passed across 3 binaries (+1 new H-4 atomicity test vs session 513's 2995)**. `cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests` succeeded; `.sqlx/` cache regenerated (no stale entries). All 33 prek hooks pass (cargo fmt fixed two pre-existing minor format issues during the run; re-staged and reran clean).

**Process notes:**

- **The user spotted that the leftover Rust WIP was being skirted around session after session.** Sessions 511, 512, 513 had each stashed it to run prek and restored it after committing — never actually engaging with the work. Investigation revealed three substantial pieces (H-4, H-13, H-9b) at very different completeness levels: H-13 was production-ready, H-4 was 90% there but missing one helper function (`delete_property_in_tx`) and the recurrence-handler-as-`_in_tx`-variant split, H-9b was framework-only (declarations without wiring). Salvaging H-4 + H-13 in one session was tractable; salvaging H-9b was not.
- **Borrow checker subtlety on the recurrence split.** With `tx: &mut CommandTx` (parameter) vs the original `let mut tx = CommandTx::...` (owned value), every `&mut **tx` had to become `&mut ***tx` (one extra deref) and every `&mut tx,` argument had to become `&mut *tx,` (reborrow). The doc comment in `db.rs:166` mentioning that `&mut CommandTx` coerces to `&mut Transaction<'_>` "automatically" is true at the *function-call site* via deref-coercion-on-reborrow, but raw `**tx` / `***tx` for `Executor` access still needs the right deref count.
- **The session-count fix** ("165 sessions" → "482 sessions") landed in its own small commit before the H-4/H-13 work to keep the diff focused. The number had been getting +1 per session for 22+ sessions without ever matching the actual SESSION-LOG.md count — a documentation-drift cluster the user flagged at the same time.
- **`cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests` is the canonical command for this repo** (per AGENTS.md). The earlier attempt with bare `cargo sqlx prepare` (no `--tests`) deleted ~10 test-only `.sqlx/` cache entries; restored via `git checkout HEAD -- src-tauri/.sqlx/` and re-prepared with `--tests`.
- **Tests in `compute.rs:tests_h17_m77` still call the pool-based `handle_recurrence` directly.** The wrapper was preserved (with `#[allow(dead_code)]`) rather than rewriting the test bodies to construct a `CommandTx` themselves — keeps the existing M-77 / H-17 atomicity tests passing without any change. If a future cleanup wants to drop `handle_recurrence` entirely, those two tests need to be refactored to call `handle_recurrence_in_tx` inside a fresh `CommandTx` they manage.

## Session 513 — Frontend test-quality batch: close 8 items (TEST-52, TEST-53, TEST-54, TEST-55, TEST-57, TEST-58, TEST-59, TEST-60) (2026-04-27)

**8 frontend test-quality items closed in one batch — the bulk of the TEST-52..TEST-64 cluster.** Five parallel build subagents on disjoint test files, paired with five review subagents — all SHIP. Zero production code touched; only test files modified. Net `+3` new tests (TEST-52's three new popup-yield cases for Enter/Escape/Backspace).

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **Top-level:** 62 open items → 54. Summary text: `46 planned work + 3 UX + 13 frontend test-quality (TEST-52..TEST-64)` → `46 planned work + 3 UX + 5 frontend test-quality (TEST-56, TEST-61, TEST-62, TEST-63, TEST-64)`. Previously-resolved bumped 575+ → 583+ across 165 sessions.

**Items closed (8) and where they landed:**

| Item | File | Change |
|---|---|---|
| TEST-52 | `src/editor/__tests__/use-block-keyboard.test.ts` | Added 3 non-tautological tests in the existing B-22 describe for Enter/Escape/Backspace popup-yield (AGENTS.md pitfall #14). New `setupHookWithRealEditor()` helper creates a real TipTap Editor on `document.body`, wires `renderHook(() => useBlockKeyboard(editor, callbacks))`, dispatches `KeyboardEvent` on `editor.view.dom.parentElement` (the element the hook's capture-phase listener attaches to). Reuses the existing `addVisiblePopup()` helper (mocks `popup.checkVisibility = () => true`) to make `isSuggestionPopupVisible()` return true. Each test asserts the relevant callback (`onEnterSave` / `onEscapeCancel` / `onMergeWithPrev` / `onDeleteBlock`) is NOT invoked AND `preventDefault` is NOT called. Backspace test specifically uses an empty editor so the production hook would otherwise call `onDeleteBlock`. Cleanup in `afterEach` removes any leaked `.suggestion-popup` nodes. |
| TEST-53 | `src/hooks/__tests__/useJournalAutoCreate.test.ts` | Rewrote the tautological "cleans up keyboard listener on unmount" test (L177-197). Fix: render with `pageMap: new Map()` (empty — no early-return on `pageMap.has(dateStr)` guard), pass `handleAddBlock: spy` AT MOUNT (not after, so the hook's effect-closure capture observes it), unmount, dispatch the keydown for `'n'` (matches `getShortcutKeys('createJournalBlock')`), assert `spy` not called. Belt-and-suspenders: `vi.spyOn(document, 'removeEventListener')` asserts cleanup invoked with `('keydown', expect.any(Function))` + `mockRestore()` for hygiene. Sanity-checked non-tautology: temporarily replaced production cleanup with `() => {}` — test failed; reverted. |
| TEST-54 | `src/hooks/__tests__/useSyncTrigger.test.ts:116-133` | Replaced single vacuous `expect(toast).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` (production code only calls `toast.success`/`.error`/`.info`, never bare `toast(...)`) with three explicit assertions for each sub-mock. The other two assertions in the test (`mockStartSync` + `useSyncStore.getState().state`) retained — they were non-tautological. |
| TEST-55 | `src/hooks/__tests__/useBatchCounts.test.ts:108-154` | Rewrote vacuous "cancels fetch on unmount" test using **option (c)** — two sibling hook instances sharing one promise. Builder verified React 19.2.5 + jsdom emits ZERO `console.error` / `console.warn` on unmounted-component setState (option (a) would have been vacuous), then implemented option (c): mock `countAgendaBatchBySource` returns one shared `Promise`, both `hookA` and `hookB` consume it, `hookA` is unmounted, `act(() => resolveAgenda(...))` resolves, `waitFor` asserts `hookB` received the data (`{ '2025-01-06': 99 }` / `{ 'page-1': 7 }`). Defensive `console.error` spy retained for a future React version that might reintroduce the warning. **Acknowledged limitation:** if the production cancellation guard at `useBatchCounts.ts:28-37` were removed, the test would still pass because `hookB`'s data-arrival assertions are independent of the unmounted hook's setState calls. The test's primary value is now exercising the promise-resolution path on both hooks; cancellation regressions remain hard to catch without more invasive instrumentation that would itself be fragile. Documented in the test file. |
| TEST-57 | `src/hooks/__tests__/useSyncTrigger.test.ts:37-58` | Added top-level `const originalOnLine = navigator.onLine` + `Object.defineProperty(navigator, 'onLine', { value: originalOnLine, configurable: true })` line in the existing `afterEach`. Removed the two inline `// Restore` blocks at original L505 / L521 that set `navigator.onLine` back to `true`. Eliminates the cascade-failure vector where a thrown assertion in either offline-test would leave `navigator.onLine === false` for the remaining 20+ tests. Pattern mirrors `src/hooks/__tests__/useOnlineStatus.test.ts:1-10`. |
| TEST-58 | `src/lib/__tests__/keyboard-config.test.ts:1, 34-36, 268-303` | Added `afterEach` import + one top-level `afterEach(() => vi.restoreAllMocks())` alongside the existing `beforeEach(() => localStorage.clear())`. Removed the three inline `vi.restoreAllMocks()` calls from the bodies of the three tests at original L276 / L290 / L304. If any prior `expect()` in those tests had thrown, `Storage.prototype.setItem` / `removeItem` would have stayed stubbed-to-throw for the remaining ~1080 lines (and the next `beforeEach`'s `localStorage.clear()` would have masqueraded as setup failure). |
| TEST-59 | `src/editor/__tests__/use-roving-editor.test.ts:540-544` | Replaced `await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 50))` in the `setup()` helper with `await waitFor(() => expect(hook.result.current.editor).not.toBeNull())`. AGENTS.md "No timing hacks" rule. The 50 ms hack is not guaranteed under CI worker contention. All 6 downstream callers of `setup()` already dereference / assert non-null on `result.current.editor`, so `editor !== null` is the correct readiness signal. |
| TEST-60 | `src/lib/__tests__/export-graph.test.ts:2, 41-77` | Replaced single vacuous `expect(blob).toBeInstanceOf(Blob)` assertion with real ZIP inspection using `JSZip.loadAsync(await blob.arrayBuffer())`. Added `import JSZip from 'jszip'` (already a direct dep). Test fixture now uses two realistic 26-char Crockford-base32 ULIDs for the two duplicate-named pages. Three new assertions: `sameNameMd.toHaveLength(2)` (catches collapse/overwrite), `new Set(sameNameMd).size === 2` (catches duplicate filenames), `regex /_[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{8}\.md$/` (catches wrong slice length). **Production-format adaptation:** spec hint suggested 26-char ULID suffix, but `src/lib/export-graph.ts:23` actually does `${name}_${page.id.slice(0, 8)}` — assertions match the actual 8-char Crockford slice. Mutation analysis confirms: removing `seen.has(name)` guard, changing `slice(0, 8)` to `slice(0, 4)` or `slice(0, 26)`, all caught by the new assertions. |

**Files touched (7 test files + 2 docs):**

- `REVIEW-LATER.md` (8 row removals + 8 detail removals; counts bumped 62 → 54; sessions 164 → 165)
- `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry)
- `src/editor/__tests__/use-block-keyboard.test.ts` (TEST-52 — added 3 tests + helpers)
- `src/editor/__tests__/use-roving-editor.test.ts` (TEST-59 — `waitFor` replaces `setTimeout(50)`)
- `src/hooks/__tests__/useBatchCounts.test.ts` (TEST-55 — option (c) sibling-hook pattern)
- `src/hooks/__tests__/useJournalAutoCreate.test.ts` (TEST-53 — fixed tautological cleanup test)
- `src/hooks/__tests__/useSyncTrigger.test.ts` (TEST-54 + TEST-57 — toast assertion + onLine afterEach)
- `src/lib/__tests__/export-graph.test.ts` (TEST-60 — real ZIP inspection)
- `src/lib/__tests__/keyboard-config.test.ts` (TEST-58 — top-level afterEach for spy restore)

**Verification:** `npx vitest run` → **8529 tests passed across 318 files, 0 failures**. `npx tsc -b` clean. Net `+3` tests vs session 512's 8526 (TEST-52's three new popup-yield cases; the other 7 items are net-zero — they fix existing test quality, not add coverage).

**Reviewers:** Five review subagents — all SHIP:
- Review A (TEST-52 + TEST-59): SHIP — confirmed TEST-52's 3 new tests are non-tautological (would fail if popup-yield guard removed), `setupHookWithRealEditor` cleans up properly, TEST-59's `waitFor` matches actual readiness signal.
- Review B (TEST-54 + TEST-57): SHIP — confirmed three replacement assertions reach the correct sub-mocks, top-level `afterEach` restore for `navigator.onLine` mirrors `useOnlineStatus.test.ts`.
- Review C (TEST-55 + TEST-58): SHIP with documented limitation — TEST-55's option (c) is acceptable given React 19's silent-drop behavior; flagged that cancellation-guard regression is technically still hard to catch (mutation analysis shows test could pass if guard removed) but this is the strongest non-invasive approach available. TEST-58's top-level afterEach correctly placed and conflicts checked.
- Review D (TEST-53): SHIP — confirmed fix on both axes (empty pageMap + spy at mount), belt-and-suspenders `removeEventListener` spy correct, `mockRestore` placement clean.
- Review E (TEST-60): SHIP — confirmed production-code adaptation (8-char slice not 26-char ULID), regex precisely matches Crockford alphabet excluding I/L/O/U, mutation analysis catches all 3 plausible regressions.

**Process notes:**

- **Smallest batch yet, fastest turnaround.** All 5 build subagents finished within ~5 minutes; reviews pipelined immediately. Total wall-clock from PLAN to COMMIT was substantially shorter than batches that touch production code, because the test-only changes have minimal cross-file ripple and require no multi-pass refinement.
- **One reviewer-flagged limitation accepted as-is.** Review C noted TEST-55's option (c) cannot fully prove the production cancellation guard isn't silently removed — but the alternatives are either vacuous (option (a) — React 19 swallows the warning) or invasive (option (b) — would require AbortController plumbing in production code). The current implementation is documented in the test file with the React 19 silent-drop nuance.
- **Production-format adaptation noted in TEST-60.** Spec called for a 26-char ULID regex; production code uses `slice(0, 8)`. Builder correctly chose to match the production format rather than the spec hint, and review confirmed the assertions catch all plausible regressions including changing the slice length.
- **No orchestrator-level fixes needed.** Unlike sessions 511 and 512 where reviewer-flagged issues required orchestrator hardening, this batch sailed through review without changes. Smaller scope + test-only diffs = lower risk surface.
- **5 frontend test-quality items remain** for a future batch: TEST-56 (useUndoStore singleton leak across 6 files), TEST-61 (makeBlock fixture duplication), TEST-62 (hand-rolled renderHook in 3 files), TEST-63 (global-state restore inline cluster), TEST-64 (weak-assertion / brittle-style cluster).
