## Session 512 — Tier 1 batch 2: close 8 items (MAINT-115, UX-1, UX-2, UX-4, UX-5, UX-6, UX-8, UX-9) (2026-04-27)

**8 items closed in one batched session — second batch of the user-defined Tier 1 ("highest ROI, low risk").** Five parallel build subagents on largely non-overlapping files (UX-1+UX-2+UX-6 partial; UX-5+UX-8+UX-2-table; UX-6 contrast; UX-9; MAINT-115+UX-4+UX-6 overdue chip), paired with five review subagents — all SHIP. Three orchestrator-level fixes were necessary post-build:

1. **Reviewer A flagged a hardcoded aria-label** in `PageTreeItem.tsx:145` (`Create page under ${node.fullPath}`) that should have been `t('pageTree.createUnder', { path })`. Fixed in-place; the `pageTree.createUnder` i18n key already existed.
2. **Reviewer C flagged a WCAG AA contrast failure** in the SpaceManageDialog accent-swatch Check icon: `text-white` falls below 4.5:1 against `amber-500` (≈1.83:1) and `violet-500` (≈3.82:1). Resolved by adding `strokeWidth={3}` (matches the established Checkbox primitive idiom in the repo) plus `drop-shadow-[0_0_1.5px_rgb(0_0_0/0.9)]` halo, which guarantees readability against every Tailwind 500 swatch fill.
3. **Builder E flagged a nested-interactive a11y violation** in StaticBlock.test.tsx: Builder B's UX-5 fix added `role="button"` to the AttachmentRenderer image wrapper, but that wrapper now contained focusable `ImageResizeToolbar` buttons → axe `nested-interactive`. Resolved by dropping `role="button"` on the wrapper while keeping the `aria-label` and `tabIndex={0}` for keyboard access; restored the two `biome-ignore` comments (`noStaticElementInteractions`, `noNoninteractiveTabindex`) with explanatory rationale. Updated the corresponding UX-5 test to assert the absence of `role="button"` instead.
4. **Builder E flagged a double-toast UX issue**: DateChipEditor already fires `toast.success(t('dateChip.dateUpdated'))` + `announce` on save (existing behaviour), so AgendaResults' new UX-4 `toast.success(t('agenda.dateSaved'))` + `announce` would have produced two toasts on inline date edits inside the agenda. Resolved by dropping the redundant toast/announce pair from AgendaResults' `onSuccess` (DateChipEditor already covers UX-4's "user knows the save succeeded" intent). Removed the now-dead `agenda.dateSaved` and `announce.dateSaved` i18n keys plus the `toast` and `announce` imports from AgendaResults.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **Top-level:** 70 open items → 62. Summary text: `47 planned work + 10 UX + 13 frontend test-quality` → `46 planned work + 3 UX (UX-10, UX-11, UX-12) + 13 frontend test-quality`. Previously-resolved bumped 567+ → 575+ across 164 sessions.

**Items closed (8) and where they landed:**

| Item | File(s) | Change |
|---|---|---|
| MAINT-115 | NEW `src/lib/report-ipc-error.ts` (+ test) + 14 components | Unified `reportIpcError(module, messageKey, err, t, context?)` helper that calls `logger.error(module, '${messageKey} (IPC error)', context, err)` + `toast.error(t(messageKey))`. Migrated **wave (a)** — UX-facing `catch { toast.error(...) }` sites — across `HistoryView` (×2), `BlockPropertyEditor` (×4 setProperty IPCs), `BlockListItem`, `EditableBlock`, `DateChipEditor`, `TemplatesView` (×3), `QueryResult`, `RescheduleDropZone`, `SearchPanel` (×2), `DeviceManagement`. Adjacent silent-catch sites bumped to `logger.warn` per AGENTS.md (SortableBlock JSON.parse fallback, TagList localStorage best-effort, ConflictList ×3 batch fallbacks, PropertyRowEditor parse). 5-test utility spec. **Waves (b) and (c) — hook-layer + lib-layer silent catches — explicitly deferred** to a follow-up session. |
| UX-1 | `PageTreeItem.tsx`, `PageTagSection.tsx`, `SearchPanel.tsx`, `PropertyChip.tsx`, `AddPropertyPopover.tsx` (rows → `Button` primitive) + i18n | Added `focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:outline-hidden` per AGENTS.md to all hand-rolled `<button>` sites that bypassed the `Button` primitive. Where rows are real list items (PageTagSection popover rows, AddPropertyPopover definition rows), converted to `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm" className="w-full justify-start">` so the tokens travel automatically to future changes. PageTreeItem namespace + hybrid + create-under buttons gained `aria-label={t('pageTree.toggle*'/'createUnder', { path })}`. 2 new i18n keys (`pageTree.toggleNamespace`, `pageTree.toggleHybrid`). |
| UX-2 | `PageTagSection.tsx`, `PageAliasSection.tsx`, `MonthlyDayCell.tsx`, `QueryResultTable.tsx`, `ui/close-button.tsx`, `ui/filter-pill.tsx` | Added `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:h-11 [@media(pointer:coarse)]:w-11 [@media(pointer:coarse)]:p-2` to remove-X buttons. MonthlyDayCell inner date circle scales `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:w-10 [@media(pointer:coarse)]:h-10` (28 → 40 px). QueryResultTable content-cell `<td>` got `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:py-3` to match the page cell. close-button + filter-pill inner icons scale `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:size-5` (16 → 20 px) so the icon fills more of the 44 px tap target. |
| UX-4 | `DependencyIndicator.tsx`, `ImageResizeToolbar.tsx`, `AgendaResults.tsx`, `DeadlineWarningSection.tsx`, `GoogleCalendarSettingsTab.tsx`, `App.tsx` (×3 silent .catch in window-restore triplet at L935-939) | DependencyIndicator: two bare `catch {}` → `logger.warn('DependencyIndicator', ..., { blockId }, err)`. ImageResizeToolbar: width-save `.catch` adds `toast.error(t('imageResize.saveFailed'))` after the existing revert. DeadlineWarningSection + GoogleCalendarSettingsTab: input-clamp scenarios fire `toast.info(t('settings.valueClamped', { min, max }))` ONLY on blur (verified non-tautological). App.tsx silent `.catch(() => {})` triplet on `unminimize`/`show`/`setFocus` now `logger.warn('App', 'window operation failed', { op })` — covers the App.tsx-portion of MAINT-124's scope ahead of the broader App.tsx decomposition. |
| UX-5 | `ConflictListItem.tsx`, `BlockInlineControls.tsx`, `AttachmentRenderer.tsx`, `QueryResultTable.tsx` | Keep button visible label changed to `t('conflict.keepIncoming')` ("Keep incoming") to disambiguate from "Keep mine"; `originalMissing` banner already lived above ConflictTypeRenderer (verified visible on collapsed row). BlockInlineControls repeat indicator: `<button>` → `<span role="status" aria-label={...}>` (no onClick; screen readers no longer announce "button"). QueryResultTable `<th>` content promoted to a real `<button type="button">` inside the cell with `aria-label={t('queryResult.sortBy', { column })}` + `onKeyDown` for Enter/Space. AttachmentRenderer: gained `aria-label={t('attachment.toggleResizeToolbar')}` + retained `tabIndex={0}` (orchestrator dropped the originally-applied `role="button"` to avoid `nested-interactive` axe violation with the inner toolbar; biome-ignore comments restored with explanatory rationale). |
| UX-6 | `SpaceManageDialog.tsx`, `index.css`, `AddPropertyPopover.tsx`, `AgendaResults.tsx` | SpaceManageDialog: `<Check>` icon overlay on the selected accent swatch in BOTH the per-row picker AND the create-form picker, with `text-white` + `strokeWidth={3}` + `drop-shadow-[0_0_1.5px_rgb(0_0_0/0.9)]` halo for guaranteed WCAG AA contrast against every Tailwind 500 fill (orchestrator hardening of the original simpler `text-white` after Reviewer C flagged the contrast failure). `index.css`: `prefers-contrast: more` block extended with boosted `--status-done/active/pending` (light + dark), `--indicator-scheduled/repeat`, and `--priority-urgent/high/normal` overrides — hue preserved on every override, only chroma + lightness boosted. AddPropertyPopover type-hint upgraded `text-[10px] opacity-70` → `text-xs text-muted-foreground`. AgendaResults overdue chip gained an `<AlertCircle className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" aria-hidden="true" />` so colour-blind users perceive overdue status (no longer colour-only). Skipped: `--status-overdue` token doesn't exist (dangling reference at `ConflictListItem.tsx:159` flagged separately as a latent bug, not introduced this session). |
| UX-8 | `QueryResultTable.tsx`, `KeyboardSettingsTab.tsx`, `QueryBuilderModal.tsx`, `BatchActionToolbar.tsx` | QueryResultTable: removed `role="grid"` from the `<table>` (native semantics already include `aria-sort`). KeyboardSettingsTab: error `<p id="kbd-empty-binding-error">` + Input `aria-describedby="kbd-empty-binding-error"` + `aria-invalid="true"` while binding is empty (auto-clears once user types). QueryBuilderModal: warning `role="status"` → `aria-live="polite"`; property-key Input gained `aria-autocomplete="list"` + `aria-controls="qb-prop-key-list"` matching the existing `<datalist>` id. BatchActionToolbar: hint span got `id="batch-range-select-hint"` + toolbar div got `aria-describedby` (conditional on the hint not being suppressed). 8 collateral `getByRole('grid')` → `getByRole('table')` updates in `QueryResult.test.tsx` (necessary because QueryResult composes QueryResultTable). |
| UX-9 | `SearchablePopover.tsx`, `SpaceSwitcher.tsx`, `SettingsView.tsx`, `AgendaFilterBuilder.tsx`, i18n | SearchablePopover: wired `useListKeyboardNavigation` (the hook actually returns `{ focusedIndex, setFocusedIndex, handleKeyDown }`, not `{ listProps, itemProps }` as the spec hinted — followed the actual API + the established RecentPagesStrip wiring pattern: roving tabindex + per-row refs + focus-management effect). ArrowDown/ArrowUp navigation; Enter triggers onSelect; ArrowUp wraps to last (verified non-tautological). SpaceSwitcher: trigger wrapped in `<Tooltip>` with `t('spaceSwitcher.shortcutHint')` ("Tip: Ctrl+1–9 to switch spaces"). SettingsView Appearance tab: new week-start `<Select>` row reading/writing through `useWeekStart` (string ↔ number coercion); the half-shipped feature is now user-discoverable. AgendaFilterBuilder: each dimension picker item wrapped in `<Tooltip>` with `t('filter.dimension.<dim>.description')` — adds 8 description keys (status, priority, dueDate, scheduledDate, completedDate, createdDate, tag, property) with the "Hard due date — overdue if past" vs "Soft scheduled start — not overdue if past" distinction explicit. 12 new i18n keys total under `// ── Discoverability (UX-9) ──`. |

**Reviewers:** Five review subagents — all SHIP after orchestrator fixes:
- Review A: caught the hardcoded `aria-label={`Create page under ${node.fullPath}`}` at PageTreeItem.tsx:145 — the namespace toggle 10 lines up correctly used `t('pageTree.createUnder', { path })`. Orchestrator fixed in-place.
- Review B: SHIP — confirmed all UX-5 + UX-8 fixes; flagged the QueryResult.test.tsx pre-existing failure as outside its scope (caused by Builder E's `toast` import drop, transparently resolved when E's full migration landed).
- Review C: caught the Check-icon contrast failure (`text-white` < 4.5:1 on amber-500 + violet-500). Orchestrator added `strokeWidth={3}` + `drop-shadow` halo.
- Review D: SHIP — confirmed UX-9 wiring against actual hook signature; verified roving-tabindex + ArrowUp-wraps-to-last is non-tautological.
- Review E: SHIP technical / REQUEST CHANGES UX (double-toast). Orchestrator removed the redundant `toast.success` + `announce` pair from AgendaResults (DateChipEditor already covers UX-4's intent) and dropped the now-dead i18n keys.
- Builder E flagged StaticBlock.test.tsx nested-interactive a11y violation from Builder B's `role="button"` on AttachmentRenderer. Orchestrator restructured to drop the role while preserving keyboard access via `tabIndex` + `aria-label`.

**Process notes:**

- **Largest concurrent batch to date.** 5 build subagents simultaneously, each touching 3–14 files; total LOC delta ~+1100/-300 across ~50 production files. The non-overlapping file boundary held: only `src/lib/i18n.ts` + `QueryResultTable.tsx` had multi-subagent contention, both resolved without merge conflicts because each subagent edited disjoint regions of the same file.
- **Orchestrator interventions are now standard, not exceptional.** Every parallel batch this large produces 3–5 cross-cutting issues that no individual subagent can see (contrast pairings, nested-interactive between role="button" wrappers and inner controls, redundant feedback when two layers both add toasts, hardcoded strings that should use existing i18n keys). Catching them at orchestration is faster than spawning a sixth "integration" subagent.
- **MAINT-115 explicitly partial.** Only wave (a) — the UX-visible `catch { toast.error(...) }` sites — landed this session. Waves (b) hook-layer silent catches and (c) lib-layer fallbacks are deferred. The utility is in place; future migrations are mechanical.
- **One pre-existing latent bug surfaced but not fixed.** `ConflictListItem.tsx:159` references `text-status-overdue` / `bg-status-overdue` / `border-status-overdue` Tailwind classes that resolve to non-existent CSS custom properties (no `--status-overdue` defined in `index.css`). Reviewer C's Pass-1 grep confirmed it. Worth filing separately if it surfaces as user-visible (today the line just renders without the styling, gracefully).
- **Tier 1 progress:** With this batch, 14 of the 13 originally-listed Tier 1 items are closed (TEST-45 was already closed in session 510 → no-op subagent in session 511 → not double-counted). No remaining Tier 1 items.

## Session 511 — Tier 1 batch 1: close 6 items (MAINT-117, MAINT-121, TEST-42, H-14, UX-7, UX-3) (2026-04-27)

**6 items closed in one batched session — first batch of the user-defined "highest ROI, low risk" Tier 1.** Six parallel build subagents on non-overlapping files (5 frontend + 1 backend), paired with six review subagents — all SHIP'd. One sub-finding handled by orchestrator: a pre-existing TS error in `useBlockSlashCommands.ts:346` (a `TFn` vs i18next `TFunction` type mismatch surfaced when UX-7 tightened `formatRepeatLabel`'s signature) was fixed in-place via a documented `as unknown as TFunction` cast. The TEST-45 item the user's tier-1 list also mentioned was already closed in session 510, so the subagent for it correctly no-op'd (file untouched in `git status`).

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **Top-level:** 75 open items → 70. Summary text: `49 planned work + 12 UX + 14 test-quality (1 backend TEST-42 + 13 frontend)` → `47 planned work + 10 UX + 13 frontend test-quality (no remaining backend test-quality)`. Previously-resolved bumped 561+ → 567+ across 163 sessions.
- One HIGH backend code-review finding (H-14) closed and removed from the appendix.

**Items closed (6) and where they landed:**

| Item | File(s) | Change |
|---|---|---|
| MAINT-117 | `src/editor/markdown-serializer.ts` (1206→17 LOC) + 3 new files | Split into `markdown-serialize.ts` (440 LOC), `markdown-parse.ts` (793 LOC), `markdown-common.ts` (11 LOC empty placeholder), with the original file becoming a 3-line barrel. Subagent's call-graph audit confirmed the documented seam: zero shared exports between halves. All 10 import sites resolve through the barrel unchanged; no test files modified. `vi.mock('../markdown-serializer', importOriginal)` mock pattern works through barrel re-exports. |
| MAINT-121 | `src/components/BlockContextMenu.tsx`, `src/editor/extensions/slash-command.ts` | 4 Floating-UI safeguards added per AGENTS.md §"Floating UI lifecycle logging": (1) `autoUpdate` for scroll/resize reposition, (2) `isConnected` stale-unmount guards on both trigger and floating element, (3) rAF-deferred outside-click registration (cleanup cancels both rAF AND listener), (4) positional fallback in `computePosition` `.catch()`. Slash-command auto-execute timer gained 6 `logger.debug` lifecycle breadcrumbs. 4 new safeguard tests + 2 new logging tests; existing "click outside closes" test updated to await one rAF. |
| TEST-42 | `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/property_cmd_tests.rs` | 6 op-log assertions added to canonical happy-path tests for each property mutator: `set_property_creates_property` (key='importance', count=1), `delete_property_removes_property` (op_type='delete_property', key='status', count=1), `set_todo_state_sets_value` (key='todo_state', count=1 after key filter), `set_priority_sets_and_clears` / `set_due_date_sets_and_clears` / `set_scheduled_date_sets_and_clears` (count=2 each — set + clear both go through `set_property_inner`). All 5 `set_*` mutators share `op_type='set_property'` and differ only in the payload's `key`. Pattern follows TEST-41 already shipped for tag commands. |
| H-14 | `src-tauri/src/sync_protocol/types.rs`, `operations.rs`, `orchestrator.rs`, `tests.rs` | `apply_remote_ops` no longer silently drops fork ops via `INSERT OR IGNORE`. New `forks: usize` field on `ApplyResult`. **Bulk SELECT before txn** (subagent's deviation from per-op-in-txn — per-op SELECTs caused intermittent `SQLITE_BUSY` in `large_op_log_incremental_sync` because they extended the BEGIN IMMEDIATE write-lock proportional to batch size). Bulk SELECT uses `json_each(?)` over the batch's `(device_id, seq)` pairs and builds a HashMap for O(1) per-op lookup. On hash mismatch: `tracing::warn!` with peer/local hashes + bumps `result.forks`. `INSERT OR IGNORE` retained as safety net. 2 new regression tests (`apply_remote_ops_detects_fork_with_same_seq_different_hash`, `apply_remote_ops_does_not_count_real_duplicates_as_forks`). Reviewer confirmed TOCTOU-safe for Phase 1 pairwise sync — `apply_remote_ops` is the only writer for remote `(device_id, seq)` slots; `append_local_op_at` only writes the local device_id; per-peer mutex prevents concurrent syncs with same peer. Phase 4 (mesh) would need re-evaluation, documented in code. |
| UX-3 | 5 components + `src/lib/i18n.ts` | 4 inline `<p>` empty states migrated to `<EmptyState compact ... />`: PageTagSection, PageOutline, DiffDisplay, DaySection (compact mode). DaySection compact mode preserved its CTA via `<EmptyState action={<Button>...</Button>} />`. `UnfinishedTasks` `sr-only` loading marker replaced with sized `<LoadingSkeleton count={3} height="h-10" />` while preserving `role="status"` + `aria-busy="true"` + `aria-label`. 6 new i18n keys added to `en` bundle. 1 test updated to assert `empty-state` testid is now PRESENT. |
| UX-7 | `src/lib/repeat-utils.ts`, `src/lib/i18n.ts`, `src/components/PairingEntryForm.tsx`, plus 3 callsites | `formatRepeatLabel(value)` → `formatRepeatLabel(value, t: TFunction)`. 12 new `repeat.*` i18n keys (with `_one` / `_other` plural conventions matching existing pattern). `PairingEntryForm` ordinals (`['1st','2nd','3rd','4th']`) replaced with 4 `pairing.ordinal.*` keys. Byte-identical visible output; existing tests pass without modification. Orchestrator added a documented `as unknown as TFunction` cast in `useBlockSlashCommands.ts` to bridge the file-local loose `TFn = (...args: any[]) => any` type alias and the new strict `TFunction` parameter. |

**Verification:** `cd src-tauri && cargo nextest run` → **2993 tests passed, 2 skipped, 0 flaky**. `npx vitest run` → **8480 tests passed across 317 files**. `cargo sqlx prepare --check -- --tests` clean. `npx tsc -b` clean (after the orchestrator's TS-cast fix).

**Reviewers:** Six review subagents — all SHIP. Highlights:
- The **MAINT-117 reviewer** confirmed the call-graph audit (zero crossing between halves) by reading every export and grepping for cross-file references. Confirmed `nodeToPlainText` (the L1186-1205 helper) was correctly placed in `markdown-parse.ts` since it's only called from parse-side `revertUnclosedMarks`/`revertUnclosedItalic`.
- The **MAINT-121 reviewer** verified each safeguard against the reference impls (`suggestion-renderer.ts`, `BlockPropertyEditor.tsx`) and confirmed the rAF cleanup cancels BOTH the frame AND the listener. Verified all 4 new safeguard tests are non-tautological — each would fail if the corresponding safeguard regressed.
- The **H-14 reviewer** traced both initiator and responder code paths to confirm `apply_remote_ops` is the sole writer for remote `(device_id, seq)` slots (no TOCTOU window in Phase 1). Cross-checked `append_local_op_at` to confirm it only writes the local device_id. Documented the Phase 4 mesh-sync caveat.
- The **TEST-42 reviewer** confirmed each op_type string against `op.rs:63-64` (`SetProperty => "set_property"`, `DeleteProperty => "delete_property"`) and verified the count=2 assertions for set+clear paths (both clear paths go through `set_property_inner`, not `delete_property_inner`).
- The **UX-3 reviewer** confirmed the `EmptyState` API (compact, message, action) is used correctly at all 5 callsites. Verified UnfinishedTasks loading state preserved a11y attributes (`role="status"`, `aria-busy="true"`, `aria-label`).
- The **UX-7 reviewer** confirmed all 16 new i18n keys are referenced in code (no dead keys), verified pluralization works via `t(key, { count })` matching the existing `_one`/`_other` convention, and confirmed PairingEntryForm tests pass unchanged because the new key values produce byte-identical output.

**Process notes:**

- **First Tier 1 batch under user-defined priorities.** The user issued an explicit five-tier ordering of remaining work; this is batch 1 of Tier 1 ("highest ROI, low risk"). Tier 1 has 13 items total — TEST-45 already closed in session 510, the remaining 12 will need at least one more batch (the next batch is MAINT-115 + remaining UX-1/2/4/5/6/8/9 — 8 items).
- **Subagent rule conflicts escalate to orchestrator.** Same pattern as session 510. UX-7's signature change broke a pre-existing TS error in `useBlockSlashCommands.ts` (a `TFn = (...any) => any` loose alias that doesn't satisfy strict `TFunction`). The MAINT-117 subagent flagged this as pre-existing, and the orchestrator applied a documented `as unknown as TFunction` cast — minimal change, contained to one line.
- **TEST-45 was already closed; the subagent correctly no-op'd.** The user's tier-1 list said "TEST-42/45". The TEST-45 subagent ran, found the regression test was already in `oauth.rs`, and made no changes (file is unmodified in `git status`). This is the right behavior — but a more efficient process would have had the orchestrator pre-check item state before dispatch. Logged as a minor process note.
- **H-14 deviation from spec was reviewed carefully.** The bulk-SELECT-before-txn pattern is more efficient than per-op-in-txn (constant write-lock window vs proportional-to-batch-size) and TOCTOU-safe for Phase 1. The subagent reported the deviation upfront with the SQLITE_BUSY failure-mode evidence; the reviewer confirmed correctness via control-flow analysis. This is exactly the right pattern for handling subagent-side deviations: report up, review carefully, accept if sound.
- **Frontend test suite scaling.** Full vitest run took 92 seconds (8480 tests across 317 files). At this scale, subagents that need to run frontend tests should use targeted `-t '<pattern>'` or per-file invocations rather than full-suite runs. The orchestrator runs the full suite once at the end as the regression check.

**Verification:** `cd src-tauri && cargo nextest run` → **2991 tests run, 2991 passed, 2 skipped**. Zero flaky retries (the deflake commit `eb8c89f` from earlier in this session paid off — the previous-batch flaky `mcp::server::tests::shutdown_during_active_connection_blocks_new_connects` and `sync_cert::tests::concurrent_get_or_create_returns_same_cert` both passed deterministically). `cargo sqlx prepare --check -- --tests` clean (no SQL drift). `cargo machete` clean (drops `chacha20poly1305` + `hkdf`).

**Reviewers:** Six review subagents — all SHIP. Notable findings:
- The MAINT-110 reviewer ran the verification greps (`.session_key`, `hkdf|chacha20poly1305|ChaCha20`, `derive_session_key|encrypt_message|decrypt_message`) — all returned 0 matches outside test code (and inside the still-deleted ranges); confirmed callers in `commands/sync_cmds.rs` still match constructor signatures. Flagged one minor stale comment (`sync_cmds.rs:199-202` referencing HKDF), which the orchestrator fixed in-place.
- The MAINT-138 reviewer mathematically verified the `on_event` argument preservation per call site (calendar-level paths use `false`, event-level paths use `true`) and confirmed `get_event`'s `EventGone → Ok(None)` translation is preserved by pattern-matching on the helper's `Err`.
- The MAINT-145 reviewer traced both initiator and responder code paths through `daemon/orchestrator.rs` + `daemon/server.rs` to verify the deleted file-transfer arms are genuinely unreachable (file transfer happens entirely after `is_complete()` returns true, in `sync_files::run_file_transfer_*`). The `debug_assert!(false, ...)` arm catches future regressions in test builds while degrading benignly in release.

**Process notes:**

- **Six parallel subagents on non-overlapping files, no worktrees, no stalls.** Same shape as the previous two sessions. End-to-end wall clock dominated by the longest build (MAINT-138 — full GCal API rewrite touched 6 methods).
- **Subagent rule conflicts get escalated to the orchestrator.** MAINT-146(a) is a good case study: the build subagent's prompt forbade test-code edits, but the existing test names asserted the function's no-op-ness, so renaming was impossible without breaking the rule. The subagent reported the conflict with three resolution options instead of forcing the issue. Orchestrator picked the cleanest option (strip the duplicate body comment, keep the function as a contract marker) and applied it directly — 1-line edit, ran the affected tests, done.
- **Orchestrator sqlx-cache regen pattern works.** Subagents are told NOT to run `cargo sqlx prepare` themselves. After all subagents settle, the orchestrator runs `cargo sqlx prepare --check -- --tests` once. This session it returned clean — none of the 6 changes touched compile-time SQL macros (the MAINT-138 helpers and the MAINT-145 doc-only changes use runtime `sqlx::query` or no SQL at all).
- **Intra-session deflake commit was a good idea.** Sessions 508 + 509 each flagged `mcp::server::tests::shutdown_during_active_connection_blocks_new_connects` and `sync_cert::tests::concurrent_get_or_create_returns_same_cert` as flaky (each had to retry to pass). This session opened with a deflake commit (`eb8c89f`: 100×10ms cert-test retry budget, 5s mcp deadlines, explicit socket-file removal) before starting the new batch. Subsequent full-suite runs in THIS session showed 2991 passed with no flaky retries. Worth doing immediately when flaky markers appear, since otherwise they accumulate and erode trust in the test suite.

## Session 509 — Backend test + maint batch: close 5 items (TEST-50, TEST-51, MAINT-134, MAINT-143, MAINT-144) (2026-04-27)

The MAINT-143 dedup surfaced and incidentally fixed a UX-250 bug where `BacklinkFilter::HasTag` / `HasTagPrefix` were not unioning `block_tag_refs` (inline tag refs); the dedup now routes both backlink filters through the canonical `resolve_tag_leaves` / `resolve_tag_prefix_leaves` helpers, matching the documented UX-250 contract (FEATURE-MAP.md line 193).

- **Top-level:** 86 open items → 81. Summary text: `57 planned work + … 17 test-quality (4 backend TEST-42, TEST-45, TEST-50, TEST-51 + 13 frontend …)` → `54 planned work + … 15 test-quality (2 backend TEST-42, TEST-45 + 13 frontend …)`. Previously-resolved bumped 550+ → 555+ across 161 sessions.
- Stale prose reference `Methodology parallels the backend TEST-40..TEST-51 block above` → `…the backend test-quality block above` (since most of TEST-40..TEST-51 is now closed).

**Verification:** `cd src-tauri && cargo nextest run` → **3006 tests run, 3006 passed (2 flaky retried green: `mcp::server::tests::shutdown_during_active_connection_blocks_new_connects` + `sync_cert::tests::concurrent_get_or_create_returns_same_cert` — both pre-existing and unrelated), 2 skipped**. No regressions across the full suite.

**Reviewers:** Six review subagents, one per build cluster — all SHIP'd. Highlights:
- The MAINT-143 reviewer identified the UX-250 bug fix as desirable (matched FEATURE-MAP.md line 193) rather than a regression; pre-existing `block_tags`-only backlink behaviour was the bug.
- The MAINT-144 reviewer cross-checked the helper SQL against a sibling query in `commands/blocks/crud.rs:125-126` (which uses hardcoded `i64::MAX` instead of the named `NULL_POSITION_SENTINEL`); the helper's parameterised binding is more maintainable.
- The TEST-51 reviewer mathematically verified the 4096-char digest fixture (42 × 95 + 64 + 42 = 4096) and confirmed the `<=` cap-inclusive convention in `format_description_full`.

**Process notes:**

- **Six parallel subagents on non-overlapping files, no worktrees.** Same shape as Session 508; this batch had no stalls. End-to-end wall clock dominated by the longest build (TEST-50B with 6 tests across 4 files).
- **Subagents observed each other's working-tree state but did NOT step on each other.** TEST-51 noted snapshot/tests.rs was being concurrently edited by TEST-50B and a transient compile-error window; TEST-51 used a saved backup workaround for its test run, then reverted. MAINT-143 noted `.sqlx/` deltas from the TEST-50B snapshot work and reverted them too. Final orchestrator-level `cargo sqlx prepare` regenerated the cache cleanly. No conflicts.
- **The orchestrator regenerates the sqlx cache once after all subagents settle.** Subagents that touch SQL should NOT run `cargo sqlx prepare` themselves (it would conflict with siblings); the orchestrator does it after the final `cargo nextest run` clean. This pattern worked well this session.
- **One commit, one prek run.** All 21 file changes ship in a single commit so the `prek run --all-files` hook covers the whole batch.
- **Incidental bug fix bonus.** MAINT-143's dedup wasn't expected to change behaviour — it just looked like a refactor. The reviewer flagged that the old `backlink/filters.rs` query was missing the inline-ref UNION (a pre-existing UX-250 contract violation) and the new helper-routed path correctly unions both. Net: REVIEW-LATER says "MAINT-143 done", and the BUG side ledger gets one more silent regression closed. This is a strong argument for dedup-style refactors when one side already has a documented invariant the other side is missing.

## Session 508 — Backend test-quality batch: close 8 TEST items (TEST-40, 41, 43, 44, 46, 47, 48, 49) (2026-04-27)

**8 TEST items closed in one batched session.** Six parallel build subagents on non-overlapping backend test files, paired with four review subagents (one per work-cluster). Pure test-only changes — no production code touched anywhere. The keyring-store subagent stalled mid-task and was replaced by direct orchestrator edits; everything else completed cleanly on the first pass.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **Top-level:** 94 open items → 86. Summary text: `24 test-quality (11 backend TEST-40..TEST-43, TEST-45..TEST-51 + 13 frontend TEST-52..TEST-64)` → `17 test-quality (4 backend TEST-42, TEST-45, TEST-50, TEST-51 + 13 frontend TEST-52..TEST-64)`. Previously-resolved bumped 542+ → 550+ across 160 sessions.
- **Backend test-quality block prose:** "12 items below bundle the 40 confirmed findings" → "items below bundle the remaining open findings".

**Items closed (8) and where they landed:**

| Item | File(s) | Change |
|---|---|---|
| TEST-40 | `commands/tests/tag_cmd_tests.rs` | Added `add_tag_deleted_block_returns_not_found` + `remove_tag_deleted_block_returns_not_found`; both `cascade_soft_delete` then assert `Err(AppError::NotFound(_))`. |
| TEST-41 | `commands/tests/tag_cmd_tests.rs` | Added `assert_eq!(count, 1)` op_log assertions to `add_tag_success` + `remove_tag_success` using `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM op_log WHERE op_type = 'add_tag' / 'remove_tag' AND json_extract(payload, '$.block_id') = ?`. |
| TEST-43 | `command_integration_tests/trash_integration.rs` | Extended `purge_all_deleted_cleans_dependent_tables` to seed + assert cleanup of `page_aliases` and `projected_agenda_cache` (the two FK tables the production purge in `crud.rs:1194-1208` deletes but the test ignored). |
| TEST-44 | `soft_delete/mod.rs` | Added `cascade_soft_delete_skips_conflict_copies` — exercises invariant #9 (`is_conflict = 0` filter in the recursive CTE) by inserting a normal child + a conflict-copy child and asserting the conflict copy is NOT swept into the cascade. |
| TEST-46 | `gcal_push/keyring_store.rs` | Rewrote `keyring_store_never_falls_back_to_plaintext_on_unavailable` to assert on the store's own `Err(AppError::Validation("keyring.unavailable: ..."))` return instead of a sibling `MemBackend` the store could never write to. Multi-line comment documents that the store has only one write path (no plaintext fallback exists in production). |
| TEST-47 | `materializer/tests.rs` | Tightened 7 metric-count assertions from `>= N` to `assert_eq!(.., N + 1)` accounting for the `Barrier` task that `flush_*` enqueues and that increments the same counter on dequeue. `high_water_*` tests intentionally untouched (high-water is recorded at enqueue, no barrier accounting). |
| TEST-48 | `materializer/tests.rs` | Added `assert_eq!(fg_errors, 1)` to `apply_op_invalid_payload` and `apply_op_unknown_op` — they previously asserted nothing, only proving "didn't panic". |
| TEST-49 | `dag/tests.rs`, `op.rs`, `commands/tests/undo_redo_tests.rs`, `mcp/tools_ro.rs`, `snapshots/agaric_lib__op_log__tests__snapshot_op_record_after_create_block.snap` | Six assertion-hygiene fixes: `is_err()` → `matches!(.., Err(AppError::InvalidOperation(_)))`; missing `is_ok()` message; `!is_empty()` → `assert_eq!(.., 3)`; two MCP `is_ok()` checks gained shape destructures (`is_array()` / `items` field); stale `assertion_line: 792` removed via `cargo insta test --force-update-snapshots`. (Site 6, `sync_daemon/tests.rs:239`, was found already fixed in upstream commit `e8ccd27` — no change.) |

**Files touched (10):**

- `REVIEW-LATER.md` (8 detail sections + 7 summary rows removed; counts bumped 94 → 86)
- `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry)
- `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/tag_cmd_tests.rs` (273 → 348 lines)
- `src-tauri/src/command_integration_tests/trash_integration.rs` (extended one test)
- `src-tauri/src/soft_delete/mod.rs` (one new test)
- `src-tauri/src/gcal_push/keyring_store.rs` (one test rewritten)
- `src-tauri/src/materializer/tests.rs` (7 assertions tightened + 2 added)
- `src-tauri/src/dag/tests.rs`, `src-tauri/src/op.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/undo_redo_tests.rs`, `src-tauri/src/mcp/tools_ro.rs` (assertion-hygiene)
- `src-tauri/src/snapshots/agaric_lib__op_log__tests__snapshot_op_record_after_create_block.snap` (metadata header only)

**Verification:** `cd src-tauri && cargo nextest run` → **2991 tests run: 2991 passed, 2 skipped**. No regressions across the full suite.

**Reviewers:** Four review subagents, each on a distinct work-cluster — Review-1 (TEST-40+41), Review-2 (TEST-43+44), Review-3 (TEST-47+48), Review-4 (TEST-49), plus Review-5 (TEST-46 after the orchestrator-direct fix). All five came back SHIP with no outstanding follow-ups. The TEST-46 reviewer additionally cross-checked the `KeyringTokenStore` for any plaintext-fallback path (none exists; the production store has only one write path through its wrapped `KeyringBackend`), confirming the new assertion-on-`result` framing is the meaningful regression guard.

**Process notes:**

- **Six-parallel subagent dispatch with non-overlapping files.** Each test file was owned by exactly one subagent so no merge-conflict risk. No worktrees needed.
- **Subagent stall handling.** TEST-46's subagent ran for ~10 min without producing output or modifying the target file; killed the shell and the orchestrator did the fix directly (~5 lines). The fallback worked because the file was already pre-read for review-readiness, so the orchestrator had full context.
- **Subagent-driven REVIEW-LATER.md edits are race-prone.** The TEST-44 subagent updated REVIEW-LATER.md inline (removed its row + detail section + bumped the summary count from 94 → 93) before the others were done. The other 7 closures were rolled up by the orchestrator at the end. For future batches: subagents should NOT edit REVIEW-LATER.md; the orchestrator handles all REVIEW-LATER edits in one pass after every subagent reports back. (The TEST-44 subagent's edits were correct in content, but the workflow risks two subagents racing to bump the same `93 → 92` line with stale snapshots.)
- **Pipelined review while builds finish.** As each build subagent reported, its review subagent launched in parallel — at peak there were 3 active builds + 3 active reviews running concurrently (6 total). End-to-end wall clock was bounded by the longest build (TEST-46's stall) rather than the sum.
- **One commit, one prek run.** All 10 file changes ship in a single commit so the `prek run --all-files` hook covers the whole batch.

## Session 507 — Architecture audit → MAINT-112 CommandTx end-to-end + H-5/H-6 close + clippy-1.95 sweep (2026-04-27)

**3 items closed (MAINT-112, H-5, H-6), 3 items filed (MAINT-112/113/114), 19 pre-existing clippy errors swept.** Long-arc session driven by an adversarial architecture audit that tested "is the architecture fit for purpose?" against four parallel explorer subagents + two reviewer subagents. The explorers produced the usual exaggerations — a claimed "70:1 LOC of sync per 1 LOC of commands" that was actually ~0.78:1, a "velocity-negative momentum" framing that the data refuted, a "ConflictFreeBlockId newtype fixes everything" recommendation the reviewer rescoped to "do not do on a deadline". The reviewers' pass refuted every dramatic recommendation; the verdict landed on **SHIP — architecture is fit for purpose, do not revisit any earlier decision, do not refactor significantly**. Three opportunistic-cleanup items came out of that audit and were filed: MAINT-112 (CommandTx), MAINT-113 (ConflictFreeBlockId, deferred indefinitely), MAINT-114 (workflows consolidation, spike first). MAINT-112 was then executed end-to-end in two phases, the pre-existing clippy-1.95 regression in the tree was swept, and the remaining two HIGH-severity materializer items (H-5, H-6) were closed — with a fix that rejected the pass-2 recommendations in favour of a simpler "remove the JoinSet entirely" redesign.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:**

- **Top-level:** 95 open items → 94 (MAINT-112 closed, MAINT-113 + MAINT-114 filed, net change −1 since the audit also filed those three in a prior commit within this session's window). Summary text: `58 planned work` → `57`.
- **Backend Code Review appendix — HIGH findings:** `(6)` → `(4)` after H-5 + H-6 deleted.
- **Stray cross-references cleaned:** MAINT-112's detail block had incorrectly cited "session 495 closed H-5 and H-6" (those items were still open until this session, and they describe JoinSet parallelism in the consumer, not the missing-dispatch failure class in the command layer — MAINT-112's actual concern). Reworded the two misattributions + removed a dangling "cf. H-3 / H-4 / H-5" reference in L-133 (only H-3 actually describes the unscoped-pages-after-bootstrap mechanism).

**Commits (5) in execution order:**

| # | Hash | Summary | Files / Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `4b33d6c` | `docs(review-later): add MAINT-112 + MAINT-113 + MAINT-114 — architecture-audit follow-ups` | `REVIEW-LATER.md`, +136 / −1 |
| 2 | `dabe449` | `feat(db): MAINT-112 — CommandTx newtype + migrate history.rs (phase A)` | 2 files, +509 / −26 |
| 3 | `d1fcf01` | `chore(clippy): clippy-1.95 sweep + bench signature drift — cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean` | 12 files, +72 / −60 |
| 4 | `8c095d6` | `feat(db): MAINT-112 phase B — complete CommandTx migration across every paired begin+dispatch site` | 15 files, +467 / −420 |
| 5 | `9206e0a` | `fix(materializer): close H-5 + H-6 — remove JoinSet parallel dispatch, run foreground tasks strictly FIFO` | 5 files, +261 / −274 |

**Arc of the session:**

| Phase | Activity | Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Audit | 4 explorer subagents on op-log/materializer/sync, frontend stores/editor, tech debt + REVIEW-LATER + commit patterns, deps + build + platforms. Then 2 adversarial reviewer subagents per the user's "review the findings for exaggerations and hallucinations" instruction. | Verdict: **SHIP — fit for purpose, no revisit, no refactor.** Every dramatic recommendation (drop sync in v1, abandon roving editor, drop Tauri, drop sqlx) refuted. Three opportunistic-cleanup items fell out. |
| 2. File follow-ups | `REVIEW-LATER.md` gains MAINT-112 (CommandTx), MAINT-113 (ConflictFreeBlockId, deferred indefinitely), MAINT-114 (workflows audit). | Commit 1. |
| 3. Phase A | Introduce `CommandTx` newtype with `Deref`/`DerefMut` to `sqlx::Transaction<'static, Sqlite>`, `enqueue_background` + `commit_and_dispatch` + `commit_without_dispatch` + `rollback` + `pending_len`, 8 unit tests. Migrate 3 callsites in `commands/history.rs` (`undo_page_op_inner`, `redo_page_op_inner`, `revert_ops_inner`) as the proof-of-concept. | Commit 2. Reviewer subagent: SHIP, high confidence. One doc precision nit applied in-place. |
| 4. Clippy sweep | Pre-existing clippy-1.95 tightening + bench signature drift had left `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` broken on HEAD (verified via `git stash` before/after). 19 issues across 7 categories: 7× `.with(|c| c.take())` → `.with(Cell::take)`, 3× `>= x + 1` → `> x`, 1× `usize::try_from(MAX_WINDOW_DAYS)` (truncation lint), 2× cast tidies in `backlink/tests.rs`, 5× doc-quote marker rewordings in `bug_report.rs`, 1× `items_after_test_module` reorder in `sync_net/connection.rs`, and 18 bench callsite fixes across 4 bench files for post-FEAT-3-Phase-2 `space_id` signature drift. | Commit 3. |
| 5. Phase B | Migrate every remaining production site that pairs `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` with a post-commit `dispatch_background*` (20 callsites across 11 files). `CommandTx` gains an `EditBackground { record, block_type }` variant for the single `edit_block_inner` caller that needs the block-type hint. `create_page_in_space_inner` + `create_space_inner` gain a `&Materializer` parameter and dispatch via `commit_and_dispatch` internally; both helper functions that had previously re-queried `op_log` post-commit to recover the freshly-appended rows (`dispatch_background_for_page_create` + `dispatch_background_for_space_create`) plus the identical re-fetch block in `create_block_inner_with_space` are **deleted entirely** — the op records never leave the transaction scope now. Python scripts handle the mechanical `&mut *tx` → `&mut **tx` rewrite (scoped to each migrated function's line range) and the 13 test-site materializer injections. Specta bindings regenerated cleanly (trailing-whitespace diff only; no public IPC signature changes). | Commit 4. Reviewer subagent: SHIP, high confidence, all 15 rubric items AFFIRM. |
| 6. H-5 / H-6 | The previous reviewer had not-quite-pushed on two HIGH items that MAINT-112's problem statement touched adjacent-to (the `dispatch_background_or_warn`-after-commit framing). On direct investigation: they describe a DIFFERENT bug class — the foreground consumer's `JoinSet` parallelism, bucketed by `extract_block_id(task)`, that could race parent/child CreateBlock ops (H-5) or could run a batch in parallel with an independent `ApplyOp` for one of the batch's non-first block_ids (H-6). The pass-2 recommendations in REVIEW-LATER called for a "root-of-op" grouping key (H-5) or multi-key overlap detection (H-6). Both were rejected in favour of a simpler fix: **delete the JoinSet, run foreground tasks strictly FIFO.** SQLite serialises writes at the engine level (WAL, single writer), so the JoinSet path bought no throughput. | Commit 5. Reviewer subagent: SHIP, high confidence, all 14 rubric items AFFIRM (one UNVERIFIABLE on historical MAINT-46 observability, resolved as "no regression"). |

**`CommandTx` surface (final shape, after phases A + B):**

```rust
// src/db.rs
pub struct CommandTx { /* inner: Transaction<'static>; pending: Vec<PendingDispatch>; label */ }
impl CommandTx {
    pub async fn begin_immediate(&SqlitePool, label: &'static str) -> Result<Self, sqlx::Error>;
    pub fn enqueue_background(&mut self, OpRecord);
    pub fn enqueue_edit_background(&mut self, OpRecord, impl Into<String>);
    pub async fn commit_and_dispatch(self, &Materializer) -> Result<usize, sqlx::Error>;
    pub async fn commit_without_dispatch(self) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error>;
    pub async fn rollback(self) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error>;
    pub fn pending_len(&self) -> usize;
}
impl Deref<Target = Transaction<'static, Sqlite>>;
impl DerefMut<Target = Transaction<'static, Sqlite>>;
```

| `commands/journal.rs` | `resolve_or_create_journal_page` (early-return uses `commit_without_dispatch`) | B |
| `commands/tags.rs` | `add_tag_inner`, `remove_tag_inner` | B |
| `commands/attachments.rs` | `add_attachment_inner`, `delete_attachment_inner` (post-commit unlink order swapped: `commit → dispatch → unlink` instead of `commit → unlink → dispatch`; benign because the materializer reads from the committed op_log, not the filesystem) | B |
| `commands/pages.rs` | `import_markdown_inner` (batch enqueue in loop) | B |
| `commands/spaces.rs` | `create_page_in_space_inner`, `create_space_inner` (both gained a `&Materializer` parameter; the two dispatch-for-*-create helpers + the identical re-fetch block in `crud::create_block_inner_with_space` deleted) | B |
| `commands/blocks/crud.rs` | `create_block_inner`, `edit_block_inner` (uses `enqueue_edit_background`), `delete_block_inner`, `restore_block_inner`, `purge_block_inner`, `bulk_restore_trash_inner`, `purge_all_deleted_inner` | B |

**H-5 / H-6 fix diff (commit 5):**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/materializer/consumer.rs` | `process_foreground_segment` — 30-line JoinSet dispatch (with a `groups.len() <= 1` fast-path) collapsed to a single FIFO for-loop. Multi-paragraph comment pins the rationale in-place so the next reviewer does not reintroduce the bucketing as an "optimisation". |
| `src/materializer/dedup.rs` | `extract_block_id` + `group_tasks_by_block_id` helpers deleted along with their 4 unit tests — no live consumers remained, `-D warnings` would flag as dead. `dedup_tasks` + `hash_id` retained for the background queue's per-block task dedup. |

| `src/materializer/tests.rs` | Deleted `batch_groups` / `batch_order` (tested the deleted helper). Renamed `parallel_groups` → `foreground_distinct_block_edits_land_in_fifo_order`. Appended H-5 + H-6 regression tests (`h5_parent_and_child_create_both_land_under_strict_fifo`, `h6_batch_and_concurrent_apply_op_serialize_in_fifo`) — both pin final block state + assert `fg_errors == 0` + `fg_apply_dropped == 0` (verifying the retry path was NOT taken). |

**Reviews:**

- Audit phase: 2 reviewer subagents (one on backend/sync audit, one on frontend + tech-debt audit). Key refutations: "70:1 sync-to-commands LOC" was actually ~0.78:1 (the explorer had included test files in the sync side); "velocity-negative momentum" was refuted by the data (sessions 489–498 closed 105 items with 4 new files); "abandon roving editor" directly contradicts the documented ARCHITECTURE.md constraint.
- Phase A reviewer: SHIP, high confidence, 10/10 rubric items AFFIRM. One doc precision nit (explicit `&mut *cmd_tx` vs. coerced `&mut cmd_tx`) applied in-place.
- Phase B reviewer: SHIP, high confidence, 15/15 rubric items AFFIRM.
- H-5 / H-6 reviewer: SHIP, high confidence, 14/14 rubric items AFFIRM (one UNVERIFIABLE on MAINT-46 historical observability — resolved as "no regression": the malformed-payload warn that previously lived inside the deleted `extract_block_id` now surfaces inside `apply_op_tx`'s `serde_json` parse path, same observability).

**Process notes:**

- **Adversarial review works.** Every high-confidence exploratory claim was tested against the code; ~1/3 were refuted or significantly downgraded (the "70:1" figure, the "velocity-negative" framing, the "abandon roving editor" recommendation). The architecture-is-fit-for-purpose verdict held up under two rounds of pressure. **Rule of thumb emerging:** explorer-only reviews are dramatic by bias; a paired reviewer subagent is cheap insurance.
- **"Delete it" as a fix strategy beat both pass-2 recommendations for H-5 / H-6.** Pass-2 proposed "root-of-op" grouping (H-5) or multi-key overlap detection (H-6); both were more elaborate than necessary. The right question was "does SQLite's WAL single-writer semantics let the JoinSet dispatch do useful work?" — answer no, so the correct simplification was "delete the JoinSet". The reviewer subagent explicitly affirmed that the simpler fix was superior to both pass-2 proposals.
- **Python scripts for mechanical rewrites pay off at scale.** The phase B migration included ~115 `&mut *tx` → `&mut **tx` rewrites scoped to specific function bodies, plus ~17 `create_page_in_space_inner(...)` test-callsite rewrites to inject `&materializer` as the 3rd argument, plus ~13 `let materializer = Materializer::new(pool.clone())` injections at the top of affected test functions. Writing one Python script per rewrite class and scoping each to a function line-range was faster and safer than manual editing across 15 files.
- **Pre-existing clippy drift is a tree-level liability.** The clippy-1.95 sweep pre-empted the prek `cargo-clippy` hook: without that sweep, every subsequent commit would have failed the hook on files the commit didn't touch. Landing the sweep as its own commit (separate from MAINT-112 phase B) made both commits cleaner to review and leaves a cleaner audit trail.
- **Specta bindings regenerate cleanly when only `_inner` signatures change.** Phase B touched 11 `_inner` functions but no Tauri command signatures. The regenerated `src/lib/bindings.ts` diff was trailing-whitespace-only. This is the expected behaviour — the `tauri-specta` pipeline reflects only the `#[tauri::command]` surface, not inner helpers.
- **5 commits in one session is on the high end.** Each commit had its own reviewer pass, so the review budget was ~4 subagent rounds including the initial audit. In a MORE-focused session I would have merged phases A + B into one commit and folded the clippy sweep into "phase A compile-prep". The separation was deliberate here so the audit-prompted filing could stand alone and so the clippy sweep could land without mixing MAINT-112 concerns.

**Files touched this session (30 unique code + 2 docs):**

- `REVIEW-LATER.md` (MAINT-112/113/114 filing → MAINT-112 closure → H-5/H-6 closure + cross-reference cleanup)
- `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry)
- `src-tauri/src/db.rs` (CommandTx module + tests)
- `src-tauri/src/commands/mod.rs`, `commands/attachments.rs`, `commands/blocks/crud.rs`, `commands/blocks/move_ops.rs`, `commands/journal.rs`, `commands/pages.rs`, `commands/properties.rs`, `commands/spaces.rs`, `commands/tags.rs`, `commands/history.rs`, `commands/bug_report.rs`
- `src-tauri/src/mcp/last_append.rs`, `mcp/tools_ro.rs`, `mcp/tools_rw.rs`
- `src-tauri/src/materializer/consumer.rs`, `materializer/dedup.rs`, `materializer/mod.rs`, `materializer/tests.rs`
- `src-tauri/src/recurrence/compute.rs`, `merge/resolve.rs`, `backlink/tests.rs`, `gcal_push/connector.rs`, `op_log.rs`, `sync_net/connection.rs`
- `src-tauri/src/command_integration_tests/common.rs`, `src-tauri/src/spaces/tests.rs`
- `src-tauri/benches/commands_bench.rs`, `benches/fts_bench.rs`, `benches/pagination_bench.rs`, `benches/undo_redo.rs`
- `src/lib/bindings.ts` (specta regen, whitespace only)

**Verification at every phase:**

- `cargo nextest run -p agaric` — 2988/2988 pass (+2 skipped) at the end of the H-5/H-6 fix. Consistent green at every phase along the way.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings` — exit 0 at every phase after commit 3.
- `cargo fmt --all --check` — clean.
- `npx tsc -b --noEmit` — exit 0.

## Session 506 — Core/materializer/compaction sweep: 4 BCR items (M-1 + M-2 + M-10 + L-42) (2026-04-27)

**4 items closed.** A non-sync defensive-coding batch spanning Core (M-1 ULID normalization on `attachment_id`), Materializer (M-10 refcount-bump clone for `BatchApplyOps`), Commands (System) (L-42 real `compact_op_log` deleted count), and a doc-accuracy fix (M-2 — ARCHITECTURE.md §15 names the actual sanitization mechanism). 3 parallel build subagents + 1 orchestrator-direct doc update; all 4 first-pass cleanly with no review-required fixes.

**REVIEW-LATER impact (BCR appendix):** Medium count 32 → 28. Domain table: Core data layer Medium 6 → 4 (M-1 + M-2); Materializer Medium 6 → 5 (M-10); Commands (System) Medium 11 → 10 (L-42).

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **M-1** (S — ULID norm) | `src-tauri/src/op.rs`, `src-tauri/src/op_log.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/attachments.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/history.rs`, `src-tauri/src/materializer/handlers.rs`, `src-tauri/src/reverse/attachment_ops.rs`, + 7 test files | `AddAttachmentPayload.attachment_id` and `DeleteAttachmentPayload.attachment_id` migrated from `String` to the existing `AttachmentId` alias of `BlockId`. `BlockId` is `#[serde(transparent)]` so the wire format is byte-identical to what shipped before, but feeding `serialize_inner_payload` → `compute_op_hash` now goes through the normalization contract required by AGENTS.md invariant #8. Without this, a frontend bug stamping a lowercase attachment_id on one device would produce a different op hash on a peer device and the hash chain would silently fail to converge — a single-user multi-device sync correctness bug. 2 production callers construct payloads via `BlockId::from_trusted(&attachment_id)`; 3 sqlx `.bind(&p.attachment_id)` sites migrated to `.bind(p.attachment_id.as_str())` (matches the canonical convention for `BlockId` binds). 2 existing op-payload snapshot files (`op_payload_json_add_attachment.snap`, `op_payload_json_delete_attachment.snap`) untouched — proof the wire format didn't move. **Test:** `attachment_id_normalization_lowercase_and_uppercase_produce_same_hash_m1` in `op_log.rs` builds two equivalent JSON payloads differing only in case, deserializes both, and asserts identical hashes. |
| **M-10** (S — refcount clone) | `src-tauri/src/materializer/mod.rs`, `src-tauri/src/materializer/consumer.rs`, `src-tauri/src/materializer/handlers.rs`, `src-tauri/src/materializer/dedup.rs`, `src-tauri/src/materializer/tests.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_protocol/operations.rs` | `MaterializeTask::BatchApplyOps(Vec<OpRecord>)` → `BatchApplyOps(Arc<Vec<OpRecord>>)`. The 3 `task.clone()` sites in `materializer/consumer.rs` (pre-first-attempt prep + two retry-dispatch sites in `run_background`) now bump a refcount instead of deep-cloning a multi-thousand-op Vec on every task. Matters most on the happy path (first attempt succeeds): previously the clone was pure waste; now it's an atomic fetch-add. Mobile (Android) RAM footprint shrinks correspondingly. Consumer pattern-match sites already use `records.first()` / `records.iter()` which compile unchanged through `Arc<Vec<T>>`'s deref — the only functional change was `handlers.rs:42` where `for record in records` had to become `for record in records.iter()` (`&Arc<Vec<T>>` isn't `IntoIterator`). 1 production construction site (`sync_protocol/operations.rs:168`) + 6 test fixtures wrapped in `Arc::new(...)`. **Test:** `batch_apply_ops_no_retry_does_not_deep_clone_m10` asserts `Arc::strong_count` semantics and `Arc::ptr_eq` identity across a task clone. |
| **L-42** (S — real compact count) | `src-tauri/src/snapshot/create.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/compaction.rs`, `src-tauri/src/snapshot/tests.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/compaction_cmd_tests.rs` | Pre-fix `compact_op_log_cmd_inner` reported `ops_deleted = eligible_in_tx` — the count eligible at the start of the pre-flight tx, NOT the count actually deleted by the subsequent `snapshot::compact_op_log`. Between pre-flight commit and inner delete, more ops could be appended (their `created_at` may still be `< cutoff` if the wall clock advanced) and the snapshot-frontier guard inside `compact_op_log` could skip some. `snapshot::compact_op_log` return type changed from `Result<Option<String>, AppError>` to `Result<Option<(String, u64)>, AppError>`; the wrapper destructures the tuple and populates `CompactionResult.ops_deleted` with the real count. `CompactionResult` wire shape unchanged (`ops_deleted: i64` stays — the new `u64` is cast). No `src/lib/bindings.ts` regen. 1 production caller updated to destructure the tuple; 4 test discard sites (`.is_some()`, `.unwrap()` without field access) unchanged. **Test:** `compact_op_log_returns_real_deleted_count_l42` asserts `result.ops_deleted == (COUNT before - COUNT after)` against the actual op_log. |
| **M-2** (S — doc accuracy, orchestrator-direct) | `ARCHITECTURE.md` | The BCR's M-2 framing was a false positive: `sanitize_internal_error` at `src-tauri/src/commands/mod.rs` IS applied at 89 of 102 Tauri command wrappers via `.map_err(sanitize_internal_error)`, collapsing `Database` / `Migration` / `Io` / `Json` / `Channel` / `Snapshot` variants to `AppError::InvalidOperation("an internal error occurred")` and emitting a `tracing::warn!` with the original. The doc's promise in §15 was upheld — it just happens at the command-wrapper `map_err`, not in `AppError`'s `Serialize` impl as the BCR assumed. Doc now names the actual mechanism: cite file/function, enumerate which variants sanitize vs pass-through, note that adding the wrapper is the contract for any new Tauri command. L-48 tracks the drift on the 13 commands that skip the wrapper — separate item, not conflated into M-2. Orchestrator-direct (no subagent needed) because the fix is a single paragraph edit. |

**Reviews:**

- R1 (technical, 21 dimensions, foreground): **PASS** with no fixes needed. All 4 items verified individually. Cross-cutting: `cargo build --tests` clean, `cargo nextest run op_log op materializer compact` shows 868 tests pass. UX reviewer correctly NOT launched — 3 code changes are backend-internal with no user-visible surface; 1 is doc-only.

**Process notes:**

- **Verifying BCR descriptions against the codebase catches false positives.** M-2's BCR description said the `AppError::Serialize` impl forwards raw messages to the frontend. Technically true at the Serialize layer, but irrelevant — 89 of 102 command wrappers transform the error via `sanitize_internal_error` BEFORE it hits Serialize, so the user-facing error IS already generic. Fixing M-2 as "change Serialize impl to sanitize" (BCR's option a) would have re-sanitized already-sanitized errors AND broken the 13 commands that rely on richer error surfaces. Orchestrator-direct reading of `commands/mod.rs` before launching a subagent revealed this — the correct fix is the doc update (option b), naming the real mechanism. **Lesson:** when a BCR description sounds like "the code doesn't do X, but the doc says it does," spend 5 minutes verifying the code's actual behavior across adjacent layers before launching a subagent to "fix" it.
- **3 parallel subagents + 1 orchestrator-direct = optimal parallelism.** The M-2 doc fix could have been a 4th subagent (with paragraph-level instructions), but reading the BCR + skimming `commands/mod.rs` is faster than spinning up a subagent context. Applying it orchestrator-direct while the 3 subagents ran returned better end-to-end time. **Rule emerging:** if a fix is a paragraph edit AND the orchestrator has already read the relevant file, do it orchestrator-direct.
- **Single-batch reviewer continues to work.** 21 dimensions across 4 items in one foreground review slot. This is now the default for defensive-coding batches regardless of domain.
- **Cross-subagent file-naming coincidence.** M-1 and M-10 both touched `materializer/handlers.rs` (M-1 for sqlx bind updates, M-10 for the `.iter()` fix). The subagents didn't conflict because they modified different lines. The M-10 subagent noted the M-1 build errors during its initial `cargo build --tests` and re-ran after a `cargo clean` once M-1's writes landed. No orchestrator intervention needed.

**Files touched (21 code + 1 doc):** `ARCHITECTURE.md` (M-2 §15 paragraph); `src-tauri/src/op.rs` (M-1 struct types); `src-tauri/src/op_log.rs` (M-1 test + test-helper); `src-tauri/src/commands/attachments.rs` (M-1 production callers); `src-tauri/src/commands/history.rs` (M-1 bind); `src-tauri/src/materializer/handlers.rs` (M-1 bind + M-10 iter); `src-tauri/src/reverse/attachment_ops.rs` (M-1 bind); `src-tauri/src/reverse/tests.rs` (M-1 fixtures); `src-tauri/src/recovery/tests.rs` (M-1 fixtures); `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/block_cmd_tests.rs` (M-1 fixtures); `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/undo_redo_tests.rs` (M-1 fixtures); `src-tauri/src/gcal_push/dirty_producer.rs` (M-1 fixture); `src-tauri/src/materializer/mod.rs` (M-10 variant); `src-tauri/src/materializer/consumer.rs` (M-10 test module); `src-tauri/src/materializer/dedup.rs` (M-10 test fixture); `src-tauri/src/materializer/tests.rs` (M-10 fixtures); `src-tauri/src/sync_protocol/operations.rs` (M-10 production); `src-tauri/src/snapshot/create.rs` (L-42 return type); `src-tauri/src/snapshot/tests.rs` (L-42 caller); `src-tauri/src/commands/compaction.rs` (L-42 wrapper); `src-tauri/src/commands/tests/compaction_cmd_tests.rs` (L-42 test).

**Commits (2):** `fix(core): close 4 BCR items (M-1, M-2, M-10, L-42)` (21 files, 424+ / 67−), `docs(session-506): close 4 BCR items + log session` (REVIEW-LATER pruning + this entry).

## Session 505 — Sync cancellation + clarity sweep: 4 BCR Medium items (M-33 + M-46 + M-47 + M-48) (2026-04-27)

**4 items closed.** A second sync defensive-coding batch focused on cancellation propagation (M-46 daemon-loop scope, M-47 file-transfer phase) plus a truncation-detection improvement (M-48) and a comment-only clarity fix (M-33). All four shipped via 3 parallel build subagents with no orchestrator-direct fallbacks needed this time. The session-504 abandonment of M-46 (CancelGuard semantics) was un-blocked here by writing a tightly-locked-in subagent prompt that picked the "return bool from try_sync_with_peer" approach explicitly, removing the option (a)/(b) ambiguity that stalled the previous run.

**REVIEW-LATER impact (BCR appendix):** Medium count 36 → 32 (4 closed). Sync stack row in domain table: 13 → 10. Commands (System) row: 12 → 11. The 4 BCR HIGH items + 6 remaining HIGH families are unchanged.

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **M-33** (S — clarity) | `src-tauri/src/commands/sync_cmds.rs` | `start_sync_inner`'s `_guard` is renamed to `_health_check_only_guard` and gets a multi-line doc-comment explaining the wrapper's `try_lock_peer` is a probe ("is the daemon currently syncing this peer?") — NOT a real exclusion lock. The guard falls out of scope when `start_sync_inner` returns microseconds later; real per-peer serialisation lives in `sync_daemon/orchestrator.rs::try_sync_with_peer` where the daemon's own `try_lock_peer` holds the lock for the duration of the actual network sync. The comment names that file/path explicitly so the next reader can find the truth without re-deriving it. Comment-only behavioural change; no tests added (no existing test asserted the unreachable "Sync already in progress" error path). |
| **M-46** (S — cancel scope) | `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/orchestrator.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/tests.rs` | `try_sync_with_peer` return type changed from `()` to `bool`: `true` iff the cancel flag was observed when the session ended, `false` for early-exit paths (backoff, lock contention, no addresses, connect failure — they didn't run a real session). The capture happens AFTER `run_sync_session` returns but BEFORE `_cancel_guard`'s `Drop::drop` fires (the guard is the first local declared so it drops last in Rust's reverse-declaration order — verified by reading the function). The daemon's Branch B (`for peer_ref in &refs`) and Branch C (`for pid in due`) capture the bool and `break` if true; Branch A (single mDNS-discovery call) discards via `let _cancelled = ...`. Four early-exit `return;` statements inside `try_sync_with_peer` were converted to `return false;`. **Tests (4):** `try_sync_with_peer_returns_true_when_cancel_observed_during_session_m46` (asserts the early-exit `false` return when the network can't be mocked), `try_sync_with_peer_returns_false_on_backoff_early_exit_m46`, `daemon_loop_breaks_round_when_cancel_observed_during_first_peer_m46` (replicates the exact `for peer_ref in &refs { let cancelled = stub(...); if cancelled { break; } }` pattern with a stub that returns true for PEER_1; asserts only PEER_1 is visited), and `daemon_loop_visits_all_peers_when_no_cancel_observed_m46` (regression guard for the no-cancel case). |
| **M-47** (M — file-transfer cancel) | `src-tauri/src/sync_files.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/orchestrator.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/server.rs` | `cancel: &AtomicBool` parameter threaded through 4 functions: `run_file_transfer_initiator`, `run_file_transfer_responder`, and the inner helpers `request_and_receive_files` + `receive_request_and_send_files` (the cancel checks live inside the inner helpers, so the parameter has to thread down). Cancel checks at per-file loop boundaries — sender at the top of the `for attachment_id in &attachment_ids` loop (falls through to the existing `FileTransferComplete` send), receiver at the top of its `loop { ... }` before `recv_json()`. **No new wire variants** — the existing `FileTransferComplete` "I'm done with files" message is reused as the cancel signal so the protocol stays backward-compatible. Production caller `run_sync_session` in the orchestrator passes its own `cancel` through (line 729). Server-side responder caller (`sync_daemon/server.rs:310`) constructs a fresh `AtomicBool::new(false)` with an inline comment explicitly noting that responder-side cancellation is a follow-up — the user-visible initiator path is the priority. 12 test call-sites of the inner helpers updated to pass `&AtomicBool::new(false)` (or a pair of `&cancel_resp` / `&cancel_init` locals where `tokio::join!` arms need two distinct refs for borrow-checker reasons). **Test:** `run_file_transfer_initiator_breaks_on_cancel_m47` — sets up two missing attachments, sets `cancel.store(true)` after file 1's bytes arrive, asserts file 1 is on disk + file 2 is NOT received + `stats.files_received == 1`. |
| **M-48** (S — truncation detection) | `src-tauri/src/sync_files.rs` | `find_missing_attachments` now compares `tokio::fs::metadata().len()` against `attachments.size_bytes` for every row. Size mismatch → re-classify as missing. Metadata errors (permission denied, race with delete, etc.) ALSO classify as missing — the defensive choice that matches AGENTS.md's "preventing accidental corruption" framing for backup/sync stacks. `tracing::warn!` cites M-48 with `attachment_id`, `path`, `expected_size`, and `actual_size` so support sessions have a breadcrumb. The function signature is unchanged (caller-compatible). The `attachments` schema is unchanged — no new `blake3_hash` column (that's the deferred "better path" the BCR also describes for content-level verification, requires user approval per "Architectural Stability"). Two existing happy-path tests had `size_bytes` adjusted from arbitrary `512` to the actual byte length of their fixture payloads (the prior tests never exercised the size comparison; arbitrary mismatched values were silently fine). **Test:** `find_missing_attachments_treats_truncated_file_as_missing_m48` — 0-byte file vs `size_bytes = 1024` is asserted to be in the missing list. |

**Reviews:**

- R1 (technical, 26 dimensions, foreground): **PASS** with no fixes needed. All 4 items verified individually plus cross-cutting build / test / AGENTS.md invariants. The reviewer ran `cargo nextest run sync_daemon sync_files sync_cmds` to spot-check. UX reviewer correctly NOT launched — every change is backend-internal with no user-visible surface beyond more accurate `tracing::warn!` lines and "cancel sync" actually stopping the round (which IS user-visible but doesn't change any layout, copy, or interaction).

**Process notes:**

- **Tightening the M-46 prompt rescued it from session 504's abandonment.** Session 504's M-46 subagent ran past the deadline because the BCR description offered options (a) and (b) without picking one, and the body of `try_sync_with_peer` is large enough that an agent without a locked-in approach can wander. This session's prompt explicitly: (1) chose "return bool from try_sync_with_peer" as THE approach, (2) listed every early-exit `return;` site to convert, (3) named the exact line where `was_cancelled = cancel.load(...)` should live (after the match block, before the `conn.close()` cleanup, before the implicit guard drop), and (4) listed every Branch (A/B/C) in the daemon loop with the exact pattern (`let cancelled = ...; if cancelled { break; }` for B/C, `let _cancelled =` for A). The subagent shipped first-pass with no orchestrator post-build fixes. **Lesson: when a previous session's subagent stalled on a multi-option BCR description, write the next prompt with the option chosen and every site enumerated.**
- **3 parallel subagents, all 3 returned cleanly first time.** Session 504 had 2 stuck builds out of 5; this session had 0 out of 3. The narrower scope per subagent (each subagent owns 1-2 closely-related items in distinct files: M-33 alone in `sync_cmds.rs`; M-46+M-47 sharing `orchestrator.rs` + `sync_files.rs`; M-48 alone in `sync_files.rs`'s `find_missing_attachments`) plus the lock-in pattern from M-46 made the difference. Cross-subagent file conflict on `sync_files.rs` between M-47 and M-48 was bounded by explicit "M-47 owns `run_file_transfer_*`, M-48 owns `find_missing_attachments`, do not touch the other's function" instructions — no merge conflict at the orchestrator level.
- **Single batch reviewer pattern continues to work.** One technical reviewer with a 26-dimension matrix replaced 4 individual reviewers. Per-item PASS/FAIL verdicts plus cross-cutting verification all in one slot. Worth using as the default for backend defensive-coding batches.

**Files touched (5):** `src-tauri/src/commands/sync_cmds.rs` (M-33 rename + comment), `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/orchestrator.rs` (M-46 bool return + Branches B/C break + M-47 caller passes `cancel`), `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/server.rs` (M-47 responder fresh-flag with deferred-trade-off comment), `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/tests.rs` (4 new M-46 tests + 3 caller updates), `src-tauri/src/sync_files.rs` (M-47 `cancel` thread through 4 functions + 12 caller updates + 1 new test; M-48 size check + 2 existing-test adjustments + 1 new test).

**Commits (2):** `fix(sync): close 4 BCR Medium items (M-33, M-46, M-47, M-48)` (5 files, 690+ / 43−), `docs(session-505): close 4 BCR Medium items + log session` (REVIEW-LATER pruning + this entry).
