## Session 504 — Sync defensive-coding sweep: 5 BCR Medium items (M-54 + M-56 + M-57 + M-58 + M-66) (2026-04-27)

**5 items closed.** A defensive-coding batch across the sync stack (cert torn-write recovery, TLS device-id binding, OnceLock migration, snapshot frontier check) and the FEAT-6 RESET path (drafts-warn). All five shipped as a single feature commit — different files, no new wire messages, no schema migrations, no architectural changes. **M-46** (cancel-flag scope between peers) was attempted but its build subagent ran past the deadline and was abandoned per the session-501 precedent — the fix needs more space than a parallel slot allowed since it requires moving `CancelGuard` from per-session to per-round semantics, a non-trivial control-flow change.

**REVIEW-LATER impact (BCR appendix):** Medium count 41 → 36 (5 closed). Sync stack row in domain table: 17 → 13 Medium. Lifecycle / Snapshots row: 18 → 17 Medium. The 4 BCR HIGH items + 6 remaining HIGH families are unchanged.

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **M-54** (S — sync_cert) | `src-tauri/src/sync_cert.rs` | `get_or_create_sync_cert` now detects an orphaned `.pem` (no matching `.hash` — the torn-write signature of a crash between the two `sync_all`s) at the top of the create path, warns, and deletes the stale `.pem` so the regen path runs cleanly. A 500 ms staleness gate (`M54_TORN_WRITE_STALENESS`) disambiguates a real torn write (post-crash, `.pem` is at minimum seconds old) from the deliberately concurrent startup race the existing `concurrent_get_or_create_returns_same_cert` test exercises (sub-millisecond gap between a winner thread's two `sync_all`s) — without the gate, a retrying loser thread would actively *delete* the winner's mid-write `.pem` and corrupt state. New tests: `get_or_create_sync_cert_recovers_from_torn_write_m54` (happy recovery — sleeps 600 ms to age past threshold) + `get_or_create_sync_cert_does_not_recover_fresh_orphan_m54` (anti-regression — fresh orphan returns `AppError::InvalidOperation`, `.pem` not deleted). |
| **M-56 + M-57** (S — TLS verifier) | `src-tauri/src/sync_net/tls.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_net/connection.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_net/tests.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/orchestrator.rs` (1-line caller update), `src-tauri/src/sync_files.rs` + `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/tests.rs` (test caller updates) | **M-56:** `PinningCertVerifier` gains optional `expected_remote_id: Option<String>`; when `Some(eid)` the verifier rejects certs whose CN doesn't match `agaric-{eid}` exactly. TOFU semantics preserved — callers without a known peer id (first-pair, tests) pass `None` and the existing `agaric-*` prefix check still applies. Production caller `try_connect_each_address` now passes `Some(peer_id)`; 19 test callsites updated to pass `None`. **M-57:** `Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Option<String>>>` → `Arc<OnceLock<String>>` for `observed_hash` shared between `connect_to_peer` and the verifier. Verifier writes via `set(...).ok()` (idempotent on chain — only the leaf is recorded). Caller reads via `get().cloned()`. No more `.lock().unwrap()`, no more poisoning. New tests: `m56_verifier_rejects_cn_for_unexpected_device_id`, `m56_verifier_accepts_cn_for_matching_device_id`, `m57_observed_hash_is_set_once_and_survives_repeated_verify`. |
| **M-58** (S — snapshot frontier) | `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/snapshot_transfer.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/server.rs`, `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/tests.rs` | Before sending `SnapshotOffer`, `try_offer_snapshot_catchup` now compares the snapshot's `up_to_seqs` against the remote's heads from the orchestrator session via the new private helper `snapshot_covers_remote_heads(snapshot_seqs, remote_heads) -> Result<(), String>`. On mismatch sends `SyncMessage::Error` (existing wire variant — no new message), returns the new `OfferOutcome::SnapshotStale { reason }`, and emits `SyncEvent::Error`. `sync_daemon/server.rs` now captures `remote_heads: Vec<DeviceHead>` from the first incoming `HeadExchange` BEFORE the orchestrator consumes it, then threads the slice through. Closes the silent failure mode where compaction-vs-retention drift could leave the responder happily offering a snapshot that doesn't cover the remote's progress, only to have the receiver re-apply an older snapshot and silently lose data. Existing `feat6_end_to_end_compact_then_snapshot_catchup` integration test was updated (the prior synthetic test was actually exercising the M-58 silent-data-loss scenario; a comment now explains why an additional FEAT6_INIT op is seeded on the responder before snapshotting). New tests: `snapshot_covers_remote_heads_truth_table` (sync, no I/O) covers empty/ahead/equal/behind/missing-device + asserts the error string names the offending device and both seqs; `try_offer_snapshot_catchup_sends_error_when_snapshot_behind_remote` is the wire-level scenario. |
| **M-66** (S — apply_snapshot drafts warn — orchestrator-direct) | `src-tauri/src/snapshot/restore.rs`, `src-tauri/src/snapshot/tests.rs` | `apply_snapshot` now reads `COUNT(*) FROM block_drafts` + samples up to 8 `block_id`s inside the same `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` wipe transaction BEFORE the existing `DELETE FROM block_drafts`, and emits a structured `tracing::warn!` with `dropped_count` + `sample_ids` when the count is > 0. The 8-row sample bounds log size on a pathological peer with hundreds of unflushed drafts; the count itself is unbounded. Surfaces drafts silently lost during FEAT-6 RESET — pre-fix this happened with no log line, no count returned, and no test asserting the drop, making "where did my typing go?" a true mystery to debug. **Applied orchestrator-direct** because the M-66 build subagent ran past its deadline (same hang pattern as M-46 and as session-501's FEAT-3p5b) — the change is small enough (~25 LOC + 1 regression test) that orchestrator-direct + immediate `cargo nextest run apply_snapshot` (9/9 pass) is faster than re-launching another subagent. New test `apply_snapshot_drops_drafts_observably_m66` seeds 3 drafts, calls `apply_snapshot`, asserts post-state empty; the comment documents that a regression re-ordering DELETE before COUNT would always count zero and silently re-introduce the bug. |

**Reviews:**

- R1 (technical, 20 dimensions, foreground): **PASS** with no fixes needed. All 5 items verified individually. Cross-cutting: `cargo build --tests` clean, `cargo nextest run sync_ snapshot` shows 526 tests pass (one flaky recovered on retry), AGENTS.md invariants preserved. UX reviewer correctly NOT launched — every change is backend-internal with no user-visible surface beyond more accurate `tracing::warn!` lines.

**Process notes:**

- **Two stuck build subagents (M-46 + M-66). One abandoned + ported to next session, one orchestrator-direct.** This is the second occurrence of the runaway-build-subagent pattern (first was FEAT-3p5b in sessions 500-501). The asymmetry: M-66 was 25 LOC + 1 test → orchestrator-direct cleanly closed it inside the same session. M-46 was a non-trivial control-flow change (`CancelGuard` scope) → safer to file as remaining open. The rule emerging from sessions 501-504: **if a stuck subagent's task is truly S-cost and the orchestrator can write the change in one focused pass, do it directly; if it's M+ or requires distinct mental models, defer to next session**. Session 503's "tight prompt → first-pass PASS" pattern works for well-scoped changes (H-8); it doesn't always work for changes that touch invariants the BCR description glosses over (M-46 specifically conflicted with the existing per-session `CancelGuard` clear-on-drop semantic, and the spec's option (a)/(b) framing didn't make the trade-off explicit).
- **Cross-subagent file conflicts on `sync_daemon/tests.rs`.** Three subagents (M-56+M-57, M-58, and indirectly the M-46 subagent that didn't finish) all needed to update test callers in `sync_daemon/tests.rs`. The M-56+M-57 subagent reported it stashed-then-popped the parallel WIP to verify its own work; the M-58 subagent reported it had to update an existing FEAT-6 integration test that was incidentally exercising the M-58 silent-data-loss scenario. The orchestrator's final `cargo build --tests` pass plus `cargo nextest run sync_ snapshot` (526 passing) confirmed the merged tree is consistent. **Lesson:** when 3+ parallel subagents touch overlapping test files, the orchestrator should expect a small reconciliation pass after the last subagent merges (here it was already-clean by the time M-46 was abandoned).
- **Single batch reviewer instead of 5 individual reviewers.** Five small backend changes with no UX impact don't warrant 5 separate review subagents — one technical reviewer covering all 5 in a 20-dimension matrix gave the same coverage with one slot instead of five. Worth doing again for this shape of batch.

**Files touched (11):** `src-tauri/src/sync_cert.rs` (+~120 / -~5, M-54 detection + 2 tests + staleness const), `src-tauri/src/sync_net/tls.rs` (M-56 expected_remote_id + M-57 OnceLock), `src-tauri/src/sync_net/connection.rs` (M-56 plumbing + M-57 OnceLock read), `src-tauri/src/sync_net/tests.rs` (3 new tests + 15 caller updates + 3 ad-hoc PinningCertVerifier struct updates), `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/orchestrator.rs` (1-line: `Some(peer_id)` for production caller), `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/snapshot_transfer.rs` (M-58 helper + 2 tests + signature change), `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/server.rs` (M-58 capture remote_heads from HeadExchange), `src-tauri/src/sync_daemon/tests.rs` (4 caller updates spanning M-56+M-57 + M-58), `src-tauri/src/sync_files.rs` (M-56+M-57 test helper update), `src-tauri/src/snapshot/restore.rs` (M-66 count + warn + comment block), `src-tauri/src/snapshot/tests.rs` (M-66 regression test).

**Commits (2):** `fix(sync): close 5 BCR Medium items (M-54, M-56, M-57, M-58, M-66)` (11 files, 834+ / 56−), `docs(session-504): close 5 BCR Medium items + log session` (REVIEW-LATER pruning + this entry).

## Session 503 — H-8 agenda cursor fix + H-3 cluster cleanup (2026-04-26)

**5 items closed.** One real backend bug fix (H-8 — `list_agenda_range` cursor encoding) plus four documentation-hygiene removals (H-3 cluster: parent + H-3a + H-3b + H-3c — all already shipped in earlier sessions but never cleaned out of REVIEW-LATER's BCR appendix). Net result: REVIEW-LATER's `HIGH findings` family count drops from 8 → 6, the Top-priority list loses H-3, the Quick wins table loses both H-3 and H-8, and the actual agenda pagination drift bug at `list_agenda_range` is fixed.

**REVIEW-LATER impact (BCR appendix):** HIGH families 8 → 6. Top-priority items 5 → 4 (renamed "Top-priority items" since the count drift is now baked-in policy as more items are closed). Quick wins table 4 → 2 rows (H-17, M-23 remain).

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **H-8** (S — backend, real fix) | `src-tauri/src/pagination/agenda.rs`, `src-tauri/src/pagination/tests.rs`, `.sqlx/` cache | `list_agenda_range`'s keyset cursor encoded a date derived from `b.due_date` / `b.scheduled_date` (BlockRow columns) into `Cursor::deleted_at`, but the SQL `WHERE` and `ORDER BY` filter on `ac.date` (the `agenda_cache.date` column). When an agenda entry's date came from a non-column source (e.g. `agenda_cache.source = 'property:custom_due'`), `ac.date != b.due_date` and the cursor drifted at page boundaries — silently skipping or duplicating entries between pages. **Fix:** refactor from `query_as!(BlockRow, …)` to raw `query!(…)` that additionally projects `ac.date as "ac_date: String"`. Build a parallel `Vec<String>` of agenda dates in lockstep with the `Vec<BlockRow>`, pre-trim both to `page.limit` so `ac_dates.last()` aligns with whatever row `build_page_response` keeps as the last row of the page. Capture `last_ac_date = ac_dates.last().cloned()` via `move` into the cursor closure. The bug-vector helper `extract_date_for_cursor` is removed entirely. `list_agenda` (single-date) is deliberately untouched — it doesn't use a composite `(date, block_id)` cursor. **Test:** new `list_agenda_range_cursor_uses_ac_date_not_block_dates_h8` regression — three blocks all with `b.due_date = '2025-01-01'` (the wrong date) + `agenda_cache.date = '2025-03-10'/'-11'/'-12'` with `source = 'property:custom_due'`. Page 1 limit=2 returns rows A+B with non-null `next_cursor`; the decoded cursor's `deleted_at == '2025-03-11'` (NOT `'2025-01-01'`). Page 2 with the cursor returns exactly C — no skip, no dupe. `pagination::tests` count: 104 → 105. `cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests` regenerated the cache. |
| **H-3** (parent), **H-3a**, **H-3b**, **H-3c** (cleanup — all shipped earlier) | `REVIEW-LATER.md` only | Verified all four entries are genuinely closed before removing: (a) H-3a — backend `create_block_inner_with_space` at `src-tauri/src/commands/blocks/crud.rs:259` rejects `block_type='page'` without `space_id` (returns `AppError::Validation`); the Tauri `create_block` IPC routes through it; tests `create_block_rejects_page_without_space_id` + `create_block_with_page_and_space_id_emits_two_ops_atomically` at `block_cmd_tests.rs:421-540` confirm the IPC contract. (b) H-3b — grep for `createBlock\(\{[^)]*blockType:\s*['"]page` across `src/` shows ZERO non-comment, non-test callsites — all 6 hits are migration comments (JournalPage, useBlockDatePicker, TemplatesView, tauri.ts) or assertion-of-non-use tests (tauri.test.ts, useBlockResolve.test.ts). (c) H-3c — `property_every_page_has_space_after_bootstrap_under_mixed_create_paths` test at `src-tauri/src/spaces/tests.rs:712` covers all three documented bypass paths (legacy IPC refusal, atomic create_page_in_space, sync replay backfill). The H-3 parent is just an umbrella — closing the three sub-items closes it. |

**Reviews:**

- R1 (technical, 14 dimensions, foreground): **PASS** with no fixes needed. Verified vector alignment between `Vec<BlockRow>` and `Vec<String>` (single-pass construction + synchronized trimming), `build_page_response` semantics (closure only invoked when `has_more = true`, after pre-trimming so `ac_dates.last()` matches the kept last row), edge cases (`ac.date` empty → impossible per schema NOT NULL + sqlx cache `nullable: false`; zero rows → closure not invoked, no panic; source filter still works). `list_agenda` (single-date) confirmed untouched. `extract_date_for_cursor` confirmed removed (zero hits anywhere). The `.sqlx/` cache regeneration was clean (one query JSON deleted, one added). No silent error swallowing, no `unsafe`, no relaxed `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`. UX reviewer was correctly NOT launched — backend-only cursor encoding fix has no user-facing surface.

**Process notes:**

- **Documentation lag is real and worth a session-half.** When PROMPT.md says "Group 3-6 related items into a batch", and the FEAT/PUB section of the summary table only has 4 actionable items left, looking deeper into the BCR appendix paid off — the H-3 cluster had been quietly shipped across sessions 497-498 (or earlier) without anyone removing the entries. Verifying-then-removing them in the same session as a real fix gave the session a clean "5 items closed" outcome instead of "1 fix, 4 stale entries left rotting". Future sessions should periodically grep for "shipped but not removed" — quick way to find these is to spot-check the open items' implementation files (`grep -n` for the symbol the entry references) before launching a build subagent.
- **The build subagent prompt was written tight enough to ship cleanly.** The H-8 fix had an obvious wrong path (rewriting `Cursor` struct, bumping a version field) and an obvious right path (use the existing `deleted_at` slot, just populate it correctly from `ac.date`). The prompt listed both and explicitly told the subagent which to pick. Returned PASS on first review with zero orchestrator post-build fixes — only one trivial `cargo fmt` line wrap that the orchestrator auto-fixed before commit.
- **No UX reviewer for backend-internal pagination changes.** PROMPT.md § 4 says "For backend-only changes, the UX reviewer is unnecessary." This was a textbook case — pure cursor-encoding bug, no IPC contract change, no user-visible surface beyond "agenda no longer skips/dupes at page boundaries" (which is itself only visible to power users with non-column-source agenda entries). Skipping the UX reviewer saved a parallel-subagent slot.

**Files touched (3 code, 2 docs):** `src-tauri/src/pagination/agenda.rs` (+44 / -22, removed `extract_date_for_cursor`, switched to `query!` + parallel ac_date Vec), `src-tauri/src/pagination/tests.rs` (+92 — single regression test), `src-tauri/.sqlx/` (1 query JSON deleted, 1 added — cache rotation), `REVIEW-LATER.md` (-52 lines: H-3 cluster + H-8 detail sections + Top-priority entry + Quick wins rows; -2 in HIGH count; section heading rename), `SESSION-LOG.md` (this entry).

**Commits (2):** `fix(pagination): H-8 — list_agenda_range cursor encodes ac.date, not block columns` (3 files, 151+ / 22−), `docs(session-503): close H-8 + H-3 cluster cleanup + log session` (this entry + REVIEW-LATER pruning).

## Session 502 — Spaces Phase 5b: per-space journal templates (FEAT-3p5b) (2026-04-26)

**1 user-facing item closed.** FEAT-3p5b — the deferred-since-session-500, abandoned-in-session-501 per-space journal templates — finally landed in a single focused frontend session. The session-501 lesson held: launching FEAT-3p5b alone (rather than batched alongside an unrelated cluster) gave the build subagent the focus it needed; one full-stack frontend subagent shipped the entire feature (helpers + JournalPage integration + SpaceManageDialog UI + 16 tests) on the first pass in normal time. Two parallel reviewers (1 technical, 1 UX) both came back with PASS / MINOR-NITS-ONLY; the orchestrator applied 1 of 3 UX polish items (`aria-describedby` linking the hint paragraph to the textarea — clear screen-reader win) and skipped the other two as gold-plating (placeholder already lists variables; the Textarea primitive's `disabled:opacity-50` already covers saving feedback).

**REVIEW-LATER impact:** 5 → 4 open items (-FEAT-3p5b). Previously-resolved counter: 541+ → 542+ across 158 → 159 sessions.

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **FEAT-3p5b** (S — frontend-only) | `src/lib/template-utils.ts`, `src/components/JournalPage.tsx`, `src/components/SpaceManageDialog.tsx`, `src/lib/i18n.ts` (+ 3 test files) | Each space block can now carry a `journal_template` text property whose `value_text` is a markdown string; per-space template takes precedence over the legacy global `journal-template` page when `JournalPage` creates a new daily page. **Frontend-only** — no Rust changes, no new IPC, no new schema. Reuses `getProperties` / `setProperty` / `deleteProperty` / `createBlock`. **`template-utils.ts`:** `loadJournalTemplateForSpace(spaceId)` calls `getProperties` and returns `value_text` (or null) of the `journal_template` row; `insertTemplateBlocksFromString(template, parentId, ctx)` splits on `\n`, drops blank lines (leading + trailing + interior), expands `<% today %>` / `<% time %>` / `<% datetime %>` / `<% page title %>` per line via the existing `expandTemplateVariables`, and emits one `createBlock` per non-empty line with per-line `try/catch` (mirrors `insertTemplateBlocks`'s resilience pattern). **`JournalPage.tsx::addBlock`:** new-page branch reads `currentSpaceId`, calls `loadJournalTemplateForSpace` first; if non-null and non-empty, runs `insertTemplateBlocksFromString` with `pageTitle: dateStr` context; otherwise falls through to the legacy `loadJournalTemplate()` flow exactly as before; defensive `try/catch` on the per-space lookup falls through to legacy on error with `logger.warn`. **`SpaceManageDialog.tsx::SpaceRowEditor`:** new `<Textarea>` row beneath the accent picker. Separate `useEffect` (cancel-flag pattern matches the emptiness probe) fetches `getProperties(space.id)` once per `space.id` and seeds the textarea state. Commit on blur — trim, no-op if unchanged, `setProperty` non-empty / `deleteProperty` empty, with toast + revert on failure (mirrors the accent picker's failure model). **Orchestrator post-build a11y polish:** added `aria-describedby` linking the hint paragraph (`<p id={journalTemplateHintId}>...overrides global...`) to the textarea so screen readers announce the per-space-overrides-global note on focus — a real screen-reader win at 4 lines of code, vs. 2 other UX-reviewer suggestions skipped as gold-plating. **i18n:** 4 new `space.journalTemplate*` keys (en only — the codebase has no `es` resource section yet, so adding one would require seeding the entire `space.*` family plus likely the rest of the i18n surface; deliberate scope limit). |

**Reviews (2 in parallel):**

- R1 (technical, 15 dimensions): **PASS** with no fixes needed. Verified the precedence-test in `JournalPage.test.tsx` actually asserts the legacy `insertTemplateBlocks` was NOT called when a per-space template exists (without that, the test could pass even if both ran). Verified the clear-test in `SpaceManageDialog.test.tsx` calls `deleteProperty` and NOT `setProperty('')` — the right semantics. Verified `journal_template` does NOT need a `property_definitions` migration: `block_properties.key` has no foreign-key constraint to `property_definitions.key`; the `accent_color` precedent (also unregistered) confirms the established pattern. The Rust `set_property_in_tx` uses `fetch_optional` on `property_definitions` so user-defined keys flow through without registration. Confirmed no `es:` resource section exists in `i18n.ts` — the en-only addition is correct.
- R2 (UX, 15 dimensions): **MINOR-NITS-ONLY**. Three suggested polish items: (a) `aria-describedby` linking hint to textarea — APPLIED; (b) persistent "Variables: today, time, datetime, page title" reference line — SKIPPED (placeholder already lists them; adding redundant on-screen text is gold-plating for an S-cost item); (c) explicit `opacity-50` while saving — SKIPPED (the existing `<Textarea>` primitive already includes `disabled:opacity-50` so the `disabled={savingJournalTemplate}` we set already gets the visual cue for free). Verified data-loss risk on close-without-blur (typing then closing the dialog drops unsaved edits) is acceptable for an S-cost item — matches the accent picker's commit-on-click model and is the same blur-on-change pattern used elsewhere; not a defect. UX reviewer flagged "one polish item if we had budget" as the persistent variable reference — filed mentally for future polish, not as a REVIEW-LATER item (gold-plating territory).

**Process notes:**

- **The session-501 deferral lesson held.** Session 501's "FEAT-3p5b stays in REVIEW-LATER and will be picked up by a future session, ideally with its own dedicated build attempt rather than batched alongside an unrelated cluster" was correct. Solo build subagent, focused prompt, normal completion time — no abandonment. The previous-session abandonment was about being parallel-launched alongside FEAT-14 with a less-focused prompt, not about FEAT-3p5b being inherently slow.
- **A single full-stack frontend subagent worked again.** No backend / frontend split needed because there's no signature handshake between layers — FEAT-3p5b is entirely additive on existing IPC (`getProperties` / `setProperty` / `deleteProperty` / `createBlock`). The session-500 "split when work crosses a layer boundary that requires distinct mental models" rule pointed correctly at "single subagent" for this work.
- **Two parallel reviewers, two perspectives, two distinct findings.** Technical reviewer caught the `property_definitions` non-registration concern (verified as a non-issue via the `accent_color` precedent + Rust `fetch_optional` pattern). UX reviewer caught the `aria-describedby` gap (applied). Neither would have caught the other's finding alone. Two reviewers in parallel is the right shape for any change with both code and user-facing impact.
- **Apply, skip, defer — orchestrator decision discipline.** All 3 UX suggestions had a defensible "apply / skip" reasoning recorded in the commit message context. For an S-cost item, applying 1 of 3 polish items (the a11y one) and skipping the rest as gold-plating is the right balance per PROMPT.md "fix what's there, don't gold-plate, don't refactor beyond the scope of the item".

**Files touched (7):** `src/lib/template-utils.ts` (+65 / -3 — 2 new exports), `src/components/JournalPage.tsx` (+~50 / -~10 — `addBlock` precedence chain), `src/components/SpaceManageDialog.tsx` (+~95 / -~3 — Textarea row + state + effect + commit handler + a11y wiring), `src/lib/i18n.ts` (+6 — 4 new keys), `src/lib/__tests__/template-utils.test.ts` (+171, 9 new tests), `src/components/__tests__/JournalPage.test.tsx` (+168, 3 new tests, total 129 in file), `src/components/__tests__/SpaceManageDialog.test.tsx` (+162, 4 new tests, total 20 in file).

**Commits (2):** `feat(spaces): FEAT-3p5b — per-space journal templates` (feature + a11y polish; 7 files, 738+ / 15−), `docs(session-502): close FEAT-3p5b + log session` (this entry + REVIEW-LATER pruning + FEATURE-MAP update).

## Session 501 — PageBrowser: unified Starred + Pages model (FEAT-14 cluster) (2026-04-26)

**4 user-facing items closed** in a single batch: the FEAT-14 umbrella + its three sub-items (FEAT-14a memo / virtualizer / keyboard-nav refactor, FEAT-14b i18n + icons + visibility flags, FEAT-14c test coverage). One full-stack frontend build subagent shipped the entire cluster cleanly on the first pass — a reminder that purely-frontend FEAT-14-class work doesn't need the 2-subagent split that backend / frontend handoffs (FEAT-3p7) require. The FEAT-3p5b attempt was deliberately deferred per the session-499 precedent — the subagent ran past 25 minutes and was abandoned without consuming orchestrator review attention.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:** 9 → 5 open items (-FEAT-14, -FEAT-14a, -FEAT-14b, -FEAT-14c). Previously-resolved counter: 537+ → 541+ across 157 → 158 sessions. The PageBrowser surface returns to a stable two-section model after FEAT-12's "any `/` flips the whole view" cliff was the last open layout problem in that view.

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **FEAT-14 + 14a + 14b + 14c** (S+M+S+S–M shipped together) | `src/components/PageBrowser.tsx`, `src/components/__tests__/PageBrowser.test.tsx`, `src/lib/i18n.ts`, `src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx`, `e2e/starred-pages.spec.ts`, `FEATURE-MAP.md` | Replaces the FEAT-12 `isTreeMode ? flat : grouped` ternary with a stable two-section row model: **`Starred`** (flat, conditional, full title) + **`Pages`** (single section interleaving top-level flat pages and namespace roots, sorted together by the active comparator). Discriminated `PageBrowserRow` union extended with `'tree-page'` kind so the row template stays small and `filteredPages[idx]` semantics differ cleanly between flat rows and namespace roots. A starred-and-namespaced page intentionally appears twice (flat row in `Starred`, nested in `Pages`) — both copies share `starredRevision` and update together on toggle. The single-page-vault shortcut (no chrome at all) is preserved. Removed `pageBrowser.otherPagesSection*` i18n keys, added `pagesSection*`. **Orchestrator-direct fixes (post-build):** (a) extended `ScrollAreaProps.viewportProps` in `src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx` with an intersection `& { [dataAttr: \`data-${string}\`]: string \| undefined }` so the new `data-has-starred` viewport tag passes TypeScript without `as` casts (and — more importantly — without future viewport `data-*` tags needing an escape hatch each time); (b) split the grouping memo into module-scope `buildSinglePageBranch` + `buildMultiPageBranch` helpers to bring biome's cognitive-complexity score below the 25 threshold (was 36); (c) extracted `hasAnyNamespacedPage` into its own `useMemo` so the inner grouping memo no longer reads `pages.some(...)` directly — keeps `useExhaustiveDependencies`'s trace clean. **Tests:** 92 PageBrowser tests pass (the build subagent rewrote the FEAT-12 `tree mode renders without grouping headers` + `a11y audit passes on tree mode` cases for the unified model and added the duplication / mixed-top-level / filter-empties-section / keyboard-nav coverage spelled out in FEAT-14c). Single new e2e spec `e2e/starred-pages.spec.ts` covers the `Starred` toggle propagation across both copies of a duplicated row. |

**Reviews:**

- R1 (FEAT-14 cluster, 17-dimension audit): **PASS** with no fixes required. The build subagent's first-pass output landed everything cleanly — including the duplication semantics (starred-and-namespaced rows render in both sections), the per-section visibility flags (`hasStarred` / `hasPages`), and the keyboard-nav `pageIndexToRowIndex` walk that includes duplicate rows. The reviewer flagged the cognitive-complexity / `useExhaustiveDependencies` biome warnings the orchestrator caught afterwards in the same prek run — those were fixed in the orchestrator's local pass before commit (helper extraction + `hasAnyNamespacedPage` memo).

**Process notes:**

- **The 2-subagent split is not a universal hammer.** Session 500 promoted backend / frontend split to "the lesson from session 499" — and it was, *for that class of work*. FEAT-14 is purely frontend with no schema, no Tauri command, no Rust touchpoint at all; one full-stack subagent shipped it without issue in normal time. The right read of session 499's lesson is "split when the work crosses a layer boundary that requires two distinct mental models", not "always split". This session validated that distinction.
- **FEAT-3p5b deferral, second occurrence of the abandonment pattern.** The build subagent for FEAT-3p5b was launched alongside FEAT-14's, ran past 25 minutes without producing output, and was abandoned per session-499 precedent. The orchestrator did not reattempt during this session — FEAT-3p5b stays in REVIEW-LATER and will be picked up by a future session, ideally with its own dedicated build attempt rather than batched alongside an unrelated cluster.
- **Orchestrator post-build fixes belong in the feature commit.** All three fixes (TypeScript `data-*` viewport prop, biome cognitive-complexity, biome `useExhaustiveDependencies`) are tied to the FEAT-14 implementation — they exist because of the new `data-has-starred` viewport tag and the new grouping memo. Bundling them into the same commit (rather than a `chore: lint fixes` follow-up) keeps `git blame` honest about why each line exists.

**Files touched (6):** `src/components/PageBrowser.tsx` (+302 / -161, the bulk of the cluster), `src/components/__tests__/PageBrowser.test.tsx` (+~250 / -~80 — 92 tests final, FEAT-12 assertions rewritten + new coverage added), `src/lib/i18n.ts` (otherPagesSection keys → pagesSection keys), `src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx` (viewportProps `data-*` accept), `e2e/starred-pages.spec.ts` (new), `FEATURE-MAP.md` (PageBrowser section regenerated for the unified model).

**Commits (2):** `feat(pagebrowser): FEAT-14 + 14a + 14b + 14c — unified Starred + Pages model` (feature + orchestrator post-build fixes; 6 files, 662+ / 170−), `docs(session-501): close FEAT-14 cluster + log session` (this entry + REVIEW-LATER pruning of 4 items + summary count update).

## Session 500 — Spaces batch: cross-space link enforcement + per-space journal (FEAT-3p7 + FEAT-3p5) (2026-04-26)

**2 user-facing items closed** + 1 new item filed (FEAT-3p5b — per-space journal templates, deliberate spec-allowed deferral). FEAT-3p7 was a re-attempt of the session-499 abandonment — split into 2 subagents (backend + frontend) per the previous session's lesson, both completed in normal time. FEAT-3p5 shipped as a single full-stack subagent. 3 review subagents pipelined behind the builds (FEAT-3p7 backend FIXES-NEEDED for missing `is_conflict = 0` filter + signature mismatch with frontend; FEAT-3p7 frontend FIXES-NEEDED for `useRichContentCallbacks` missed-callsite + the same signature mismatch from the other side; FEAT-3p5 PASS with non-blocking nits).

**REVIEW-LATER impact:** 10 → 9 open items (-FEAT-3p5, -FEAT-3p7, +FEAT-3p5b). Previously-resolved counter: 535+ → 537+ across 156 → 157 sessions. FEAT-3p7 — the previous session's abandonment — landed cleanly this time after the 2-subagent split.

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **FEAT-3p7** (M) | `src-tauri/src/commands/blocks/queries.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/pages.rs`, `src-tauri/src/mcp/tools_ro.rs`, `src-tauri/src/pagination/tests.rs`, `src/stores/resolve.ts`, `src/App.tsx`, `src/lib/tauri.ts`, `src/components/BlockTree.tsx`, `src/hooks/useBlockResolve.ts`, `src/hooks/useTemplateSelection.ts`, `src/hooks/useSyncEvents.ts`, `src/hooks/useRichContentCallbacks.ts` (+ tests + bindings + .sqlx) | Cross-space `[[ULID]]` chips now render via the existing broken-link UX (`block-link-deleted` styling, "Broken link — click to remove" tooltip, click deletes — undoable). Click does NOT navigate; per the locked-in "no links between spaces, ever" decision. **Backend:** `batch_resolve_inner(ids, space_id: Option<String>)` — the `Option` shape mirrors `list_page_history`'s FEAT-3p8 transitional pattern; `Some` = space-scoped (FEAT-3p7 broken-chip rendering), `None` = cross-space (the ~13 legacy callsites in TrashView, SearchPanel, agenda/due/done panels, TagFilterPanel, useBacklinkResolution, etc. — they'll be promoted to `Some` by FEAT-3p4 when it ships). Added `AND b.is_conflict = 0` filter (AGENTS.md invariant #9 — without it conflict copies leaked into resolution results, surfacing duplicate / corrupted titles). `get_page_inner` gains `space_id: &str` with post-fetch membership check returning `AppError::Validation` on mismatch. MCP RO `handle_get_page` reads the page's own `space` property first and threads it through (preserves "MCP agents see every space" semantics). `get_block_with_children_inner` does NOT exist in the codebase — verified via grep; `list_block_history` left intentionally unscoped per spec carve-out. **Frontend:** `useResolveStore.cache` restructured from `Map<ULID, ResolveResult>` to `Map<${spaceId}::${ulid}, ResolveResult>` with `__global__` sentinel for pre-hydrate state. New `clearAllForSpace(prevSpaceId)` action (linear prefix scan, O(n)). `App.tsx` extends the existing space-switch subscriber with a `prevSpaceIdRef`-driven effect that calls `clearAllForSpace(prev)` BEFORE the new space takes effect. 12+ consumer callsites composed into `keyFor(spaceId, ulid)`. **Tests:** 5 Rust tests (4 from spec + the orchestrator-added `batch_resolve_excludes_conflict_copies` after the `is_conflict` review finding) + ~10 frontend tests covering composite-key cache, foreign-chip broken-link UX (assertion: click does NOT call `onNavigate`), markdown-serializer round-trip stability, and the App.tsx subscriber order. |
| **FEAT-3p5** (M) | `src-tauri/src/commands/journal.rs`, `src-tauri/src/mcp/tools_ro.rs`, `src/stores/journal.ts`, `src/components/QuickCaptureDialog.tsx`, `src/lib/tauri.ts`, tests + bindings + 2 snapshots regenerated + .sqlx | Each space owns its own daily note keyed by `(date, space_id)`. **Backend:** `today_journal_inner` / `navigate_journal_inner` / `journal_for_date_inner` / `quick_capture_block_inner` + private `resolve_or_create_journal_page` all gain required `space_id: &str`. Lookup query filters by both `content` (date) AND the `space` ref property. New-page creation emits atomic `CreateBlock` + `SetProperty(space)` ops inside a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` (mirrors `create_page_in_space_inner`). MCP `JournalForDateArgs` adds required `space_id` per the FEAT-4 "agents specify which space" contract. **Frontend:** `useJournalStore` extended with `currentDateBySpace: Record<SpaceId, string>` (ISO YYYY-MM-DD) + `modeBySpace: Record<SpaceId, JournalMode>`. New `persist` middleware (was unwrapped) with `partialize` persisting ONLY the per-space slices (the flat `currentDate` / `mode` are derived from the active slice on rehydrate via the subscriber). Module-scoped `useSpaceStore.subscribe(...)` flushes outgoing → outgoing slice and pulls incoming slice → flat fields, defaulting to today + `daily` for fresh spaces and rehydrate-after-deletion fallback. `QuickCaptureDialog` reads `currentSpaceId` from `useSpaceStore` and threads it through; defensive `currentSpaceId == null` throw + error toast. Per-space journal templates DEFERRED to **FEAT-3p5b** (filed in REVIEW-LATER.md per the spec's escape clause). **Tests:** 3 new Rust tests + 9 new frontend tests in `useJournalStore.test.ts` + 2 in `JournalPage.test.tsx`. 2 snapshots regenerated for MCP tool descriptions + `journal_for_date` response shape. |

**Reviews (3 reports collected — all pipelined behind their builds):**

- R1 (FEAT-3p7 backend): **FIXES-NEEDED — missing `is_conflict = 0` filter** in `batch_resolve_inner`. Without it, conflict copies leak into resolution results and surface duplicate titles in breadcrumbs / link chips. **Orchestrator-direct fix:** added the filter + a new `batch_resolve_excludes_conflict_copies` Rust test that seeds an original + a conflict copy with identical content and asserts only the original is returned. Also flagged: `get_block_with_children_inner` was confirmed not to exist (subagent's check verified); `list_block_history` carve-out accepted (admin/diagnostics surface).
- R2 (FEAT-3p7 frontend): **FIXES-NEEDED — `batchResolve` optional/required mismatch + `useRichContentCallbacks` missed.** The frontend wrapper kept `spaceId` optional but the backend enforced required, so ~13 legacy cross-space callers (TrashView / SearchPanel / agenda / due / done / TagFilterPanel / useBacklinkResolution / etc.) would have crashed with serde "expected String, found null" at runtime. AND: 5 `cache.get` callsites in `useRichContentCallbacks.ts` + `useTagClickHandler` were missed during the cache-key restructure (the file is the lightweight rendering subset of `useBlockResolve` and didn't surface in the spec's "consumer callsite" list). **Orchestrator-direct fix:** changed the backend signature from `space_id: String` to `space_id: Option<String>` (mirrors session 498's FEAT-3p8 transitional pattern) — when `Some`, scope; when `None`, cross-space. The 13 legacy callsites work as before; FEAT-3p7's actual enforcement is via `useResolveStore.preload(currentSpaceId)` + the cache key + the App.tsx subscriber, not per-call `batchResolve`. Updated `useRichContentCallbacks` (4 resolves + `useTagClickHandler`) to compose composite keys via `keyFor(currentSpaceId, id)`. Updated 2 test fixtures (`PropertyRowEditor.test.tsx`, `BlockPropertyDrawer.test.tsx`) to use `keyFor(null, id)` for the `__global__`-sentinel default.
- R3 (FEAT-3p5): PASS with 2 non-blocking nits. The atomicity of `resolve_or_create_journal_page` (M-22 TOCTOU fix) is preserved — concurrent test still passes. Date↔string conversion at the slice boundary is correct (`dateToISO` + `parseISODate` helpers). `partialize` correctly excludes flat fields. MCP `space_id` is required (preferred contract). Snapshot regeneration is minimal and focused. Nits: (a) FEAT-3p5b was added to the detail section but missing from the summary table — orchestrator added it. (b) No QuickCaptureDialog null-space test (other components have one) — left as a future polish.

**Orchestrator-direct fixes during the commit dance (the long tail):**

After the feature changes landed:

- **Clippy `manual_let_else`** on `mcp/tools_ro.rs::handle_get_page`'s space-id `match`. Refactored to `let Some(space_id) = … else { … };` per clippy's suggestion.
- **`cargo fmt`** cleanup across multiple files (subagent leftovers + post-merge).
- **`biome format`** cleanup on 6 files (post-merge).
- **3 single-line `batch_resolve_inner(...)` callsites** in test files needed `Some(...)` wrapping after the `Option<String>` signature change. Bulk `sed` script + 3 manual fixes.
- **2 multi-line `batch_resolve_inner(...)` callsites** in `pagination/tests.rs` property test needed `Some((*candidate_space).to_string())` wrapping.

**Quality gate:**

- `cd src-tauri && cargo nextest run -p agaric` → all tests pass (full suite via prek).
- `npx vitest run` → all affected files green.
- `prek run --all-files` → all 33 hooks pass.
- `cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests` regenerated the cache (5 new entries committed: 3 from FEAT-3p7's space-membership query + journal's per-space lookup, 2 net adds across the boundary).
- `bindings.ts` regenerated — `batchResolve(ids, spaceId: string | null)`, `quickCaptureBlock(content, spaceId: string)`. The `string | null` shape on `batchResolve` is the cleanest signal that the frontend may pass `null` for the cross-space callsites and a real space ID for the in-space ones.
- 2 commits ahead of session-499 baseline:
  - `67247e8` `feat(spaces): FEAT-3p7 + FEAT-3p5 — cross-space link enforcement + per-space journal`
  - `<this commit>` `docs(session-500)` — REVIEW-LATER + SESSION-LOG + FEATURE-MAP updates

**Notes for next session:**

- **FEAT-3p4 promotes the legacy callsites.** `batch_resolve_inner`'s `Option<String>` is the transitional shape. When FEAT-3p4 ships, every legacy caller (TrashView / SearchPanel / agenda / due / done / TagFilterPanel / useBacklinkResolution / etc.) gets promoted to pass `currentSpaceId`, and the `?2 IS NULL OR …` clause becomes dead code. At that point the parameter can be tightened back to `String` (with `bindings.ts` losing the `| null`). File a `MAINT-*` item only if there's risk of forgetting; the inline doc comment in `batch_resolve_inner` already calls this out.
- **Subagent split for cross-cutting work.** The session-499 FEAT-3p7 single-subagent runaway (25+ min, no output) was solved this session by splitting backend + frontend into 2 subagents. Each had a tight scope and finished in normal time. The pattern: when a feature touches 8+ files across both stacks AND requires a structural change (cache key restructure, IPC widening), default to splitting.
- **Test fixture brittleness around the cache key restructure.** Tests that constructed `useResolveStore.cache` literals via `new Map([[ulid, value]])` broke after the composite-key change. There were 2 such tests this session (`PropertyRowEditor.test.tsx`, `BlockPropertyDrawer.test.tsx`); next time scan for `cache: new Map(` to find them BEFORE running prek, and bulk-update via sed/Python.
- **`useRichContentCallbacks` missed during the FEAT-3p7 frontend pass.** The file is the "lightweight rendering subset" of `useBlockResolve` and isn't an obvious cache-key consumer. When extending a cache architecture, grep for ALL `.cache.get(` and `cacheRef.current.get(` callsites — not just the obvious ones — and double-check the "unused suppression"-flagged biome-ignore reports for the canonical "you missed a thing" signal. This session's grep caught 11 files; 1 of those was missed by the subagent and surfaced via failing prek vitest.
- **MCP `JournalForDateArgs.space_id` is required.** Per FEAT-4's "agents specify which space" contract. Document this clearly in the FEAT-4 v2 RW spec when it lands so write-tool args follow the same pattern.
- **`is_conflict = 0` is the canonical filter for every space-scoped query.** Session 500's review caught the missing filter on `batch_resolve_inner`; session 498 caught a similar (different shape) issue on `delete_block_inner`'s space guard. Adopt as a standing rule: every new query that joins `block_properties` for space scoping MUST include `AND b.is_conflict = 0` on the parent `blocks` row, regardless of whether the spec mentions it. The recursive-CTE pattern in AGENTS.md invariant #9 generalizes to ALL space queries, not just CTE walkers.

## Session 499 — Spaces batch: PageBrowser invisible-page invariant + per-space visual identity (BUG-1 + FEAT-3p10) (2026-04-26)

**2 user-facing items closed** out of a planned 3 — the third (FEAT-3p7 cross-space link enforcement) was abandoned mid-build after its subagent ran 25+ minutes without completing. BUG-1 + FEAT-3p10 shipped via parallel build subagents on a single working tree, with pipelined reviewers (FEAT-3p10 PASS; BUG-1 FIXES-NEEDED for one missed callsite that the orchestrator fixed directly). Single feature commit + this docs commit.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:** 12 → 10 open items (-BUG-1, -FEAT-3p10). Previously-resolved counter: 533+ → 535+ across 155 → 156 sessions. FEAT-3p7 stays open — re-launches in the next batch with a tighter prompt.

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **BUG-1** (S–M) | `src-tauri/src/commands/blocks/crud.rs`, `src-tauri/src/spaces/bootstrap.rs`, `src/components/JournalPage.tsx`, `src/components/TemplatesView.tsx`, `src/components/WelcomeModal.tsx`, `src/hooks/useBlockDatePicker.ts`, tests + e2e + bindings.ts | Pages created via `createBlock({ blockType: 'page' })` without a `space` property used to disappear from PageBrowser after the bootstrap fast-path triggered. Daily user impact via `JournalPage.handleAddBlock`. Four-part fix landing together so partial fixes don't leave the silent-divergence behaviour partially in place: **H-3a** new `create_block_inner_with_space` wrapper rejects `block_type='page' + space_id=None` with `AppError::Validation` and otherwise routes through `create_page_in_space_inner`'s atomic 2-op tx; non-page block types ignore `space_id` and fall through unchanged so the 565+ existing `create_block_inner` callsites remain valid. **L-133** bootstrap fast-path split: seeded `is_space="true"` ops keep their once-per-boot guard; the `pages_without_space` backfill runs on every boot (idempotent additive scan against `idx_block_properties_space`). **H-3b** 4 frontend callsites swapped from `createBlock(blockType:'page')` to `createPageInSpace` — `JournalPage.tsx` (3), `TemplatesView.tsx` (1), `WelcomeModal.tsx` (2), and `useBlockDatePicker.ts` (the date-picker "date" mode site that the deep-CR finding missed and the BUG-1 reviewer caught). Each adds a defensive `currentSpaceId == null` throw. **H-3c** deterministic 5-step regression test in `spaces/tests.rs::property_every_page_has_space_after_bootstrap_under_mixed_create_paths` (the codebase has neither `proptest` nor `quickcheck` set up — the spec's fallback recipe applies). New e2e `spaces-coverage.spec.ts` verifies Welcome + Journal + Templates flows all surface in the active space's PageBrowser. |
| **FEAT-3p10** (M) | `src-tauri/src/spaces/bootstrap.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/spaces.rs`, `src/index.css`, `src/components/SpaceAccentBadge.tsx` (NEW), `src/components/SpaceStatusChip.tsx` (NEW), `src/lib/tauri.ts`, `src/stores/space.ts`, `src/App.tsx`, `src/lib/i18n.ts`, `src/lib/tauri-mock/handlers.ts`, tests + bindings.ts + 2 .sqlx files | Per-space visual identity. Switching space now flips four signals at once: SpaceSwitcher label (existing), new sidebar-footer status chip (`SpaceStatusChip`), new collapsed-rail accent badge (`SpaceAccentBadge`), and OS window title prefix (`<SpaceName> · Agaric`). Backend: `SpaceRow` extended with `accent_color: Option<String>` via `LEFT JOIN block_properties ... key='accent_color'`. Bootstrap seeds `accent-emerald` for Personal and `accent-blue` for Work via a new idempotent `ensure_accent_color_property` helper that only fires when no value already exists; 2 Rust tests prove single-fire + cross-boot stability. Frontend: 7 oklch accent tokens in `index.css` (emerald / blue / violet / amber / rose / slate / orange) with `:root` + `.dark` + `prefers-contrast: more` triplets. New `--accent-current` CSS var bound by an `App.tsx` effect on `currentSpaceId` change. New `SpaceAccentBadge` (32 px circle, 44 px on `pointer:coarse`, first letter on accent ground; click cycles to the next space). New `SpaceStatusChip` (left-bordered chip with accent stripe; click focuses + opens the SpaceSwitcher). New `setWindowTitle` Tauri wrapper with no-op fallback for non-Tauri runtimes. `useSpaceStore.getCurrentAccent()` selector. |

**Reviews (2 reports collected — FEAT-3p10 PASS, BUG-1 FIXES-NEEDED):**

- R1 (FEAT-3p10): PASS, no fixes needed. LEFT JOIN correctly preserves spaces with `accent_color: null`. Bootstrap idempotency verified (only 1 op per seeded space across 2 boots). `setWindowTitle` no-op fallback safe for vitest. CSS `--accent-current` references `var(--<token>)` (semantic), never literal hex. Touch targets 44 px on coarse pointers. Tests cover all 4 signals (CSS update + window title + chip + badge). Two non-blocking nits: missing explicit test for null-accent space appearing in `list_spaces` (SQL structure guarantees it), no RTL test for the chip's left-border (semantic styling handles it). Reviewer flagged the `mockedToastError.mockClear()` workaround in the FEAT-3p11 `Ctrl+5` test as a temporary stopgap pending BUG-1's review.
- R2 (BUG-1): **FIXES-NEEDED — missed callsite at `useBlockDatePicker.ts:136`.** The deep-CR finding listed JournalPage / TemplatesView / WelcomeModal but missed the date-picker's "date" mode handler, which still called `createBlock({ blockType: 'page', content: dateStr })` and would leak date pages on every "/date" command. **Orchestrator-direct fix:** migrated the callsite to `createPageInSpace` with the `currentSpaceId == null` throw, plus updated `useBlockDatePicker.test.ts` (3 tests + new `useSpaceStore.setState` in `beforeEach`) + updated `BlockTree.test.tsx`'s date-format regression test. R2 also noted: 8-arg signatures on `create_block_inner_with_space` + `create_block` triggered `clippy::too_many_arguments`; orchestrator added `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]` with rationale comments (refactoring to a builder pattern for 565+ callsites was out of proportion to the gain). 130+ `.sqlx/` deletions reverted by the builder were verified safe (other agents' refactors, not BUG-1's).

**Orchestrator-direct fixes during the commit dance (the long tail):**

After the feature changes landed in the working tree, multiple downstream test files broke from the structural changes:

- **`SpaceRow` adoption fanout (FEAT-3p10).** `SpaceRow` gained a required `accent_color: Option<String>` field. 14 frontend test files (App / BlockTree / HistoryView / JournalPage / PageBrowser / PageHeader / SearchPanel / SpaceManageDialog / SpaceSwitcher / TemplatesView / WelcomeModal / useBlockResolve / navigation / recent-pages) carried `SpaceRow` literals like `{ id: 'X', name: 'Y' }` that no longer satisfied the type. Bulk-fixed via a Python script that injected `, accent_color: null` before the closing `}` on each literal. Total fixed sites: ~30+ across 14 files.
- **`createBlock` IPC payload widening (BUG-1).** `createBlock` now always sends `spaceId: null` for non-page block types (since the wrapper forwards `params.spaceId ?? null`). 14 frontend test files asserting on the IPC payload shape needed `spaceId: null` added to their expected objects. Bulk-fixed via a second Python script that brace-counted `'create_block', { ... }` blocks and injected `spaceId: null,` before the matching `})`. Total fixed sites: ~14.
- **Date-picker test drift (BUG-1).** `BlockTree.test.tsx`'s "creates date page in YYYY-MM-DD format" test asserted on `create_block` for the date-picker flow, but the H-3b migration of `useBlockDatePicker` shifted that callsite to `create_page_in_space`. Test updated to assert the new IPC + the active space + a negative guard against the legacy bypass.
- **`cargo fmt` (subagent leftovers).** Both subagents left some hand-formatted Rust that diverged from rustfmt's preference; one `cargo fmt --all` cleaned it up.
- **Biome formatter on `e2e/spaces-coverage.spec.ts` + on the Python-injected fixtures across `App.test.tsx`** (the script's `accent_color: null` insertion at JSX-prop boundaries triggered single-line collapses); one `npx biome format --write` resolved both.

**Quality gate:**

- `cd src-tauri && cargo nextest run -p agaric` → all tests pass after the BUG-1 + FEAT-3p10 + delete-block guard land.
- `npx vitest run` → all affected files green; full suite via `prek` confirmed.
- `prek run --all-files` → all 33 hooks pass after the orchestrator-direct cleanups above.
- `cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests` regenerated the cache; 3 new entries committed (1 from BUG-1's `create_block_inner_with_space` op-log re-fetch, 2 from FEAT-3p10's accent-color presence checks + new LEFT JOIN).
- `bindings.ts` regenerated via `cargo test -- specta_tests --ignored` — `SpaceRow.accent_color` + `createBlock.space_id` both surfaced. `ts_bindings_up_to_date` test passes.
- 2 commits ahead of session-498 baseline:
  - `f57a634` `feat(spaces): BUG-1 + FEAT-3p10 — restore PageBrowser invisible-page invariant + per-space visual identity`
  - `<this commit>` `docs(session-499)` — REVIEW-LATER + SESSION-LOG + FEATURE-MAP updates

**FEAT-3p7 abandonment notes (for the next session):**

The FEAT-3p7 build subagent ran 25+ minutes without producing output. Likely root causes: the cross-space `[[ULID]]` enforcement work touches a lot of surfaces (`batch_resolve_inner`, `get_page_inner`, `get_block_with_children_inner`, `useResolveStore` cache key restructure, `App.tsx` subscriber extension, BlockLink editor extension, markdown serializer round-trip test). The prompt asked the subagent to also restructure `useResolveStore.cache` from flat `Map<string, ResolveResult>` to nested `Map<spaceId, Map<ulid, ResolveResult>>` (or composite keys) — that ripples through every `cache.get(ulid)` consumer. Next session: split FEAT-3p7 into TWO subagents — one for backend (`batch_resolve` + `get_page` membership check + tests) and one for frontend (resolve-store cache key + App.tsx subscriber + BlockLink + tests). This shrinks each subagent's surface and avoids the 25+ minute single-subagent runtime.

**Notes for next session:**

- **Subagent timeout cap.** When a single subagent runs past ~15-20 minutes without producing output, kill it and either re-launch with a narrower scope or split it across 2 subagents. The PROMPT.md guideline "5-6 concurrent subagents" implicitly assumes each finishes in well under an hour. FEAT-3p7's 25+ minute runaway this session blocked the batch; the abort + ship-without-it call kept the session productive but cost one item.
- **`SpaceRow` type churn ripple.** Adding a required field to a widely-imported type (like `SpaceRow.accent_color`) breaks every test file that constructs the literal. Two cleaner alternatives next time: (a) make the new field optional via `accent_color?: string | null` so existing literals still satisfy the type, then enforce non-optionality at callsites; (b) introduce a `makeSpaceRow()` factory in `src/__tests__/fixtures/index.ts` and migrate all literals to it before adding the field. Bulk Python regex worked here but is fragile for multi-line literals.
- **`createBlock` IPC payload widening.** When the wrapper always forwards `params.spaceId ?? null`, EVERY existing test that pins the full IPC payload shape needs the new field added. Same root cause as the SpaceRow case — pin-shape assertions are brittle. Future tests should prefer `expect.objectContaining({ ... })` over full-shape assertions for IPC payloads where the shape may grow.
- **Clippy `too_many_arguments` and `_inner` shapes.** `create_block_inner` was already at the 7-arg clippy limit; adding `space_id` made it 8. Adding `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]` with a rationale comment is the right call when the alternative (refactoring 565+ callsites to an args struct) is wildly disproportionate. File this as a "structural cleanup" candidate only if a future change pushes the wrapper to 10+ args.
- **`.sqlx/` cache deletions.** `cargo sqlx prepare` will queue deletions for queries whose source code has been removed. If you see "130+ deletions" queued, double-check whether they're (a) genuinely stale (then keep them), (b) belonging to other agents' uncommitted work (then `git checkout -- src-tauri/.sqlx/` to revert and only keep new entries you added), or (c) legitimate deletions from your own work (then commit them). The BUG-1 builder's revert was correct here — the deletions belonged to other agents' refactors.
- **Sonner mock + `toast.error` assertions** (carried forward from session 498). Tests asserting toast firings must use `expect(vi.mocked(toast.error)).toHaveBeenCalledWith(...)` against the global `vi.mock('sonner')` mock, NOT `screen.getByText(...)` against the toast copy (the mock returns `vi.fn()`, not a DOM-mounted toast). The FEAT-3p11 `Ctrl+5` test's `mockedToastError.mockClear()` workaround now stays in place permanently — the JournalPage auto-create flow legitimately fires `toast.error` during App mount when the test mock returns empty/wrong data, and clearing the mock pre-assertion is the correct scoping.

## Session 498 — Spaces batch: digit hotkeys + history scoping + manage dialog + Personal→Work migration (FEAT-3p11 + FEAT-3p8 + FEAT-3p6 + MAINT-1) (2026-04-26)

**4 user-facing items closed** — all FEAT-3 phases or spaces-adjacent. Shipped via 4 parallel build subagents on a single working tree (no worktrees) + 4 review subagents pipelined behind each build, plus an orchestrator-direct backend hardening fix (delete-block guard against orphaning) flagged by the FEAT-3p6 reviewer. Single feature commit + this docs commit.

**REVIEW-LATER impact:** 16 → 12 open items (-FEAT-3p6, -FEAT-3p8, -FEAT-3p11, -MAINT-1). Previously-resolved counter: 529+ → 533+ across 154 → 155 sessions. A concurrent agent added FEAT-14, FEAT-14a, FEAT-14b, FEAT-14c (PageBrowser unified namespace tree) into the table during this session — those carry into the next batch unaffected.

**Batch composition:**

| ID | Files | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **FEAT-3p11** | `src/lib/keyboard-config.ts`, `src/App.tsx`, `src/components/SpaceSwitcher.tsx`, `src/lib/i18n.ts` (+ tests) | `Ctrl+1`…`Ctrl+9` (`Cmd+1`…`Cmd+9` on macOS) switch directly to the Nth space in alphabetical `availableSpaces` order. Existing `matchesShortcutBinding` accepts both `ctrlKey` and `metaKey` — no platform-aware case-by-case wiring needed. New global keydown effect in `App.tsx` with `e.repeat` guard + `isTypingInField` short-circuit + silent no-op for out-of-range (`availableSpaces[n-1] == null`) and same-space (`target.id === currentSpaceId`). Hint chips on each non-disabled SelectItem (`Ctrl+N` / `⌘N`) for discoverability — chips scoped via `MAX_HOTKEY_SPACES = 9` and a `spaceHotkeyHint(idx)` helper. The "Manage spaces…" placeholder row carries no chip (FEAT-3p6 owns that line). Tests: 3 in App.test.tsx + 1 in SpaceSwitcher.test.tsx. |
| **FEAT-3p8** | `src-tauri/src/pagination/history.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/history.rs`, `src/components/HistoryView.tsx`, `src/components/HistoryFilterBar.tsx`, `src/lib/i18n.ts` (+ tests + bindings.ts) | `HistoryView` defaults to current-space-only when `pageId === '__all__'`. Backend `list_page_history` gained `space_id: Option<&str>`; the global query appends `AND (?7 IS NULL OR json_extract(payload,'$.block_id') IN (SELECT bp.block_id FROM block_properties WHERE key='space' AND value_ref = ?7))` only when both conditions are met. Per-page mode (real ULID) ignores the param. New "All spaces" Radix `Switch` in HistoryFilterBar (off by default, NOT persisted across sessions). Frontend computes `effectiveSpaceId = showAllSpaces ? undefined : currentSpaceId` and threads to the IPC; selection auto-clears on filter flip. Empty-state copy `history.emptyCurrentSpace` ("No changes in this space yet…") only when scoped + a space is active; otherwise the existing `noEntriesFound` falls through. The new HistoryFilterBar props are OPTIONAL so the per-page `HistoryPanel` consumer doesn't need to thread them — toggle hides when either is omitted. 3 new Rust tests + 5 HistoryFilterBar tests + 3 HistoryView tests. 14 undo_redo + 12 pagination call sites updated to pass `None`. `bindings.ts` regenerated. |
| **FEAT-3p6** | `src-tauri/src/commands/spaces.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/blocks/crud.rs`, `src-tauri/src/commands/mod.rs`, `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, `src/components/SpaceManageDialog.tsx` (NEW), `src/components/SpaceSwitcher.tsx`, `src/lib/tauri.ts`, `src/lib/tauri-mock/handlers.ts`, `src/lib/i18n.ts` (+ tests + bindings.ts + 5 .sqlx files) | SpaceSwitcher's "Manage spaces…" placeholder enabled; opens new `SpaceManageDialog` (Radix Dialog). Per-row inline rename (existing `editBlock`), accent-color picker (7-swatch grid storing `accent_color` text property — consumed later by FEAT-3p10), delete (disabled when non-empty AND on the last remaining space) with `AlertDialog` confirmation. Inline create-new-space form via the new atomic `create_space` Tauri command (`BEGIN IMMEDIATE` → CreateBlock(page) → SetProperty(`is_space`='true') → optional SetProperty(`accent_color`) → commit). First-render onboarding hint with localStorage dismissal. **Defense-in-depth:** `delete_block_inner` now refuses to delete an `is_space="true"` block while it still carries child pages; the check runs INSIDE the existing `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` tx so no concurrent `CreateBlock` with-`space`-property can sneak in between count and cascade. tauri-mock-parity adds a `create_space` handler so e2e flows don't silently get `null`. i18n: 1 key removed (`space.manageComingSoon`), 1 modified (`space.manage` — dropped trailing "ⓘ"), 21 added. 3 Rust unit tests + 2 new delete-guard tests + 16 SpaceManageDialog tests (incl. IPC rejection-path) + 1 SpaceSwitcher test. |
| **MAINT-1** | `src-tauri/src/spaces/bootstrap.rs`, `src-tauri/src/spaces/mod.rs`, `src-tauri/src/spaces/tests.rs`, `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` (+ 3 .sqlx files) | New boot-time `migrate_personal_pages_to_work` routine. Two guards: (1) fast-path skip via `personal_to_work_migration_v1` marker on `SPACE_PERSONAL_ULID`; (2) ULID time-gate via `MIGRATION_THRESHOLD_ULID = "01KQ5WWYR00000000000000000"` (the ULID for `2026-04-26T22:00:00Z`, computed via `Ulid::from_parts(timestamp_ms, 0)` — random portion zeroed for determinism). For every page where `id < THRESHOLD AND space=Personal AND is_space != "true"`, emits `set_property(space=Work)` op via `set_property_in_tx`; marks complete with marker SetProperty in the same `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` tx. Telemetry post-commit logs `pages_moved` + `threshold` + `marker_set`. Boot wiring at `lib.rs:638` runs after `bootstrap_spaces` succeeds; failure is non-fatal (warn + retry next boot). 7 Rust tests covering the 6 install-matrix scenarios from the spec plus the ULID-validation guard, including a `multi_thread` concurrent-runs test that proves the in-tx marker re-check + `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` serialization yield exactly N ops, not 2N. |

**Reviews (4 reports collected — all dimensions checked, one BLOCKING fix applied directly):**

- R1 (FEAT-3p11): PASS, no fixes needed. Confirmed `matchesShortcutBinding` accepts both `ctrlKey` + `metaKey` for the macOS path; `isTypingInField` covers TipTap's contenteditable; out-of-range and same-space short-circuit cleanly; `availableSpaces` is backend-sorted; coexistence with FEAT-3p6's `MANAGE_SENTINEL` placeholder is clean (different lines in SpaceSwitcher.tsx). Only nit: no explicit macOS test (`metaKey=true`) — implementation is correct, coverage gap acceptable.
- R2 (FEAT-3p8): PASS, no fixes needed. SQL precedence correct; per-page mode genuinely ignores `space_id`; cursor stability preserved across filter changes; `revert_ops` invariant honored; runtime `query_as` justified (dynamic optional WHERE clause that can't use the compile-time macro). 8 new tests + 3 Rust tests + bindings.ts regenerated.
- R3 (FEAT-3p6): **BLOCKING — orphan-page risk.** Frontend disables Delete only when the empty-check IPC returns 0 pages, but the BACKEND `delete_block_inner` had no `is_space=true` + child-count guard. A concurrent device creating a page in the same space between the frontend probe and the delete IPC would leave the space soft-deleted with orphan pages whose `space` ref now dangles. **Orchestrator-direct fix:** added the guard inside the existing `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` tx in `crud.rs` — no race window between count and cascade. Two new Rust tests in `commands/spaces.rs::tests_p6` (`delete_block_refuses_to_delete_non_empty_space` + `delete_block_allows_deletion_of_empty_space`) prove the guard rejects non-empty spaces with `AppError::InvalidOperation` while still allowing empty deletions. Other reviewer findings (atomicity test doesn't exercise rollback — acceptable since `create_space_inner` has no validation that could fail; missing IPC failure-path tests for rename/accent/create/delete — non-blocking, error handling is in place) deferred. Test count: 16 SpaceManageDialog (added 1 IPC-rejection test for the `prek` `ipc-error-path-coverage` hook).
- R4 (MAINT-1): PASS with 2 non-blocking nits. Boot order verified (`bootstrap_spaces` then migration), failure handling acceptable (non-fatal + warn-log retried next boot — captured in bug-report bundle), `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` + in-tx marker re-check serializes concurrent runs to exactly N ops, ULID time-gate uses TEXT lexicographic comparison (correct given Crockford base32 = timestamp order), space-block exclusion via `NOT EXISTS is_space="true"` matches spec. Nits: (a) marker property `personal_to_work_migration_v1` is visible in the user's properties panel for Personal — could prefix with `__` to mark system-internal; deferred. (b) Permanent-failure silent-loop risk extremely low (only writes are op_log appends + property UPSERTs); accept as-is.

**Orchestrator-direct fixes for hook regressions (during commit dance):**

- `src/lib/__tests__/tauri.test.ts` two cases asserting `listPageHistory` payload shape needed `spaceId: null` added (FEAT-3p8 widens the IPC). Updated with comments explaining why.
- `src/lib/tauri-mock/handlers.ts` needed a new `create_space` handler so the `tauri-mock-parity` hook doesn't fail on Playwright runs (FEAT-3p6's new IPC). Mirrors `create_page_in_space`'s handler shape.
- `src/components/__tests__/SpaceManageDialog.test.tsx` needed an IPC-rejection test (`ipc-error-path-coverage` hook). Asserts `vi.mocked(toast.error).toHaveBeenCalledWith(t('space.renameFailed'))` against the Sonner mock (the global `vi.mock('sonner')` in `test-setup.ts` makes `toast.error` a `vi.fn()` rather than a DOM-mounted toast).
- `src/components/HistoryFilterBar.tsx` props `showAllSpaces` / `onShowAllSpacesChange` made OPTIONAL so the per-page `HistoryPanel` consumer doesn't need to thread them; the toggle hides when either is omitted. The FEAT-3p8 builder hadn't accounted for the `HistoryPanel` consumer that doesn't show "All spaces" — TypeScript surfaced it during `prek run`'s tsc hook.
- `src/components/SpaceManageDialog.tsx` two `role="group"` on accent-swatch groups needed `{/* biome-ignore lint/a11y/useSemanticElements */}` JSX comments with rationale (a swatch picker isn't a `<fieldset>`-style form group; `role="group"` + per-button `aria-pressed` is the correct WAI-ARIA pattern for a single-select toolbar of toggle buttons). Same biome-ignore convention as `SearchPanel.tsx:416`.
- 5 `noNonNullAssertion` violations in `SpaceManageDialog.test.tsx` (e.g. `groups[0]!`, `deleteBtns[0]!`) replaced with `as HTMLElement` casts.
- `cargo fmt` cleanup (orchestrator-direct) for the FEAT-3p8 + MAINT-1 subagent test file edits.

**Quality gate:**

- `cd src-tauri && cargo nextest run -p agaric` → **2950/2950 passed** (1 flaky retry on `batch_conflict_resolution_multiple_properties_and_move` — same flake noted in session 496; passes in isolation; documented as a known issue not introduced by this session).
- `npx vitest run` (the 5 most-affected files: SpaceManageDialog, SpaceSwitcher, HistoryView, HistoryFilterBar, App) → **199/199 passed**.
- `prek run --all-files` → all 33 hooks pass after the orchestrator-direct fixes above.
- `cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests` regenerated the `.sqlx/` cache; 8 new entries committed alongside their queries (5 from FEAT-3p6 / `create_space`, 3 from MAINT-1's new `pages_to_migrate` queries).
- `bindings.ts` regenerated via `cargo test -- specta_tests --ignored` (FEAT-3p8 added `spaceId: string | null`, FEAT-3p6 added `createSpace`).
- 2 commits ahead of session-497 baseline:
  - `07f92c2` `feat(spaces): FEAT-3p11 + FEAT-3p8 + FEAT-3p6 + MAINT-1 — digit hotkeys, history scoping, manage dialog, Personal→Work migration`
  - `<this commit>` `docs(session-498)` — REVIEW-LATER + SESSION-LOG updates

**Notes for next session:**

- **Backend defense-in-depth on every "frontend disables, backend permits" pattern.** The FEAT-3p6 reviewer caught a frontend-only delete guard for spaces — the backend would happily soft-delete a space block, leaving every page with `space=<that space>` pointing at a deleted ref. Whenever a UI control gates a destructive op on a precondition queried via IPC, the backend MUST re-check the precondition INSIDE the same `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` tx as the mutation. Frontend probes are UX hints, not correctness guarantees — there is always a TOCTOU window from the probe to the IPC call (concurrent device, sync replay, malicious agent calling MCP-RW). The `delete_block_inner` guard added this session is the canonical pattern; replicate it for any future "delete X only if Y is empty / inactive / drained" workflow.
- **`HistoryFilterBar` prop optionality matters.** The FEAT-3p8 builder didn't account for `HistoryPanel.tsx` (the per-page history consumer) which uses the same `HistoryFilterBar` component. TypeScript caught it via `prek`'s tsc hook. Lesson: when extending a shared component's props, search for ALL consumers (`grep -rn "<HistoryFilterBar" src/`) before deciding whether the new props are required or optional. Per-page-mode consumers don't need cross-page features — make them optional and short-circuit the rendering inline.
- **Sonner mock + `toast.error` assertions.** Component tests that rely on `toast.error` to surface a failure cannot use `screen.getByText(...)` against the toast copy because `src/__tests__/mocks/sonner.ts` makes `toast.error` a `vi.fn()` (no DOM mount). Instead: `import { toast } from 'sonner'` then `expect(vi.mocked(toast.error)).toHaveBeenCalledWith(t('key'))`. The `prek` IPC-error-path-coverage hook detects whether ANY rejection-mock pattern exists in the test file but doesn't check whether the assertion target is correct — verify locally before committing.
- **`prek` runs `cargo fmt --check` per module, not workspace-wide.** Subagents that add Rust tests sometimes leave hand-formatted code that diverges from rustfmt's preference. Run `cargo fmt --all` once after all subagents settle, BEFORE the first `prek run`. This session needed two cycles (initial commit attempt → cargo fmt failure → cargo fmt --all → re-run) — saves a cycle next time.
- **`MIGRATION_THRESHOLD_ULID` deterministic construction.** The `ulid::Ulid::from_parts(timestamp_ms, 0).to_string()` recipe documented in MAINT-1's bootstrap.rs comment is reusable for any future time-gated one-shot. Random portion = `0` for a clean lower bound; the timestamp portion is the only meaningful field. Don't let a future migration use `Ulid::new()` (random portion non-zero) — that would mean different builds produce different thresholds, breaking the time-gate guarantee.
- **Concurrent agent added FEAT-14 mid-session.** A parallel agent landed FEAT-14 (PageBrowser unified namespace tree) + FEAT-14a/b/c during this batch's window. Re-reading REVIEW-LATER.md before the docs commit (per PROMPT.md's concurrent-edit guidance) caught the new entries cleanly — the count went 16 → 12 (net -4: my batch resolved 4, concurrent agent already added 4 before I started counting). Keep the re-read habit.
