- **Flake fix #1:** `materializer::tests::wait_for_pending_block_count_refreshes_returns_after_tasks_already_finished` was using a 1000-iteration `tokio::task::yield_now()` budget on the default single-threaded `#[tokio::test]` runtime, but `sqlx::query_scalar(...).fetch_one` suspends across multiple polls — under CI load 1000 yields wasn't enough. Switched to `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)]` + `tokio::time::sleep(1ms)`-polled wait against a 5s deadline. 10x repro loop passes 10/10.
- **Flake fix #2:** `JournalPage`/`JournalCalendarDropdown` calendar-dropdown-positioning tests overwrote `window.visualViewport` with `{ height, width }` and never restored it. Subsequent Radix Popover/Tooltip mounts in the same worker tripped on `ancestor.addEventListener` inside floating-ui's `autoUpdate` (the polluted mock has no `addEventListener` method), interrupting `userEvent.click` event sequences in unrelated tests — symptom was `Today button navigates to today in weekly mode` asserting `'daily'` but seeing `'agenda'`. Two-pronged fix: (1) global `afterEach` in `src/test-setup.ts` deletes `window.visualViewport`, (2) `try/finally` cleanup in the four offending tests. No production code changed. 4 consecutive full-suite runs all green.
- **4 deferred regression tests added:** M-42 first/last position malformed-payload tests, I-Sync-2 emoji-boundary text-preview tests, L-81 conflict-page-title regression test, I-Search-3 truncation test at FTS_ROW_CAP+1 (10001 rows via INSERT…WITH RECURSIVE bulk helper, runs in <500ms).

**Quality gate:**
- `cargo nextest run` → **2836 tests passed**, 2 skipped (one stable run; the previously-flagged op_log warn-capture test isolation works correctly per R1's investigation — the L-1 tests use scoped `tracing::subscriber::set_default()` not global `init()`).
- `npm test` → **8341 tests / 312 files**, all green (visualViewport leak fix from the earlier polish commit prevents the prior PopperContent flake class).
- `cargo test specta_tests::regenerate_ts_bindings --ignored` → bindings regenerated for L-45's `GcalStatus.enabled` removal; `cargo nextest run specta_tests` confirms `ts_bindings_up_to_date` passes.
- `prek run --all-files` → all 33 hooks green after one clippy fix on `sync_net/websocket.rs:343` (`as u64` → `u64::try_from(...).unwrap_or(u64::MAX)` for the backoff_ms tracing field) and a few `cargo fmt` rewraps.

**Reviewer findings (5 reviewers, all PASS, no blocking issues):**

- *R1 Sync (PASS-7) was earlier; this session's reviewers were R1-Core+Materializer (PASS-5), R2-Cache+Search (PASS-6 plus one trivial outdated test comment), R3-Commands (PASS-7 plus a soft suggestion that M-31's 50ms timing assertion may flake on slow CI runners — acceptable for now), R4-Sync stack (PASS-6), R5-Lifecycle+GCal+MCP (PASS-8).*
- The R1 reviewer specifically investigated the L-1 op_log test isolation pattern and confirmed it uses scoped `tracing::subscriber::set_default()` with a guard (NOT global `init()`), so the warn-capture flake S1 + S4 reported during their build runs is from a different test elsewhere using global `init()`, not from L-1 itself. Acceptable for this session; if the flake reappears, future investigation should look elsewhere.

**Reviewer suggestions deferred (cosmetic only):**
- `fts/tests.rs:1021` outdated comment ("Bare 'OR' with no surrounding terms is quoted as a literal word") doesn't match the new I-Search-2 sanitizer behaviour (it's now dropped as sub-trigram). The test itself still asserts the correct thing (no crash); just the comment is slightly stale.
- `commands/bug_report.rs::collect_metadata_completes_quickly_for_oversized_log_file` 50ms assertion is tight for slow CI runners; consider 100ms ceiling if it flakes.

**Architectural notes:**
- **Edit-tool persistence anomaly observed twice this session.** Several subagent edits (and one of mine — M-41 ARCHITECTURE.md + hash.rs) reported success but did not persist on disk; the orchestrator caught the M-41 revert via a deliberate `git diff HEAD ARCHITECTURE.md` check and re-applied it. S1 + S4 + S5 also reported re-applying their edits after the same anomaly. All final state is verified via `cargo nextest run` + `prek run --all-files` passing. Worth flagging upstream as a tooling issue.
- **5-parallel-subagent + Track-A pattern** continues to scale cleanly for cleanup batches. This was the second 30+ item batch in two sessions; in both cases domain partitioning kept file-overlap minimal and the orchestrator could land Track-A doc edits in parallel with subagent compute.
- **One genuine architectural concern landed:** L-11 (record_failure race) was closed last session with an atomic `INSERT … ON CONFLICT … RETURNING attempts`. This session's M-7 + M-8 fix is similarly motivated — a backpressure drop that was previously invisible. The pattern across both is "every silent drop should increment some metric"; the code now has end-to-end observability for the materializer's drop-classes (retry-exhausted, backpressure-shed, dispatch-malformed all log at warn with structured fields including seq + device_id).
- **AGENTS.md "Architectural Stability" upheld.** No new tables, no new op types, no new stores, no new sync message types. Every change stayed inside existing abstractions. The L-45 binding regen (drop `GcalStatus.enabled`) was an existing-type narrowing, not an expansion.

## Session 489 — Backend cleanup batch: 38 small REVIEW-LATER findings closed across sync / cache / commands / materializer / core (2026-04-26)

**38 REVIEW-LATER items resolved in a single batch** across 5 parallel build subagents + 6 orchestrator-only doc edits, all pipelined with 5 technical reviewers. Backend Code Review section: MEDIUM 96 → 82, LOW 129 → 111, INFO 79 → 73 (CRITICAL 3 / HIGH 17 unchanged). Top-of-file table (FEAT/PERF/PUB) untouched at 13 open items. Pure cleanup pass — every item was already pre-classified `Cost: S`, no new abstractions, no schema migrations, no architectural changes. The largest single REVIEW-LATER cleanup pass to date (typical sessions resolve 3-6 items); the 5-parallel-subagent + pipelined-review pattern from PROMPT.md scaled cleanly because items were domain-partitioned with minimal file overlap. All 5 review subagents returned PASS-on-correctness with 4 minor test-coverage-gap suggestions deferred (code is sound; targeted regression tests would be nice but aren't required for safety).

Batch composition (38 items):

- **Sync stack (S1, 7):** M-42 (`apply_remote_ops` all-or-nothing JSON validation — middle-malformed payload now rejects whole batch), M-45 (dead `shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>` deleted from `SyncDaemon`), M-49 (`Path::exists()` → `tokio::fs::metadata().await.is_err()` — fixes blocking syscall in async fn on tokio runtime), L-87 (`update_last_address` raw `sqlx::query` → `sqlx::query!` macro + `.sqlx/` cache regenerated), L-89 (`update_device_name` error string `peer_ref` → `peer_refs ({peer_id})` consistency with siblings), I-Sync-2 (new `describe_message` helper to avoid `format!("{:?}", binary_msg)` dumping multi-MB byte buffers into log lines), L-60 (`\x00` separator inserted into `build_salt` between sorted IDs — prevents `("AB","CD")` vs `("A","BCD")` collision since `\x00` cannot appear in Crockford ULIDs).

- **Cache + Search predicates (S2, 7):** M-14 (`reindex_block_links` adds `AND is_conflict = 0` — conflict-copy sources no longer leak `[[ULID]]` tokens into backlinks), L-20 (`list_agenda` / `list_agenda_range` / `list_backlinks` defensive `AND b.is_conflict = 0` — closes TOCTOU window between conflict-mark op and cache rebuild), L-81 (`eval_unlinked_references` page-title query filter), M-59 + M-60 (recursive CTE oracles in `tag_query::resolve_expr_cte` and `backlink::resolve_root_pages_cte` get `AND depth < 100` bound — matches AGENTS.md invariant #9; M-60 also picks up `is_conflict = 0` on both recursive walk and final projection), M-61 (`strip_for_fts` async path adds `is_conflict = 0` to tag/page reference lookups), I-Search-3 (`backlink/grouped.rs` inline `LIMIT 10001` → `format!("LIMIT {}", FTS_ROW_CAP + 1)` — bumping the constant alone now keeps the SQL in sync).

- **Commands hygiene (S3, 7):** L-34 (`add_tag_inner` rejects `block_id == tag_id` with `AppError::InvalidOperation`), M-38 (`compact_op_log_cmd_inner` rejects `retention_days < MIN_RETENTION_DAYS = 7` — protects undo/redo history from accidental Settings-tab bug), M-36 (`disconnect_gcal_inner` with `delete_calendar=true` no longer aborts on transient keyring failure — local cleanup proceeds, keyring failure logged at warn), L-33 (5 sites in `commands/properties.rs` swap `let _ = app.emit(...)` for the logged-Err pattern — silent emit failures previously meant a stale FE store with no diagnostic), L-44 (`get_gcal_status_inner` on `KeyringBackendError::PlatformUnavailable` returns `connected=false` rather than propagating — Settings tab no longer goes blank when keyring is transient), L-49 (`set_gcal_window_days_inner` replaces dead `try_from(...).unwrap_or_else(...)` fallback with `.expect("clamped to [MIN_WINDOW_DAYS, MAX_WINDOW_DAYS] always fits i32")` — proves the invariant rather than masking it), L-50 (`#[cfg(not(tarpaulin_include))]` on `update_peer_name` + `set_peer_address` Tauri wrappers — coverage-report consistency with siblings).

- **Materializer + MCP (S4, 7):** M-11 (`dispatch_background_or_warn` warn now includes `seq` + `device_id` — matches dedup-side parse pattern, makes triage of malformed payloads possible), M-15 (`rebuild_page_ids` moved to FIRST in both `rebuild_all_caches` sequence and `snapshot/restore.rs::rebuild_tasks` array — fixes silent template-page filter corruption after snapshot restore), L-10 (`Barrier(notify)` arm inlined in `process_single_foreground_task` — drops unnecessary `tokio::spawn` + Arc clones; mirrors the bg-side direct-handle pattern), L-11 (`record_failure` SELECT-then-INSERT → single atomic `INSERT … ON CONFLICT(block_id, task_type) DO UPDATE SET attempts = materializer_retry_queue.attempts + 1 … RETURNING attempts` — closes a real concurrency race where two simultaneous failure inserts could both observe `prior=None`, both INSERT with `attempts=1`, and lose one increment), I-Materializer-2 (`pending_count` `pub` → `pub(crate)` for visibility hygiene with sibling helpers), L-101 (`cascade_soft_delete` adds `tracing::debug!` at entry + `tracing::info!(seed_block_id, descendants_marked, deleted_at)` after commit — diagnoses "I lost a tree" reports), I-MCP-8 (3 multi-byte UTF-8 boundary tests for `ActivityResult::Err` 200-char clip; existing `chars().take(200).collect()` already byte-safe — tests pin the invariant against future regressions to byte-based truncation).

- **Core invariants (S5, 4):** L-3 (`BlockId::from_trusted` `to_uppercase()` → `to_ascii_uppercase()` to match `Deserialize` byte-for-byte; new proptest verifies parity for arbitrary strings), L-4 (`compute_op_hash` 4× `debug_assert!` → `assert!` for null-byte field separators — these are the wire-format hash-chain contract, not debug-only invariants; tests now run unconditionally including under `cargo nextest run --release`), L-6 (`validate_set_property` rejects empty/whitespace-only `value_text` / `value_date` / `value_ref` after `.trim()` — closes a class of "set property to empty, then everything that touches it breaks downstream" bugs that originated from MCP/import paths), L-41 (`home_dir_string()` `std::env::var("HOME")` → `dirs::home_dir()` — closes a Windows-only privacy leak in bug-report exports where the user's `C:\Users\<name>\…` paths were not redacted because Windows doesn't export `$HOME`; new explicit `dirs = "6.0.0"` dep, no transitive-only assumption).

- **Orchestrator docs (Track A, 6):** M-9 (`ARCHITECTURE.md §5` backoff 50/100ms → 150/300ms; documents adaptive FTS-optimize threshold formula `max(500, block_count / 10_000)`; cross-link comment added next to `INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS` in `materializer/consumer.rs`), M-13 (`QueueMetrics::fg_high_water` / `bg_high_water` and `StatusInfo::fg_high_water` / `bg_high_water` doc-comments now spell out the windowed-reset semantics — peak within current 5-minute snapshot window, not all-time peak), M-65 (`ARCHITECTURE.md` line 1168 draft recovery `created_at >= draft.updated_at` → `created_at > draft.updated_at`, plus annotation calling out the deliberate strict-comparator + Z-suffix lex-monotonic invariant), L-88 (`peer_refs::upsert_peer_ref_with_cert` doc-comment "INSERT OR REPLACE" → "INSERT … ON CONFLICT(peer_id) DO UPDATE" + explicit note that `last_hash` / `synced_at` / `reset_count` are preserved across re-pairing), I-Search-1 (`fts/search.rs` doc rewritten — claims `unicode61` tokenizer in CJK section, but migration `0006_fts5_trigram.sql` switched to trigram precisely to fix CJK substring search), I-Cache-1 (`pagination/mod.rs` `Cursor` doc-comment now enumerates the `list_page_history` composite overload — `deleted_at` stashes `created_at`, `id` stashes `device_id`, `seq` keeps its meaning — so future cursor-bearing queries reuse the slot pattern rather than expanding the struct).

**Quality gate:**
- `cargo nextest run` → **2775 tests passed**, 2 skipped, 1 transient flake (`materializer::tests::wait_for_pending_block_count_refreshes_returns_after_tasks_already_finished`, retried successfully — known concurrency-flaky test).
- `npm test` → 8341 tests / 312 files. Two `JournalPage.test.tsx` Today-button tests show as failed in the full-suite run but pass in isolation; root cause is a `<PopperContent>` Radix UI error from prior-test state pollution (pre-existing flake, not introduced this session — confirmed by isolated re-run).
- `cargo test specta_tests::regenerate_ts_bindings --ignored` → bindings regenerated; `cargo nextest run specta_tests` confirms `ts_bindings_up_to_date` now passes.
- `prek run --all-files` → all 33 hooks green after one biome auto-fix on `src/components/__tests__/BugReportDialog.test.tsx` and a few `cargo fmt` rewraps in `sync_protocol/tests.rs`, `sync_daemon/tests.rs`, `commands/bug_report.rs`, `commands/gcal.rs`, `pagination/tests.rs`, `commands/tests/compaction_cmd_tests.rs`.

**Reviewer findings (5 reviewers, all PASS, 4 deferred test-gaps):**

- *R1 Sync (PASS-7):* No actionable items. Two LOW test-coverage suggestions deferred — M-42 explicit first/last malformed-payload position tests and I-Sync-2 emoji-at-text-preview-boundary test. Implementation correctness verified through existing tests + reasoning about `chars().take(N)` UTF-8 safety.
- *R2 Cache + Search (PASS-7):* All code PASS. Two NEEDS-FIX flagged but both are test-coverage gaps deferred — L-81 explicit conflict-page-title regression test and I-Search-3 truncation test at `FTS_ROW_CAP + 1`. The runtime fix is correct; tests would harden against future regressions.
- *R3 Commands hygiene (PASS-7):* No actionable items. Verified the keyring-transient detection pattern (`AppError::Validation(msg) if msg.starts_with("keyring.unavailable")`) matches the exact prefix emitted by `KeyringTokenStore::map_platform_unavailable` — neither too narrow nor too broad.
- *R4 Materializer + MCP (PASS-7):* No actionable items. Note on I-Materializer-2: `pending_count` has no production caller and is gated by `#[cfg_attr(not(test), allow(dead_code))]` — acceptable as a placeholder for future `StatusInfo` wiring (MAINT-24-style follow-up); orchestrator chose not to either delete or wire it in this batch.
- *R5 Core invariants (PASS-4):* No actionable items. `dirs::home_dir()` confirmed not previously a transitive dep; explicit `cargo add dirs --no-default-features` was the right call.

**Architectural notes:**
- **The biggest correctness win was L-11.** `record_failure` previously had a real two-writer race (both observers see `prior=None`, both INSERT with `attempts=1`, the second's `ON CONFLICT` overwrites with `excluded.attempts=1`, losing one increment). The race was rare in practice because the bg consumer is largely single-threaded, but the off-by-one shifted the backoff schedule by one step every time it fired. The new SQL-side atomic increment with `RETURNING attempts` closes it; pinned by a new concurrent test that the orchestrator verified would fail under the prior code path.
- **M-49 was a long-overdue async-correctness fix.** Any `std::fs` call inside an `async fn` on a tokio runtime is a latent runtime-stall risk (`Path::exists()` syscalls `stat(2)` synchronously); this was one of the more visible offenders in the sync stack and on Android with cold FS caches it was extending the sync handshake noticeably.
- **The recursive-CTE oracle bounds in M-59 and M-60 close a parity-test risk.** Oracle-side CTEs were silently allowing depth-101+ chains and conflict-source ancestors that the production code already guarded against. Oracle parity tests would have passed for the wrong reason on corrupted inputs (oracle and prod both return the same wrong answer). The bounds make the invariant explicit on both sides.
- **AGENTS.md "Architectural Stability" upheld.** No new tables, no new op types, no new stores, no new materializer queues, no new sync message types. Every change stayed inside existing abstractions. The one new dependency (`dirs = "6.0.0"`) replaces a manual `std::env::var("HOME")` lookup that was platform-broken on Windows.
- **The 5-parallel-subagent + pipelined-review pattern is becoming the default for "many small items in disjoint domains" sessions.** Domain partitioning was clean enough that no merge conflicts occurred between subagents working on the main tree. Track A docs ran in the orchestrator and dovetailed with subagent file-locks (deferring `peer_refs.rs` and `materializer/consumer.rs` doc edits until the corresponding subagent finished).

## Session 488 — Spaces Phase 3 + publish prep: per-space tabs/recent-pages + updater URL pinned + CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md pointer fix (FEAT-3p3 / PUB-5 partial / PUB-7) (2026-04-26)

**2 REVIEW-LATER items fully resolved + 1 partially advanced.** Open items: 13 → 11. Mixed batch: a frontend-only Zustand refactor that finally lands per-space tabs + recent pages (FEAT-3p3 — third overall attempt, succeeded with a hybrid keep-flat-mirror approach the prior subagents didn't consider), the agent-actionable mechanical bits of PUB-5 (updater URL pinned to `jfolcini/agaric`; keypair + secrets remain user-only), and PUB-7 closed by recognising that SECURITY.md was already comprehensive and just updating the Code of Conduct's stale pointer to it. Tech reviewer flagged 1 HIGH (cross-store rehydration race in the new module-load subscriptions) + 1 MEDIUM (`BUG_TRACKER` const in `src/lib/config.ts` still pointed at the old `agaric-app/...` URL — bug-report dialog would 404) + 5 LOW/NIT polish notes; both BLOCKER-tier findings fixed in-session.

Batch composition (3 items):

- **FEAT-3p3** — Spaces Phase 3 (third attempt; subagents in sessions 485 and 486 both stalled). The successful approach was a **hybrid** rather than the strict "tabs → tabsBySpace replacement" the spec called for:
  - Added `tabsBySpace: Record<string, Tab[]>` and `activeTabIndexBySpace: Record<string, number>` as the per-space partitioned source of truth.
  - **Kept** the flat `tabs` / `activeTabIndex` fields as the active-space mirror — this avoided rewriting hundreds of `setState({ tabs: [...] })` test-fixture lines across `TabBar.test.tsx`, `App.test.tsx`, `navigation.test.ts`, etc.
  - Every action that mutates tabs writes BOTH (via a `spliceTabs` helper) keyed by `useSpaceStore.getState().currentSpaceId ?? '__legacy__'`.
  - A `useSpaceStore.subscribe` registered at module load handles space switches: flush flat → outgoing slice, pull incoming slice → flat, plus reset `selectedBlockId`.
  - New selectors `selectTabsForSpace` / `selectActiveTabIndexForSpace` + the equivalent `selectRecentPagesForSpace` for `useRecentPagesStore`.
  - Persistence migration: bumped both stores from version `0 → 1`. The migrate body moves legacy flat fields into `*BySpace.__legacy__` while preserving the flat fields themselves.
  - 11 new tests (+7 in `navigation.test.ts`, +4 in `recent-pages.test.ts`) cover persistence round-trip for the new maps, the v0→v1 migration, stale-`currentSpaceId` rehydrate fallback, per-space dedup invariants, and cross-space tab-open isolation.
  - Files touched: `src/stores/{navigation.ts,recent-pages.ts}`, `src/components/{TabBar.tsx,RecentPagesStrip.tsx}`, `src/App.tsx` (TAB_SHORTCUTS callbacks read through the new selectors), and the corresponding tests.

- **PUB-5 partial** — orchestrator updated `src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:30` so the updater endpoint now points at `https://github.com/jfolcini/agaric/releases/latest/download/latest.json` (the publish target was decided in a prior session). The `pubkey` in `tauri.conf.json` is still empty and the `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY*` env vars in `release.yml:93-95` remain commented out — the agent intentionally did not uncomment them because uncommenting before the secrets exist would cause tauri-action to attempt signing with empty inputs. REVIEW-LATER PUB-5 entry retitled to reflect the now user-only remaining work.

- **PUB-7** — SECURITY.md was already comprehensive (it was added in a prior session that the SESSION-LOG didn't cover). Just updated `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md:39` so the stale "Contact details will be published in `SECURITY.md` before the first public release" placeholder now resolves to a concrete pointer at `[SECURITY.md § How to report]`. PUB-7 closed.

**Quality gate:** `npx tsc -b` clean. `npm test` → 312 files / 8341 tests passed (11 new in this batch). `cargo nextest run` → 2747 tests passed (2 skipped). `prek run --all-files` → all 31 hooks green.

**Reviewer findings (2 actionable, both fixed in-session):**

- *Tech reviewer (HIGH):* `src/stores/navigation.ts:500` and `src/stores/recent-pages.ts:135` initialised `prevSpaceKey` from `useSpaceStore.getState().currentSpaceId` at module-load time. Zustand's persist middleware rehydrates asynchronously; if `useSpaceStore` rehydrates AFTER the navigation/recent-pages stores, the first subscriber fire sees a stale → rehydrated change and incorrectly flushes the just-rehydrated flat tabs into the wrong slice (the `__legacy__` slot rather than the active space). For returning users with v0→v1-migrated data this would have meant their tabs disappeared from the active-space view. **Fix:** lazy-initialise `prevSpaceKey` to `undefined`; on the first subscriber fire, treat it as an initialisation pass — if `tabsBySpace[newKey]` is missing, seed it from the rehydrated flat tabs (so a user migrated from v0 keeps their tabs accessible from the active space) and set `prevSpaceKey` without flushing. Subsequent fires take the real flush+pull path.
- *Tech reviewer (MEDIUM):* `src/lib/config.ts:13-17` and `src/lib/__tests__/bug-report.test.ts:20-26` still hardcoded the old `agaric-app/org-mode-for-the-rest-of-us` owner/repo. The bug-report dialog (FEAT-5) would have built a 404 GitHub-issues URL because the `BUG_TRACKER` constant has to stay in sync with the updater endpoint changed in PUB-5. **Fix:** updated both files to `{ owner: 'jfolcini', repo: 'agaric' }` and rewrote the test assertion's expected URL prefix.

**Reviewer findings deferred (low-impact polish):**

- LOW (`prevSpaceKey` not reset between test runs) — fragile but currently works because tests reset stores in `beforeEach` and the lazy-init means stale `prevSpaceKey` from a prior test still triggers the right code path (post-init real flush+pull).
- LOW (`nextTabId` derivation silently ignores NaN tab IDs) — defensive logging would be nice but not a real bug; tab IDs are integer-stringified, NaN can only appear if persisted state was hand-edited.
- LOW (selector fallback `tabsBySpace[spaceId] ?? state.tabs` returns previous space's tabs for a never-visited space, briefly, before the subscriber pulls) — by design; the alternative (return empty) would cause a flash of empty UI on every space switch.
- MEDIUM (migration doesn't validate tab element shape beyond `Array.isArray`) — defensive validation would catch hand-edited corruption but doesn't fix any current path.
- NIT (migration `as NavigationState` cast is loose) — same; would benefit from a runtime schema validator if one is ever introduced.
- NIT (Code of Conduct `[SECURITY.md § How to report](SECURITY.md#how-to-report)` anchor link assumes GitHub's auto-generated heading anchor) — works on github.com renders.

**Architectural notes:**
- The hybrid keep-flat-mirror approach is a pragmatic alternative to a strict "lift state into a per-space map" refactor. It's defensible because:
  - The flat field is the canonical "what the user sees right now" projection.
  - Per-space slices are the source of truth for cross-space invariants (verified by the cross-space invariant test).
  - The space-switch subscriber is the single point that keeps the two in sync.
  - Existing tests + consumers don't need to thread the space id through every read.
- The lazy-init fix to the subscriber registration is a generally useful pattern for module-level Zustand subscriptions that depend on another store's persisted state. Worth recording for future cross-store coordination.
- AGENTS.md "Architectural Stability" — this stays inside existing abstractions: no new tables, no new IPC commands, no new stores, no schema migration. The persistence-version bump is internal to the existing Zustand persist middleware contract.

## Session 487 — Tauri plugin trio: deep-link + global-shortcut quick-capture + autostart (FEAT-10 / FEAT-12 / FEAT-13) (2026-04-25)

**3 REVIEW-LATER items fully resolved.** Open items: 16 → 13. Three Tauri plugin adoptions in parallel — the `tauri-plugin-*` and `@tauri-apps/plugin-*` versions follow AGENTS.md "Coupled Dependency Updates" by adding the new plugins at the existing major (`2`) without bumping any pinned slice. Three build subagents in parallel coordinated their writes to shared files (`Cargo.toml`, `package.json`, `lib.rs`, `capabilities/default.json`, `tauri.conf.json`, `SettingsView.tsx`) by appending to existing blocks rather than inserting; no merge conflicts. Tech reviewer flagged 2 BLOCKERs (chord re-bind via storage event was a no-op stub) + 1 MEDIUM (frontend ULID case normalisation gap vs Rust router) + 1 HIGH from UX (autostart toggle silently hidden) and 11 LOW/MEDIUM UX polish items. The 2 BLOCKERs and the MEDIUM tech finding were fixed in-session; the rest deferred as future polish.

Batch composition (3 items, all closed):

- **FEAT-10** — `tauri-plugin-deep-link` adoption. Cross-platform plugin (no `#[cfg(desktop)]` gate) — the whole point on Android is the Custom-Tabs OAuth callback that FEAT-5g needs.
  - **Backend:** `tauri-plugin-deep-link = "2"` and `url = "2"` (declared explicitly so `cargo machete` sees the dependency rather than relying on transitive resolution). New `src-tauri/src/deeplink/mod.rs` module exposes `parse_deep_link(url: &str) -> Result<DeepLinkRoute, ParseError>` and `register_deeplink_handlers(app: &AppHandle)` which subscribes to the plugin's `deep-link://new-url` event and re-emits typed events: `agaric://block/<ULID>` → `deeplink:navigate-to-block`, `agaric://page/<ULID>` → `deeplink:navigate-to-page`, `agaric://settings/<tab>` → `deeplink:open-settings`. ULID validation goes through the canonical `BlockId::from_string` parser (uppercase Crockford normalisation per AGENTS.md). Single-instance handler updated to call `app.deep_link().handle_cli_arguments(args.into_iter())` so Linux/Windows second-instance launches forward URLs to the primary process. 22 new Rust tests cover parse paths, ULID rejections, scheme typos, multi-slash paths, query strings, payload shapes.
  - **Config:** `tauri.conf.json` gets a `plugins.deep-link` block with `desktop.schemes: ["agaric"]` and a placeholder `mobile: [{ host: "agaric.app", pathPrefix: ["/o/"] }]` (host needs to be set to the published HTTPS App-Link host once the project goes public). `capabilities/default.json` gets `"deep-link:default"`.
  - **Frontend:** `useDeepLinkRouter` hook (`src/hooks/useDeepLinkRouter.ts`) listens for the three typed events and dispatches via `useNavigationStore` and the `agaric-settings-active-tab` localStorage key. Mounted once at the `App.tsx` root. On mount also calls `getCurrentDeepLink()` and replays the launch URL through a frontend-side `dispatchLaunchUrl` parser — Linux/Windows fire the plugin event before the React listener is attached, so without this backfill the very first deep-link of a session would be silently dropped. The frontend backfill mirrors the Rust router's parsing rules including ULID uppercase normalisation. 35 new tests in `useDeepLinkRouter.test.ts`.

- **FEAT-12** — `tauri-plugin-global-shortcut` adoption + quick-capture flow. Desktop-only via `#[cfg(desktop)]`.
  - **Backend:** `tauri-plugin-global-shortcut = "2"`. New `quick_capture_block(content: String)` Tauri command in `src-tauri/src/commands/journal.rs` resolves today's journal page (creating it via the existing `today_journal_inner` helper if absent) and appends a content block. Reuses the `create_block` validation chain (size limits, content shape). Registered in the `collect_commands![]` macro and the `specta_tests::specta_builder` list; `src/lib/bindings.ts` regenerated via `cargo test specta_tests::regenerate_ts_bindings --ignored`. 4 new integration tests in `command_integration_tests/page_integration.rs` (happy path, idempotent reuse of today's page, two captures append two blocks, oversize-content `Validation` error).
  - **Frontend wrappers:** `src/lib/tauri.ts` gets `quickCaptureBlock`, `registerGlobalShortcut`, `unregisterGlobalShortcut`, `isGlobalShortcutRegistered` plus a private `isMobilePlatform()` UA-based check (the existing `useIsMobile` hook is React-only and can't be called from a wrapper module). Mobile is a no-op for the global-shortcut wrappers; the IPC commands themselves still work.
  - **Quick-capture dialog:** `src/components/QuickCaptureDialog.tsx` uses the `Dialog` primitive with a textarea + Capture / Cancel buttons. Cmd/Ctrl+Enter submits. Whitespace-only content disables the Capture button. Toast on success ("Captured to today's journal"), `logger.error` + toast on failure with the dialog staying open. `axe(container)` clean.
  - **Settings UI:** new `QuickCaptureRow` in the General tab. Shows the current chord; Edit reveals an inline input + Save/Cancel. Save probes the new chord with a temporary `register/unregister` pair (so the user gets immediate failure feedback), then writes to localStorage and dispatches a synthetic `storage` event. Default chord `Ctrl+Alt+N` (Linux/Win) / `Cmd+Alt+N` (macOS) via the existing `modKey()` helper in `src/lib/platform.ts`. Hidden on mobile via `useIsMobile`.
  - **App.tsx integration:** the chord is now lifted into React state (`quickCaptureChord`) with a lazy initializer reading from localStorage. Two effects: a storage-event listener that updates the chord state when SettingsView writes a new chord, and a registration effect that depends on `[quickCaptureChord]` so the chord can be re-bound with the live `setQuickCaptureOpen` handler whenever it changes. Earlier draft of this code had an empty dep array + a no-op storage handler — tech reviewer flagged it as a BLOCKER (the new chord would never re-bind with the live handler), fixed in-session. Window-focus path uses `getCurrentWindow().unminimize()` → `show()` → `setFocus()` in that order. 27 new frontend tests across `tauri.test.ts`, `QuickCaptureDialog.test.tsx`, and `SettingsView.test.tsx`.
  - **9 new i18n keys** (`settings.quickCapture.label/description/editButton/saveFailed`, `quickCapture.dialogTitle/placeholder/saveButton/successToast/failureToast`).

- **FEAT-13** — `tauri-plugin-autostart` adoption. Desktop-only.
  - **Backend:** `tauri-plugin-autostart = "2"` registered with `MacosLauncher::LaunchAgent` and `Some(vec!["--silent"])`. `capabilities/default.json` gets `"autostart:default"`.
  - **Frontend wrappers:** `isAutostartEnabled`, `enableAutostart`, `disableAutostart` in `src/lib/tauri.ts` use the dynamic-import pattern matching `clipboard.ts` / `relaunch-app.ts` (the plugin already wraps `invoke` internally; another `invoke('plugin:autostart|enable')` layer would duplicate that).
  - **Settings UI:** new `AutostartRow` in the General tab below `<DeadlineWarningSection />`. Three-state pattern (`null` = loading or plugin unavailable → row hidden, `true` / `false` = visible toggle). Optimistic update on click + revert-on-failure with a `toast.error` and `logger.error` call. Desktop-only via the rejection-of-`isAutostartEnabled()` signal — collapses mobile, browser dev, and IPC denial into the same "hide row" branch (no separate desktop check needed).
  - **3 new i18n keys** (`settings.autostart.label/description/toggleFailed`).
  - 7 new tests in `tauri.test.ts` + 7 in `SettingsView.test.tsx` (covering happy path, hidden-on-mobile, optimistic update, revert-on-failure with `mockRejectedValueOnce` per MAINT-99's IPC-error-path rule).

**Quality gate:** `npx tsc -b` clean. `npm test` → 312 files / 8330 tests passed (76 new in this batch). `cargo nextest run` → 2747 tests passed (2 skipped; 26 new in this batch). `prek run --all-files` → all 31 hooks green (the new IPC commands are tracked: `quick_capture_block` added to the `KNOWN_UNMOCKED` allowlist in `scripts/check-tauri-mock-parity.mjs` because the OS-level shortcut + dialog are exercised at the per-component vitest level, not through Playwright).

**Reviewer findings (4 actionable, all addressed):**

- *Tech reviewer (BLOCKER):* `App.tsx:841` global-shortcut `useEffect` had empty dep array `[]` but the comment claimed re-bind happens via the cleanup. The cleanup only runs on unmount, so the new chord set in SettingsView would never get re-registered with the live handler. **Fix:** lifted the chord into React state via a lazy `useState(loadQuickCaptureShortcut)`; added a separate effect that listens for the `storage` event and updates state; the registration effect now depends on `[quickCaptureChord]` so it cleans up + re-registers on every chord change. App.tsx tests still pass (100/100).
- *Tech reviewer (BLOCKER):* `SettingsView.tsx:382` — if the new chord registration failed, the restore path re-registered the previous chord with a no-op handler, leaving the user without a working shortcut. **Fix:** restructured Save flow so SettingsView only PROBES the new chord (register + immediately unregister) before writing localStorage. The live binding stays owned by App.tsx throughout. On probe failure we toast and leave localStorage untouched; the previous chord remains bound by App.tsx without any restore-the-no-op-handler step. SettingsView tests updated to match.
- *Tech reviewer (MEDIUM):* `useDeepLinkRouter.ts:224` `dispatchLaunchUrl` did not normalise lowercase ULIDs to uppercase like the Rust router does. **Fix:** uppercase the identifier before passing to `handleNavigatePayload` for `block` and `page` hosts; settings tab names are not ULIDs and pass through unchanged. 3 new tests cover lowercase ULIDs + the settings-tab case.
- *Tauri-mock parity hook:* the new `quick_capture_block` IPC was missing a mock handler. **Fix:** added to `KNOWN_UNMOCKED` with a documented rationale (no Playwright surface yet; per-component vitest mocks suffice).

**Reviewer findings deferred (all UX polish — no functional impact):**
- HIGH (autostart toggle hidden silently) — kept the silent-hide behaviour. Showing a disabled "Not supported" row would clutter the General tab on every desktop user; the silent path matches how the rest of the app handles platform-specific features.
- MEDIUM (chord input is text-only, not key-recording) — true; recording-mode UI would need a focus-trapped key listener with cancel / confirm phases. Worth a separate UX item.
- MEDIUM (no pre-save chord conflict detection) — the probe-on-Save catches conflicts with toast feedback. Real-time debounced detection while the user types would need throttling + a separate visual indicator.
- MEDIUM (Cmd/Ctrl+Enter on Capture not in tooltip / aria-label) — minor discoverability; reasonable follow-up.
- MEDIUM (whitespace-only blocks button silently) — could add an inline helper text.
- MEDIUM (success toast lacks Undo) — would require capturing the just-created block ID and a `delete_block` IPC call. Worth a separate UX item.
- MEDIUM (no confirmation of block location after capture) — could auto-navigate to today's journal or add a "View in journal" toast action.
- LOW (invalid deep-links silently logged with no user toast) — defensible: user opened a malformed URL elsewhere; a toast for invalid OS-level URLs may be more annoying than helpful.
- LOW (unknown settings tab falls back silently to General) — same rationale.
- NIT (i18n tone consistency) — minor copy polish.
- NIT (General tab layout density) — three rows in General is fine; revisit if more get added.

**Architectural notes:**
- The chord state lifting is a clean pattern for OS-level resources whose identity can change at runtime: own the resource lifetime via state-driven effects, and use storage events only as a state-update signal. The earlier draft tried to coordinate via storage events alone — that doesn't work because React effects don't react to storage events without a state shim.
- The probe-then-unregister pattern in SettingsView is the right boundary: SettingsView's job is "validate the chord is available"; App.tsx's job is "own the live binding". Don't have SettingsView claim the chord with a no-op handler that App.tsx then has to forcibly re-claim.
- AGENTS.md "Architectural Stability" — adding plugins at an existing major (`2`) is fine; the rule against bumping a coupled stack slice in isolation does not apply to new plugin additions, only to version bumps.

## Session 486 — Internal-quality bundle: BlockPropertyEditor portal refactor + the two harder prek test-rule hooks (MAINT-103 / MAINT-99 residue; FEAT-3p3 stalled, deferred) (2026-04-25)

**2 REVIEW-LATER items fully resolved.** Open items: 18 → 16. Pure internal-quality bundle — no user-facing behavior change. The MAINT-103 portal refactor was the third attempt (two prior subagents stalled in sessions 483 and 484); this attempt landed cleanly with explicit scope guards and the 0×0-anchor strategy. The two harder MAINT-99 hooks (IPC error-path coverage + snapshot redaction) finish the test-rule enforcement work started in session 485, surfacing 2 real violations that were fixed in-session. FEAT-3p3 (per-space tabs + recent pages refactor) was the third planned item; its subagent stalled with no file changes after 10+ minutes and the orchestrator killed it; the item is deferred to a future session given the cross-store coupling complexity.

Batch composition (3 items planned; 2 fully closed + 1 deferred):

- **MAINT-103** — `BlockPropertyEditor` inline editor portal refactor. Third attempt; two prior subagents stalled. Strategy: aggressive scope guards in the prompt + a 0×0 inline `<span>` anchor placeholder so the component can avoid plumbing trigger refs through `SortableBlock`. Implementation:
  - `src/components/BlockPropertyEditor.tsx`: popup rendering converted from inline `absolute z-50` div to a portal via `createPortal(popup, document.body)`. Both popups (value editor + key rename) carry `data-editor-portal=""` so the existing `EDITOR_PORTAL_SELECTORS` entry in `useEditorBlur.ts` continues to match. Key popup retains `.property-key-editor` class for back-compat with the `useEditorBlur.ts` selector list.
  - Imperative `@floating-ui/dom` (`computePosition` + `autoUpdate` + `flip` + `offset` + `shift`) — mirrors the established `suggestion-renderer.ts`, `BlockContextMenu.tsx`, `LinkPreviewTooltip.tsx` patterns. `@floating-ui/react` is not in `package.json`, so the imperative API is the right call.
  - Two `useEffect` hooks per popup: a position effect calling `autoUpdate(anchor, popup, update)` with `placement: 'bottom-start'` + 4px offset + flip + shift middleware, and a rAF-deferred `pointerdown` capture-phase outside-click effect (mirrors `suggestion-renderer.ts` so the click that opens the popup doesn't immediately close it).
  - Popups start at `(-9999px, -9999px)` until `computePosition` resolves (avoids the (0, 0) flash) — same as `suggestion-renderer.ts`.
  - Lifecycle logging via `logger.warn('BlockPropertyEditor', ...)`: `'value popup mounted without refs'`, `'anchor unmounted while value popup open'`, `'value popup computePosition failed'` (the `.catch`), and the symmetric three for the key popup.
  - Tests: `BlockPropertyEditor.test.tsx` 19 → 36 (+17). Mocks `@floating-ui/dom` with the same contract used by `suggestion-renderer.test.ts` and `LinkPreviewTooltip.test.tsx` (`computePosition` returns `{ x, y }`; `autoUpdate` invokes `update()` once + re-invokes on `resize`). New tests cover: portal location (in `document.body`, not in container), `data-editor-portal` + `.property-key-editor` attributes, `computePosition` + `autoUpdate` called on mount, resize re-triggers `computePosition`, portal cleanup on close, `logger.warn` on rejection, `logger.warn` on anchor unmount lifecycle (`Object.defineProperty` trick to flip `isConnected` without breaking React's commit phase), outside-click dismissal vs in-popup click, input auto-focus, and `axe(portal)` a11y audit. `SortableBlock.test.tsx` got 2 cosmetic line edits (`container.querySelector` → `document.querySelector` for the now-portaled popup).
  - TDZ regression caught locally during test development: `let frameId: number | null = requestAnimationFrame(() => { frameId = null; … })` blew up in tests where rAF runs synchronously. Split into `let frameId: number | null = null; frameId = requestAnimationFrame(...)`.

- **MAINT-99 (residue)** — The two harder hooks completing the test-rule enforcement coverage. Both follow the session-485 pattern (local repo, system language, `pass_filenames = false`, scoped `files` regex).
  - **`scripts/check-ipc-error-path.mjs`** (208 lines) enforces the `AGENTS.md:198` rule that every component calling Tauri `invoke` has at least one error-path test. Scope NARROWED to top-level `src/components/*.tsx` only — subdir components (`agent-access/`, `journal/`, `block-tree/`, `ui/`) frequently route their tests through parent component test files (e.g., `agent-access/ActivityFeed.tsx` is exercised by `AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx`, not by a sibling), and a simple `<basename>.test.tsx` lookup would false-flag these. Rejection patterns scanned: `mockRejectedValueOnce`, `mockRejectedValue(`, `Promise.reject`, `throw new Error`. Surfaced 2 real violations on first activation:
    - `LinkEditPopover.test.tsx`: added a test mocking `fetchLinkMetadata` rejection, asserting `logger.warn` fires and apply still completes (+43 lines).
    - `WelcomeModal.test.tsx`: added a test mocking `createBlock` rejection, asserting `toast.error` fires, the modal stays open, and the onboarding flag is NOT set (+28 lines).
    - FOLLOW-UP documented in script header: broaden the hook to subdir components once those grow their own `__tests__/` siblings.
  - **`scripts/check-snapshot-redaction.mjs`** (296 lines) enforces the `src-tauri/tests/AGENTS.md:284-313` rule that snapshot tests redact ULIDs, blake3 hashes, ISO-8601 timestamps, and base64 cursors. Scans all `.snap` files under `src/` and `src-tauri/`. Auto-derives a fixture allowlist by scanning `src-tauri/src/**/*.rs` for verbatim source-string occurrences (per the documented "deterministic data, no redaction needed" carve-out at `src-tauri/tests/AGENTS.md:319`). Inline `# MAINT-99-allow-<kind>: <reason>` markers escape-hatch the rare exceptions. 0 violations on first activation. Synthetic-violation testing confirmed all 4 categories (ULID, HASH, TIMESTAMP, CURSOR) trip correctly, and the marker correctly suppresses a known fixture.
  - `prek.toml` got 2 new `[[repos.hooks]]` blocks. Per-hook timings (well under 2s budget): hook 1 = 34ms, hook 2 = 62ms.

- **FEAT-3p3 (deferred)** — Spaces Phase 3: per-space tabs + per-space recent pages. The build subagent stalled with no file changes after 10+ minutes; the orchestrator killed it. The cross-store coupling between `useSpaceStore.currentSpaceId` and `useNavigationStore.tabs` / `useRecentPagesStore.recentPages` plus the persistence schema migration on rehydrate plus the consumer-swap surface (`TabBar.tsx`, `RecentPagesStrip.tsx`, the active-tab dropdown switcher, `keyboard-config.ts`) added up to a scope the subagent couldn't converge on. REVIEW-LATER FEAT-3p3 entry kept verbatim for a future session that can dedicate the full block. The orchestrator declined to take over manually given the persistence-migration risk and the size of the consumer-swap surface.

**Quality gate:** `npx tsc -b` clean. `npm test` → 310 files / 8251 tests passed (52 new in this session). `cargo nextest run` → 2721 tests passed (2 skipped). `prek run --all-files` → all 31 hooks green (the existing 26 + the 3 from session 485 + the 2 new this session).

**Reviewer findings:** Tech reviewer ran a thorough analysis of the portal refactor (focused on stale closures, ref attachment timing, autoUpdate cleanup, outside-click capture-phase semantics, logger contract) and the new prek hooks (rejection-pattern coverage, fixture-allowlist correctness). Final verdict: **no technical issues found**. UX review skipped — the changes are internal-quality only (portal refactor visible behavior unchanged; prek hooks have no UI surface).

**Architectural notes:**
- The MAINT-103 0×0 anchor strategy is a clean way to introduce floating UI to components that don't already have a trigger ref plumbed through. Worth recording as a pattern for future portal refactors of other components in similar shape.
- The MAINT-99 fixture-allowlist auto-derivation (regenerated each `prek` run) is more robust than a hand-maintained list — it can't go stale, and Rust source strings have ~130-bit ULID entropy that makes accidental collisions vanishingly improbable.
- AGENTS.md "Architectural Stability" — this session stays firmly inside existing abstractions: `createPortal` + `@floating-ui/dom` was already in use elsewhere; `prek` hooks were already a documented pattern; no new schema, IPC, or store changes.

## Session 485 — Internal-quality + UX bundle: e2e console-error watcher + component decomposition + 3 new prek hooks + breadcrumb primitive + discoverability sweep (TEST-4 / MAINT-96 / MAINT-99 partial / UX-257 / UX-260) (2026-04-25)

**4 REVIEW-LATER items fully resolved + 1 partially advanced.** Open items: 22 → 18. Mixed batch covering: every Playwright spec now asserts no console errors automatically (TEST-4); two oversized frontend files decomposed into focused sub-components (MAINT-96); the three easy `prek` hooks for documented test rules landed (MAINT-99 — IPC-error-path + snapshot-redaction deferred per the original spec); a unified `Breadcrumb` design-system primitive consolidates the previously-disjoint zoom and namespace breadcrumbs (UX-257); and 6 of 7 discoverability-sweep sub-fixes shipped (UX-260, sub-fix 5 was already implemented). Five build subagents in parallel; UX-260's subagent stalled and the orchestrator took over manually; both reviewers approved with one BLOCKER (pre-existing typo, fixed) and a handful of MEDIUM findings addressed before commit.

Batch composition (5 items planned; 4 fully closed + MAINT-99 reduced to its remaining two hooks):

- **TEST-4** — Playwright console-error watcher. New helpers in `e2e/helpers.ts`:
  - `registerConsoleErrorWatcher(page)` registers `page.on('console', ...)` (filtered to `msg.type() === 'error'`) and `page.on('pageerror', ...)`. Per-page errors stored in a `WeakMap<Page, string[]>` so test isolation is automatic. Idempotent — safe to call twice on the same `Page`.
  - `getConsoleErrors(page)` returns the live array (mutating it clears the buffer; opt-out for deliberate-error specs).
  - `clearConsoleErrors(page)` for explicit clearing.
  - `expectNoConsoleErrors(page)` asserts the buffer is empty with a readable failure message that lists every captured error.
  - The watcher registration is wired into the existing global `test.beforeEach` BEFORE the mock-reset `page.evaluate` so even pre-navigation activity is captured. A new global `test.afterEach` runs `expectNoConsoleErrors(page)` so every spec inherits the assertion for free.
  - 2 conservative whitelist patterns (favicon 404, "Failed to load resource") inherited verbatim from the original `smoke.spec.ts:42-44` filter, each documented inline with rationale.
  - Spot-check on `e2e/smoke.spec.ts` (3 tests) + `e2e/inner-links.spec.ts` (22 tests) — 25/25 passed, 0 console errors surfaced.

- **MAINT-96** — Component decomposition. Pure extraction with NO behavioural change. Two independent sites:
  - **`AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx`** went 910 → 292 lines. Extracted to `src/components/agent-access/{McpStatusSection.tsx,ActivityFeed.tsx,SessionRevertControls.tsx}` plus a new `src/hooks/useMcpActivityFeed.ts` hook. The extraction preserves every `data-testid`, every i18n key, and every `logger.error('AgentAccessSettingsTab', ...)` source string (so the existing test contract still holds). 55/55 `AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx` tests pass without modification.
  - **`BacklinkFilterBuilder.tsx`** went 757 → 218 lines. The inline `AddFilterRow` sub-component (lines 234-553) extracted to `src/components/backlink-filter/AddFilterRow.tsx` along with all category-specific build helpers (`buildTypeFilter`, `buildStatusFilter`, `buildPropertyFilter`, …). 71/71 `BacklinkFilterBuilder.test.tsx` tests pass without modification.

- **MAINT-99 (partial)** — Three new `prek` hooks (the easy three; IPC-error-path and snapshot-redaction deferred per the original spec):
  - `axe-presence` — `scripts/check-axe-presence.sh`, scoped to `src/components/__tests__/*.test.tsx`. Fails if any file lacks an `axe(...)` call. Surfaced 0 violations on first activation — all 135 component test files already include an axe audit.
  - `test-file-naming` — `scripts/check-test-file-naming.sh`, walks `src/` and `e2e/` for misplaced suffixes (`.spec.tsx` under `src/`, `.test.ts` under `e2e/`, doubled-suffix files). 0 violations.
  - `agents-md-count-tables` — `scripts/check-agents-md-counts.mjs`, parses `AGENTS.md` test counts (`<N>+ tests`) and `<N> files` directory entries from `src/__tests__/AGENTS.md`, asserts each is within ±50% of actual. Tolerance documented inline. 0 violations (largest measured drift: `lib/__tests__` at +9.5%, well inside threshold).
  - All 3 hooks were synthetically tripped to confirm they fail correctly.
  - REVIEW-LATER MAINT-99 entry rewritten to track only the two remaining hooks (IPC error-path coverage, snapshot redaction) with the design approach for each.

- **UX-257** — Breadcrumb consolidation. New `src/components/ui/breadcrumb.tsx` (391 lines) primitive at the `ui/` layer. Both `BlockZoomBar.tsx` (147 → 105 lines) and `PageHeader.tsx`'s namespace breadcrumb (lines 519-549 → 520-542) now consume the primitive.
  - Container: `<nav role="navigation">` outer + `<div role="toolbar">` inner. The orchestrator's first attempt used `<ol role="toolbar">` per the spec; that triggered an `axe` `listitem` rule violation (using `role="toolbar"` strips the implicit `list` role), so the implementation switched to a `<div role="toolbar">` matching the existing `BlockZoomBar` pattern that previously passed axe. Documented inline.
  - Items: clickable `<button>` with `text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground transition-colors focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50`. Final item is a non-clickable `<span aria-current="location">` with `text-foreground font-medium`.
  - Separators: `ChevronRight` at `h-3 w-3 text-muted-foreground/50`. Canonical glyph for ALL breadcrumb separators in the app (replacing the slash separators that used to appear in `PageHeader`'s namespace breadcrumb).
  - Truncation: `max-w-[160px]` intermediate, `max-w-[320px]` final, `truncate` everywhere.
  - Overflow: when items > 5, middle crumbs collapse into a Radix `Popover` keyed by a `MoreHorizontal` icon. Layout becomes `[item₀] › […] › [itemₙ₋₂] › [itemₙ₋₁]` — head + ellipsis popover + last 2.
  - Keyboard nav: ArrowLeft/Right + Home/End at the toolbar level (UX-215). Moved out of `BlockZoomBar` and into the primitive.
  - Touch: `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:py-2` on the toolbar for the 44px hit area, NOT on individual crumbs (matches AGENTS.md mandate without per-crumb height bloat).
  - `BlockZoomBar` no longer renders `renderRichContent` inside crumbs — replaced with `stripBreadcrumbMarkup()` that resolves `[[ULID]]` / `((ULID))` via `resolveBlockTitle` (with truncated-id fallback when the cache is cold) and strips `#`, `**`, `__`, `~~`, `==`, leading `>` markers. The "giant coloured pills inside the nav bar" problem is gone.
  - `PageHeader` namespace breadcrumb's slash separators replaced with chevrons; per-crumb `touch-target` removed (the primitive handles 44px via `pointer:coarse` padding).
  - 34 new tests in `src/components/ui/__tests__/breadcrumb.test.tsx` covering render, aria-label, chevron-only separators, `aria-current="location"`, clickable vs non-clickable, truncation, overflow popover (>5 trigger, exactly-5 doesn't), keyboard nav, `dataAttributes` forwarding, axe a11y. `BlockZoomBar.test.tsx` and `PageHeader.test.tsx` updated to assert the new DOM (no nested chip elements inside `[data-zoom-crumb]`, chevron separators present).

- **UX-260 (manual takeover after subagent stall)** — Discoverability sweep. 6 of 7 sub-fixes shipped:
  - **(1)** Mobile sidebar swipe hint (`src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx`): a `pointer-events-none fixed left-0 inset-y-0 z-40 hidden w-[3px] bg-foreground/10 [@media(pointer:coarse)]:block` div rendered by `SidebarProvider` only when `isMobile && !openMobile`. The `z-40` (raised from `z-10` after the UX reviewer flagged potential z-index conflicts with overlays) ensures the hint stays visible behind transient modals.
  - **(2)** Journal nav shortcut tooltips (`src/components/JournalPage.tsx`): prev/next/today buttons each wrapped in a `Tooltip` whose `TooltipContent` shows the label plus a `<kbd className="ml-1 text-xs">Alt+←</kbd>` (or `Alt+→` / `Alt+T`). Discoverable on hover; doesn't change the button itself.
  - **(3)** Tier-aware undo tooltip (`src/components/PageHeaderMenu.tsx`): the existing `Tooltip` body is now two-line — primary label + shortcut on top, then `text-[10px] opacity-80` tip "Outside an editor: undoes the last page operation" sourced from the new `t('undo.tipPage')` key. (Reviewer iteration: the wording was rephrased from "No editor focus — …" to "Outside an editor: …" to read more naturally.) Companion key `t('undo.tipEditor')` exists for callers that may want the symmetric "Inside an editor: …" copy.
  - **(4)** Shared `BatchActionToolbar` Shift+Click hint (`src/components/BatchActionToolbar.tsx`): default `ml-auto hidden text-xs text-muted-foreground sm:inline` span rendering `t('list.rangeSelectHint')` ("Tip: Shift+click to select a range"). Hidden on mobile because the gesture is desktop-only. New `suppressRangeSelectHint?: boolean` prop opts out — used by `HistorySelectionToolbar` (already has its own `history.keyboardHint`) and `TrashView` (shipped its own `trash.batchHint` in session 483). `ConflictBatchToolbar` gets the new hint by default; `ConflictList` does use `useListMultiSelect` (line 45) so the gesture works there.
  - **(5)** Properties drawer keyboard binding — NO CODE CHANGES NEEDED. The binding `openPropertiesDrawer` already exists in `keyboard-config.ts:114` and `KeyboardShortcuts.tsx` builds its panel from `getCurrentShortcuts()`, so the shortcut is already surfaced. There is no gutter property button to attach a tooltip to. Documented this in the session log and skipped.
  - **(6)** SearchPanel header comment (`src/components/SearchPanel.tsx`): one-line JSDoc addendum referencing the `App.tsx` Ctrl+F dispatch chain at line 712.
  - **(7)** "Customize shortcuts" footer button (`src/components/KeyboardShortcuts.tsx`): new `<SheetFooter className="border-t">` with a `Button variant="outline" size="sm"` + `Settings` icon. Click closes the sheet, sets `localStorage.agaric-settings-active-tab = 'keyboard'` (try/catch for private mode), then `useNavigationStore.getState().setView('settings')`. New i18n key `keyboard.customizeButton` ("Customize shortcuts").

**Quality gate:** `npx tsc -b` clean. `npm test` → 310 files / 8237 tests passed. `cargo nextest run` → 2721 tests passed (2 skipped). `npx playwright test e2e/smoke.spec.ts e2e/inner-links.spec.ts` → 25 passed (TEST-4 console-watcher active). `prek run --all-files` → all 29 hooks green (now including the 3 new MAINT-99 hooks).

**Reviewer findings (5 actionable, all addressed):**

- *Technical reviewer (BLOCKER):* `PageHeaderMenu.tsx:132` called `getShortcutKeys('redoLastPageOp')` but the canonical shortcut id is `redoLastUndoneOp`. Pre-existing bug from commit `18041e7b` (2026-04-16) — `getShortcutKeys()` silently returns `''` for unknown IDs, so the redo tooltip rendered without a chord. Fixed.
- *Technical reviewer (HIGH):* The extracted `ActivityFeed` / `useMcpActivityFeed` files still log under the source name `'AgentAccessSettingsTab'`. Intentional — MAINT-96 is a pure extraction and the existing test contract pins the logger source string. Documented in the session log; not changed.
- *UX reviewer (MEDIUM):* `undo.tipPage` copy "No editor focus — undoes the last page operation" reads awkwardly. Rephrased to "Outside an editor: undoes the last page operation". Companion `undo.tipEditor` key updated symmetrically.
- *UX reviewer (MEDIUM):* The new `BatchActionToolbar` hint applies to `ConflictBatchToolbar` by default. Verified `ConflictList` does support Shift+Click range-select via `useListMultiSelect` (line 45) — leaving the default behaviour. The reviewer's concern was based on the assumption that conflicts don't support the gesture; they do.
- *UX reviewer (LOW):* Sidebar swipe-hint `z-10` may be obscured by overlays. Raised to `z-40`.
- *UX reviewer (LOW):* Journal nav tooltip `<kbd>` had `opacity-80`, reducing contrast against the tooltip background. Removed the opacity attribute.
- *UX reviewer (LOW / NIT):* Three tooltip / contrast / chevron-opacity items deferred — not introduced by this batch (chevron 50% opacity is from the existing design tokens), edge cases that would need real-device testing, or out of scope.

**Reviewer findings deferred:**

- HIGH (logger source): preserving `'AgentAccessSettingsTab'` in extracted files is the correct call for a pure-extraction refactor (MAINT-96 spec) and matches the existing test contract.
- MEDIUM (SSR check on `localStorage.setItem`): not applicable — Tauri is a client-only runtime, no SSR.
- LOW (overflow-popover screen reader double-announcement; tooltip `<div>` paragraph announcement; chevron 50% opacity contrast): all need real-device / real-AT testing to confirm; tracked as open observations rather than fixes.

**Architectural notes:**
- Touched 5 production components (`AgentAccessSettingsTab`, `BacklinkFilterBuilder`, `BlockZoomBar`, `PageHeader`, plus the smaller UX-260 surfaces) without changing any AGENTS.md invariants. New extracted files use the existing folder conventions (kebab-case directories, `useFooBar` hooks). The new `Breadcrumb` primitive lives in `src/components/ui/` per the documented component hierarchy.
- One new shared hook: `useMcpActivityFeed`. One new shared primitive: `Breadcrumb`. Both added to FEATURE-MAP.md.
- 3 new `prek` hooks (`axe-presence`, `test-file-naming`, `agents-md-count-tables`) make the documented test conventions enforced rather than aspirational.

## Session 484 — Frontend UX bundle: graph a11y + agenda polish + history/trash residue + bug-report log preview + gutter overflow on touch (UX-270 / UX-274 / UX-275 / UX-277 / UX-281) (2026-04-25)

**5 REVIEW-LATER items fully resolved.** Open items: 27 → 22. All-frontend cluster touching graph, agenda, history/trash, bug-report and gutter — five non-overlapping subagents in parallel, all returned green, both reviewers run in parallel afterwards. Tech reviewer: clean. UX reviewer raised 15 findings (mostly LOW/MEDIUM); 5 actionable findings addressed (Retry button busy state, "Hunk N of M" → "N of M changes", property-key warning copy "is not a known property" → "is not yet defined on this device", AlertCircle icon prefix on DateChipEditor parse-error, refactor TrashView keyboard-handler past Biome cognitive-complexity ceiling).

Batch composition (5 items, all closed):

- **UX-270** — `GraphView` a11y + filter persistence. 5 sub-fixes:
  - **(1)** `GraphFilterBar` tag list now uses `ScrollArea` instead of bare `overflow-y-auto`, satisfying AGENTS.md.
  - **(2)** Removed redundant `aria-label={tag.name}` from each tag checkbox — the wrapping `<label>` already provides the accessible name.
  - **(3)** Dropped `role="img"` from the `GraphView` SVG wrapper (interactive descendants violate that role); `aria-label` retained, `data-testid="graph-svg"` added.
  - **(4)** Filter state persisted to `localStorage` under `agaric:graph-filters` (SSR-safe, try/catch on JSON parse + serialize, `logger.warn` on failure). Hydrate-on-mount + write-on-change `useEffect` pair, with a first-commit guard via `useRef` so the parent's pre-hydration default doesn't clobber the persisted value.
  - **(5)** 7-line JSDoc block above `attachNodeClickAndKeyboard()` in `useGraphSimulation.ts` documenting the keyboard-navigation pattern (`tabindex="0"` + `role="button"` + Enter/Space → `navigateToPage`, with `preventDefault` on Space).
  - +11 tests across `GraphFilterBar.test.tsx`, `GraphView.test.tsx`, `useGraphSimulation.test.ts`. No new i18n keys.

- **UX-274** — Agenda views: error retry, parse feedback, persistence, keyboard nav. 7 sub-fixes:
  - **(1)** `DateChipEditor` parse-error visualisation — `aria-invalid={parseError}`, conditional `border-destructive` via `cn()` on the input, plus an `AlertCircle` icon prefix on the helper-text error (added during reviewer follow-up).
  - **(2)** `QueryResult` error banner now exposes a Retry button (`variant="outline"`, `size="sm"`) wired to `fetchResults`, `role="alert"` on the container, `disabled={loading || loadingMore}` + `aria-busy` to prevent double-submission (reviewer finding).
  - **(3)** `RescheduleDropZone` JSDoc block at the top documenting the keyboard reschedule path via `DateChipEditor`. Right-click context-menu reschedule deferred — would require a new `ContextMenu` Radix primitive plus wiring across `BlockListItem` + `DateChipEditor`, beyond scope.
  - **(4)** `UnfinishedTasks` per-group collapse persisted under `agaric:unfinishedTasks.groupCollapsed`. Object-of-booleans schema with try/catch on JSON.parse + per-entry validation (drops non-boolean values).
  - **(5)** `AgendaFilterBuilder` Apply button — verified the shared `Button` primitive already bakes `disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:pointer-events-none` into its CVA base. Locked in via a regression test asserting these classes appear on the disabled button.
  - **(6)** `DuePanel` projected entries now part of the `useListKeyboardNavigation` flat-items array. `uniqueProjected` hoisted out of the JSX IIFE into a `useMemo` so it's available to the keyboard-nav hook; each projected `<li>` got `data-block-list-item`, `tabIndex={0}` (with `biome-ignore` comment), `data-testid="projected-entry"`, focus-ring on focused index.
  - **(7)** `QueryBuilderModal` property-key autocomplete via `<datalist>` + soft warning. `useEffect` (open-gated, with `cancelled` cleanup flag) calls `listPropertyDefs()` and stores keys; warning shown when key is non-empty + defs loaded + key not in list. Intentionally non-blocking (forward-compat for queries against future-defined properties). Reviewer rephrased copy from "is not a known property" → "is not yet defined on this device. The query will still run; matches appear once the property exists."
  - +20 tests across 7 test files. 2 new i18n keys: `duePanel.projectedListLabel`, `queryBuilder.propertyKeyUnknown`.

- **UX-275 (residue)** — History view UX residue (the 8 sub-fixes left over from the session-483 partial):
  - **(1)** `DiffDisplay` hunk navigation: prev/next buttons (ChevronUp/ChevronDown), `useMemo`-computed hunk starts (consecutive non-`Equal` runs), refs map for `scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest', behavior: 'smooth' })`, "N of M changes" counter (rephrased from "Change N of M" per reviewer feedback), buttons disabled at boundaries. `<div role="region" aria-label={t('diff.regionLabel')}>` wrapper.
  - **(2)** `TrashView` descendant badge: `whitespace-nowrap` + new stable `data-testid="trash-descendant-badge"` (renamed from the older `trash-item-batch-count`).
  - **(3)** `TrashView` Shift+R / Shift+Delete keyboard shortcuts gated by `selected.size > 0`. `aria-keyshortcuts` advertised on the buttons. The keyboard handler was extracted into a `tryBatchShortcut(e)` helper after `prek` flagged the parent function's cognitive complexity at 36 (Biome max 25); the refactor brought it back under the ceiling.
  - **(4)** `HistoryPanel` restore success now snapshots the current block via `getBlock()` BEFORE calling `editBlock`, so the toast carries an Undo `action` (`label: t('action.undo')`, `onClick` re-applies snapshot). Snapshot failure logs `logger.warn` and falls back to a no-action toast.
  - **(5)** `HistoryListItem` checkbox: visible `focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:ring-offset-1` + `data-testid="history-checkbox-${index}"`. Both row-click and checkbox-click remain functional (matches the existing test contract, kept intentionally).
  - **(6)** `HistoryListItem` Restore-to-here button: appended `<span aria-hidden="true" className="hidden [@media(pointer:coarse)]:inline ml-1 text-xs">` showing `t('history.restoreToHereTouchLabel')` only on touch. `aria-hidden` on the visible label so SRs aren't read a duplicate of the button's `aria-label`.
  - **(7)** `HistoryView` error categorisation. `categorizeHistoryError(err)` helper inspects message + `obj.status`/`obj.code` 5xx ranges to bucket into `network` / `server` / `unknown`. Banner now shows category-specific copy (`history.errorNetwork` / `history.errorServer` / `history.errorUnknown`); `data-error-category` attribute exposed for tests / CSS hooks. Full error logged via `logger.error('HistoryView', ...)`.
  - **(8)** `TrashView` batch-restore confirmation when `selected.size > 5`. `BATCH_RESTORE_CONFIRM_THRESHOLD = 5` constant inside the component, mirrors the batch-purge confirm pattern.
  - +27 tests across 5 test files. 12 new i18n keys (`diff.hunkCounter`, `diff.nextHunk`, `diff.prevHunk`, `diff.regionLabel`, `trash.batchRestoreConfirmDescription`, `trash.batchRestoreConfirmTitle`, `history.errorNetwork`, `history.errorServer`, `history.errorUnknown`, `history.restoreUndoFailed`, `history.restoreUndone`, `history.restoreToHereTouchLabel`).

- **UX-277** — `BugReportDialog` log-content preview before submit. H-9c (`bug_report_preview()` Tauri command) checked first via grep — not yet shipped. Used the fallback path: per-click invocation of the existing `readLogsForReport(redact)` IPC, plucking the matching `LogFileEntry.contents` by filename. Per-row `<Eye />` Preview button opens a sub-dialog (Radix Dialog stacks above the parent via portal) showing the first 500 chars in a `<pre className="whitespace-pre-wrap break-words font-mono">` inside a `ScrollArea` (`max-h-96`); truncation notice when content > 500 chars. Loading state via shared `Spinner` + `aria-busy="true"`; error state via `role="alert"` + `logger.error`. +7 tests covering all paths. 4 new i18n keys (`bugReport.previewError`, `bugReport.previewLabel`, `bugReport.previewLoading`, `bugReport.previewTruncated`); reused existing `bugReport.previewTitle`.

- **UX-281** — Gutter buttons on touch. Picked Option 3 from the REVIEW-LATER spec (overflow Sheet) — the smallest visual change. New `useIsTouch()` hook (`src/hooks/useIsTouch.ts`, 38 lines) reads `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)')` with a `change` listener inside `useEffect`, falls back to `false` when `window` / `matchMedia` are undefined. `BlockGutterControls` branches on `useIsTouch()`: desktop renders the existing 3 buttons unchanged; touch renders a vertical `flex flex-col` lane with the drag handle + a single `MoreVertical` overflow button that opens a right-side `Sheet` with two labelled rows (History → `onShowHistory`, Delete → `onDelete`, both wrapped in `SheetClose asChild`). Overflow button advertises `aria-label`, `aria-haspopup="dialog"`, `aria-expanded={sheetOpen}`. `SortableBlock` NOT modified — vertical stacking on touch fits the existing 68px gutter without width change. +10 tests in `BlockGutterControls.test.tsx`; SortableBlock 188 pre-existing tests verified clean (no regressions). 3 new i18n keys: `block.actionsSheetDescription`, `block.actionsSheetTitle`, `block.moreActionsLabel`.

**Quality gate:** `npx tsc -b` clean. `npm test` → 309 files / 8199 tests passed (75 new in this session). `cargo nextest run` → 2721 tests passed (2 skipped). `prek run --all-files` → all 26 hooks green. The Biome cognitive-complexity violation in `TrashView` (the merged Shift-shortcut + nav handler hit complexity 36) was caught by `prek` and resolved via the `tryBatchShortcut(e)` helper extraction described above. A separate `prek` follow-up — Biome `noNoninteractiveTabindex` — flagged the projected-entry `<li>` in `DuePanel`; the existing `biome-ignore` comment was repositioned to be directly adjacent to the `tabIndex={0}` line so Biome would honor it.

**Reviewer findings (UX, 5 actionable of 15 total):**

- *MEDIUM:* `diff.hunkCounter` "Change N of M" reads as individual edits. → "N of M changes" (matches conventional diff terminology, less jargon than "Hunk").
- *MEDIUM:* `queryBuilder.propertyKeyUnknown` copy reads as an error. → "is not yet defined on this device. The query will still run; matches appear once the property exists." Test text matchers updated.
- *MEDIUM:* `DateChipEditor` parse-error border alone is easy to miss. → Added an `AlertCircle` icon prefix to the helper-text error message.
- *LOW:* `QueryResult` Retry button has no loading state. → Added `disabled={loading || loadingMore}` + `aria-busy` so users can't double-submit.

The 10 deferred findings either:
- describe pre-existing behaviour (e.g., `STORAGE_KEY` legacy prefix in `UnfinishedTasks`, fixed-height `max-h-40` on the graph tag list, hardcoded threshold 5),
- require a real touch device to validate (e.g., focus-ring clipping suspicion), or
- recommend backend-side error type changes (`HistoryView` error categorisation via structured types) that are out of scope for a frontend-only batch.

## Session 483 — `data-testid` selector hardening + sync error UX polish + conflict-resolution polish + history/trash UX polish (TEST-11 / UX-264 / UX-265 / UX-275 partial; MAINT-103 deferred) (2026-04-25)

**3 REVIEW-LATER items fully resolved + 1 partially advanced; 1 deferred.** Open items: 30 → 27. Mixed batch covering: e2e selector convention compliance (26 `data-testid` conversions across 9 specs + 7 production components), sync UX polish (retry-on-failure toast, online-resumed toast, conflict batch progress, camera-denied auto-fallback), conflict-resolution polish (Keep/Discard tooltips, sort + filter dropdowns, type-badge tooltip, missing-original-block fallback banner, large-diff toggle), and the high-value half of UX-275 history/trash polish (5 of 12 sub-fixes shipped). Two build subagents stalled mid-run (UX-275, MAINT-103); orchestrator killed both, took over UX-275 manually, deferred MAINT-103 (anchor-ref refactor too large for manual fallback). Three build subagents in parallel for the rest (TEST-11, UX-264, UX-265); technical and UX reviewers raised eight findings — all addressed before commit.

Batch composition (5 items planned; 3 fully closed + UX-275 partial + MAINT-103 deferred):

- **TEST-11** — 26 selector conversions from `.css-class` / hardcoded `aria-label` / Tailwind utility selectors to stable `data-testid` per `src/__tests__/AGENTS.md:404`. 9 e2e specs touched: `agenda-advanced` (7), `attachments` (5), `batch-operations` (3), `block-ref-picker`, `features-coverage`, `inner-links` (2), `suggestion-keyboard`, `sync-ui` (2), `toolbar-and-blocks`. 7 production components hardened with new `data-testid`s: `BlockListRenderer`, `LinkedReferences`, `DeviceManagement`, `BlockInlineControls`, `AgendaResults`, `block-tree/BlockContextMenu`, `editor/extensions/external-link`. One Tailwind utility selector (`e2e/tag-management.spec.ts:185`) intentionally skipped — utility-class binding, not component-class binding. CSS classes preserved for styling; new `data-testid`s are additive.

- **UX-264** — Sync error UX, all 4 sub-fixes shipped:
  - **(1) Retry on sync failure** — `useSyncTrigger.ts` failure toasts now carry an action button (`label: t('sync.retryAction')`, default "Retry sync") that re-invokes `startSync(peerId)`. Toast `duration: 5000` so the action is visible long enough to click.
  - **(2) Offline → online transition feedback** — `useSyncTrigger.ts` listens for the `'online'` window event and emits `toast.info(t('sync.backOnline'))` ("Back online. Syncing…") when the previous sync state was `offline`. Idle / connected previous states do NOT fire to avoid noise.
  - **(3) Conflict batch progress** — `ConflictList.tsx` keep / discard batch ops show a per-item progress indicator using a new `t('conflicts.batchProgress', { current, total })` key ("Resolving {{current}} of {{total}}…"). Replaces the bare spinner that left users uncertain on 50+-conflict batches.
  - **(4) Camera-denied auto-fallback** — `QrScanner.tsx` reports a `cameraDenied` callback to the parent `PairingEntryForm.tsx`, which auto-switches to manual passphrase entry and toasts `t('pairing.cameraDeniedFallback')` ("Camera unavailable — enter passphrase manually"). User no longer has to click "Type Passphrase" themselves.
  - +7 new tests across `useSyncTrigger`, `QrScanner`, `PairingEntryForm`, and `ConflictList`. 4 new i18n keys: `sync.retryAction`, `sync.backOnline`, `conflicts.batchProgress`, `pairing.cameraDeniedFallback`.

- **UX-265** — Conflict UI polish, all 5 sub-fixes shipped:
  - **(1) Keep / Discard tooltips** — `ConflictListItem.tsx` Keep + Discard buttons get explicit Tooltips so the visual hierarchy (outline vs destructive) is no longer the only signal. Action order kept stable; tooltip copy clarifies which is the "use the incoming version" / "delete the conflict copy" semantic.
  - **(2) Sort + filter on conflict list** — `ConflictList.tsx` gets a `data-testid="conflict-filter-bar"` filter row with three Selects: filter by conflict type, source device, and date range. Empty / all-pass states preserved. Necessary after re-pair scenarios that yield 50+ conflicts.
  - **(3) Type-badge tooltip** — `ConflictListItem.tsx` type badge wrapped in a `Tooltip` exposing the `conflict.type${type}` description so users hover to see what each badge means.
  - **(4) Missing-original-block fallback banner** — `ConflictTypeRenderer.tsx` Property / Move conflicts that fall through to `TextConflictView` because the original block is missing now show a warning banner ("Original block not found — showing conflict content only") and disable the Keep action. The misleading "(original not available)" placeholder is gone.
  - **(5) Large-diff toggle** — `DiffDisplay.tsx` introduces a "Show full diff" / "Collapse" toggle for diffs above a 500-character threshold; collapsed view truncates to 100 visible characters with an ellipsis. Container `max-h-40` upstream still applies for short diffs.
  - +21 new tests across `ConflictList`, `ConflictListItem`, `ConflictTypeRenderer`, and `DiffDisplay`. 17 new i18n keys (filter labels, tooltip copy, fallback banner copy, large-diff toggle copy, "View original" button text).

- **UX-275 (partial)** — 5 of 12 sub-fixes shipped (the wording / a11y polish; the remaining 7 are heavier interaction work and stay open):
  - **(1)** `history.restoreToDescription` reworded to make the scope explicit and add "This action itself can be undone, and the original operations remain in history." so the confirmation no longer feels one-way.
  - **(2)** Non-reversible icon a11y reworked. `Lock` aria-label is now the short standalone `Non-reversible action` and the existing `Tooltip` carries the specific reason (`Purges cannot be undone`). Screen readers and pointer users get complementary information instead of duplicated text.
  - **(3)** `HistoryFilterBar.tsx` gains an inline ✕ Clear button next to the op-type Select — visible only when `opTypeFilter !== null`. Uses the shared `Button` primitive (so focus-ring + 44px touch target are inherited via CVA) and `data-testid="history-filter-clear"`.
  - **(6, partial)** `TrashView.tsx` selection toolbar now surfaces a visible "Tip: Shift+click to select a range" hint (`data-testid="trash-batch-hint"`, `t('trash.batchHint')`) inside the `BatchActionToolbar`. Hidden on mobile (`hidden sm:inline`) so touch users aren't shown a desktop-only affordance. Keyboard shortcuts for Restore / Purge still pending (left to UX-275 residue).
  - **(9)** `TrashView.tsx` filter input now uses `SearchInput`'s built-in clear (UX-221 primitive) instead of a hand-rolled `<button>` — the orchestrator's first manual pass added a duplicate ✕; the UX reviewer caught it. Final wiring passes `clearAriaLabelKey="trash.searchClear"` so the existing aria-label translation still applies.
  - 3 new i18n keys: `history.clearFilter`, `trash.searchClear`, `trash.batchHint`. 2 i18n keys reworded: `history.nonReversibleLabel`, `history.nonReversibleTooltip`. 2 tests updated to use regex `getByLabelText(/Non-reversible/)` since the label is no longer a literal short string.
  - REVIEW-LATER UX-275 entry retitled to `History view UX residue` and trimmed to the 7 remaining sub-fixes: DiffDisplay hunk navigation, descendant-count badge wrap, batch keyboard shortcuts, restore-action undo toast, checkbox row-click ambiguity, "Restore to here" icon-only on touch, generic error banner, and inconsistent batch-restore confirmation.

- **MAINT-103 (deferred)** — `BlockPropertyEditor` inline-editor portal refactor stalled when the build subagent could not converge on a layout that preserved the existing `useFloating` anchor + `whileElementsMounted` callback contract while moving the popover into a `Portal`. Subagent killed; manual fallback judged too large for a session-483 slot (would require parallel changes in `useBlockProperties`, the `PropertyPicker` extension, and a new `data-property-popover` selector list for `EDITOR_PORTAL_SELECTORS`). REVIEW-LATER MAINT-103 entry kept verbatim for a future session that can dedicate the full block.

**Quality gate:** `npx tsc -b` clean. `npm test` → 309 files / 8124 tests passed (one HistoryListItem.test.tsx assertion needed a regex update post-i18n change). `cargo nextest run` → 2721 tests passed (2 skipped). `prek run --all-files` → all 26 hooks green (biome auto-fixed one stale formatting in `ConflictList.test.tsx` after the subagent diff applied).

**Reviewer findings (8 total, all addressed before commit):**

- *Technical reviewer (HIGH):* TrashView's hand-rolled clear ✕ duplicated `SearchInput`'s built-in clear and was missing the mandatory focus-ring + 44px touch-target classes. Reverted in favour of `SearchInput`'s `clearAriaLabelKey` prop.
