## Session 466 — UX-252 + UX-253 resolved: activity-feed Undo-after-success + touch discoverability (2026-04-23)

**2 REVIEW-LATER items resolved (UX-252, UX-253), 0 new filed.** Open-items count 19 → 17. Both were S-cost pure-frontend items filed during the review passes of sessions 464 and 465, both touching `src/components/AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx`. Session 465's notes explicitly called them out as a natural batch. Implemented together in one build subagent, one review subagent, one commit (`83ce963`), and one bookkeeping commit (this entry).

### What changed (commit `83ce963`)

**UX-252 — Revert-session / Undo buttons disappear after success:**

- **`src/components/AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx`** — new ephemeral state `revertedOpKeys: Set<string>` (lazy-initialized, keyed by `${device_id}:${seq}`, scoped to the component lifetime). `handleUndo` adds the reverted opRef's key to the set in the `try` block AFTER `await revertOps({ ops: [opRef] })` succeeds; `confirmRevertSession` iterates `target.ops` and adds every key in the `try` block after the batch `revertOps({ ops: target.ops })` succeeds. Error paths leave the set unchanged so the user can retry. The `undoableBySession` memo gains `revertedOpKeys` in its dep array and filters entries whose opRef key is in the set; the per-entry `canUndo` predicate gets the same filter. Result: after a successful revert, the affected button (or header, when all ops of a session have been reverted) simply disappears from the DOM — the entry row itself stays visible so users still see the history of what the agent did.
- The pattern avoids reusing `undoingKeys` (slice 3 in-flight marker) and `revertingSessions` (slice 4 in-flight marker); those are transient, `revertedOpKeys` is terminal. Keeping them separate preserves the semantics of each.
- No persist wiring. No Zustand. No localStorage. Scoped to the component lifetime, which matches the activity feed's own ephemeral 100-entry render ring.

**UX-253 — Per-entry Undo button gains visible text on touch:**

- **`src/components/AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx`** — the non-spinner branch of the per-entry Undo button body now renders `<Undo2>` + `<span className="hidden [@media(pointer:coarse)]:inline ml-1">{t('agentAccess.undoAgentOp.buttonText')}</span>`. Desktop (fine pointer) keeps the existing icon-only chrome; touch devices (coarse pointer) show `[↶ Undo]`. Matches the `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:` pattern already used by `TrashView`, `SearchPanel`, `PageBrowser`, `PropertyDefinitionsList`, `TemplatesView`, and `SourcePageFilter`.
- **`src/lib/i18n.ts`** — one new key `agentAccess.undoAgentOp.buttonText` → `"Undo"`.
- The button's `aria-label` is unchanged (still carries the full verb "Undo this agent action" for screen readers) — double-labeling "Undo" + "Undo this agent action" would be noise, so the visible span's short word + the aria-label's full phrase cover complementary audiences.
- The session-header button is untouched — it already has a visible "Revert session" label.

**Tests — `src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx`:**

- **+6 UX-252 tests** (`describe('... revertedOpKeys tracking (UX-252)', ...)`):
  1. After successful per-entry Undo, the button disappears for that entry.
  2. Entry metadata (Badge, summary, time) stays rendered.
  3. After successful per-session Revert, the session header disappears.
  4. Error path keeps the button clickable (retry possible).
  5. Header count decreases as individual undos happen (5 → 4 → …).
  6. Header disappears when remaining undoable op count drops below 2.
- **+2 UX-253 tests** (`describe('... touch discoverability (UX-253)', ...)`):
  1. The per-entry Undo button contains a `<span>` with the "Undo" i18n text AND the `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:inline` class (pins the responsive-visibility contract; jsdom can't apply CSS media queries, so the test asserts the DOM structure + className rather than visual behaviour).
  2. While in-flight, the `<span>` is not in the DOM — the spinner replaces icon + text together.
- **1 existing test updated** — `"shows a spinner and disables the button while the revert is in flight"`: pre-UX-252, the test held a reference to the button DOM node and asserted `not.toBeDisabled()` after resolve. UX-252 removes the node from the React tree on success, so the detached reference's `disabled=true` from its last render persisted. Fixed per the spec's flagged "likely-affected" call-out: replaced the stale reference with `queryByLabelText(undoLabel)` (returns `null` once the button leaves the DOM) + a row-still-rendered assertion.

### Verification

- `npx vitest run src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx`: **55/55 passed** (was 47; +8 net, 1 updated). Final run duration ~3.2 s.
- `prek run --all-files`: all 26 hooks green after a single biome formatter auto-fix (one multi-line `toHaveTextContent` call collapsed to single-line).
- No Rust, backend, sqlx, or specta change — no backend test runs required.

### Design decisions

- **Ephemeral in-component state, not persisted.** The REVIEW-LATER entry's Option 1 was explicitly about keeping state scoped to the component lifetime. Persisting reverted-opRef tracking would couple the activity feed (a short-lived UI surface) to long-term storage for no durable benefit — the feed itself rolls over at 100 entries. Option 2 (refresh-from-backend) was rejected for the same scoping reason.
- **Keep `revertedOpKeys` distinct from `undoingKeys` / `revertingSessions`.** Three independent Sets, three independent lifecycles. `undoingKeys` clears in the per-entry `finally`. `revertingSessions` clears in the per-session `finally`. `revertedOpKeys` only grows (within the component lifetime) and is cleared implicitly on unmount. Conflating any two would entangle in-flight with terminal states and subtly break either retry or the disappear-after-success guarantee.
- **Responsive text for UX-253, not always-visible.** Option 2 (always-visible "Undo" next to the icon, matching the session-header button's pattern) would add ~40-50 px of chrome weight to every activity row on desktop. The activity feed is a compact 100-row scroll region; adding a text label to each button would visibly thicken it. Gating behind `[@media(pointer:coarse)]` preserves desktop minimalism AND solves the touch-discoverability gap.
- **Test with DOM structure, not visual behaviour, for media-query classes.** jsdom does not apply CSS `@media` rules, so a test can't assert "the span is visually hidden on desktop". The substitute is to assert (a) the span is in the DOM (with its i18n text) when the icon is visible, and (b) the span carries the `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:inline` class — which pins the contract that, on real devices, the CSS will do the right thing. The axe test (shipped by session 463) still validates a11y regardless of media-query state.

### Architectural invariants respected (AGENTS.md)

- **Op log append-only, CQRS split, sqlx compile-time queries** — all untouched. Frontend-only change.
- **No new tables, op types, Zustand stores, materializer queues, sync message types.** `revertedOpKeys` is component-local `useState`.
- **No `biome-ignore`, `@ts-ignore`, non-null assertions, or silent catch blocks.** All error paths continue to use `logger.error` + toast.
- **Radix UI + `cn()` + semantic tokens** — no new hardcoded Tailwind colors; the new utility class is the established `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:` arbitrary-media-query pattern.
- **`exactOptionalPropertyTypes` / `noImplicitReturns` / `useExplicitLengthCheck`** — all satisfied.
- **React 19 conventions:** no `React.forwardRef`, no `React.ComponentRef`, functional setState updates throughout, lazy `useState` init.

### Review summary (fresh-eyes `subagent_explore`)

APPROVE WITH NOTES. Zero P1/P2 blockers. All critical correctness checks passed: `revertedOpKeys` is added only on success (not in `finally`, not on error), both memo dep arrays are correct, `canUndo` predicate is extended, `firstSeenIdxBySession` is deliberately untouched (header hides via the existing `sessionOps.length >= 2` gate), UX-253's span is inside the non-spinner branch, `aria-label` + tooltip are preserved. P3s — session-revert error-path test gap (code path identical to per-entry, low risk), concurrent per-entry + session race (pre-existing, inherent to user concurrency, doesn't get worse), ring rollover dead-key memory (≤ 4 KB, negligible). All accepted as non-actionable.

### Notes for the next session

- **FEAT-4 v2 now polished as well as shipped.** Slices 1-4 done; activity-feed UX tightened against double-revert and touch-icon obscurity. Only FEAT-4i (v3 Mobile) remains in the FEAT-4 umbrella, DEFERRED.
- **Remaining open items (17):** FEAT-3, FEAT-4, FEAT-4i, FEAT-5, FEAT-5g, FEAT-7, FEAT-8, FEAT-9, PERF-19, PERF-20, PERF-23, PUB-2, PUB-3, PUB-5, PUB-7, UX-249, UX-250. FEAT-7 (shell-level TabBar) is the next ready-but-sign-off-gated item; everything else is either deferred, an explicit non-fix (PERF-*), or blocked on user sign-off (FEAT-3 needs 8-question sign-off, FEAT-7 needs visual-direction sign-off, FEAT-8/9 depend on FEAT-7, UX-249 pairs with UX-250, UX-250 needs Option A/B decision).
- **Next PROMPT.md batch is narrow.** The ready + no-sign-off set is now empty. Next session likely needs user input before more batches can be productively worked.

## Session 465 — Fresh-eyes review of recent MCP work + two polish fixes + UX-253 filed (2026-04-23)

**0 REVIEW-LATER items resolved, 1 new filed (UX-253).** Open-items count 18 → 19. User asked for a fresh-eyes review of the recent MCP work (sessions 463 + 464 — FEAT-4c emission wiring + FEAT-4h slice 3 per-entry Undo + FEAT-4h slice 4 per-session bulk-revert) treated holistically as if following PROMPT.md's step 4 (no self-reviews; technical + UX dimensions in parallel). Two read-only subagents ran concurrently; both returned APPROVE WITH NOTES with zero P1 blockers. Triaged findings into "apply now" (two polish fixes) and "file as follow-up" (one new UX item). One small code commit closes the loop.

Reviewers:

- **Technical subagent (`subagent_explore`):** APPROVE WITH NOTES, zero P1/P2. Verified every claim from session 463 + 464: `ACTOR` outer / `LAST_APPEND` inner scope nesting, op_ref capture inside the scope before it exits, Unicode-safe error-message clipping via `.chars().take(200)`, session-id stability across requests, `skip_serializing_if` omits `opRef` from the wire payload when None, snapshot-at-click semantics for slice 4's `pendingSessionRevert`, in-flight state cleanup in `finally` with defensive Set-check, per-task isolation of `LAST_APPEND.scope` via `multi_thread` tokio test. Confirmed all AGENTS.md invariants (op log append-only, CQRS split, sqlx compile-time queries, no new `unsafe`, no new `#[allow]` / `biome-ignore` / `@ts-ignore`, no new non-null assertions, no new tables / op types / stores / materializer queues / sync message types). Only P3s surfaced (coexistence test + double-click test + touch tooltip — all acceptable or already covered).
- **UX subagent (`subagent_explore`):** APPROVE WITH NOTES. Three P2 findings — (1) session-header visible `<span>` duplicated the button's `aria-label` verbatim; (2) per-entry Undo button icon-only on touch where Radix Tooltip doesn't fire; (3) tooltip content == aria-label (codebase pattern, skipped). Three P3 findings — narrow-viewport visual distinction for the header, confirm-description copy ("from" → "in"), ring-rollover-during-confirm documentation (already has comments). Copy review passed verbatim across all 16 new i18n keys.

### What changed this session (commit `TBD`)

**Polish fix P2-1 — session-header label clarity:**

- **`src/lib/i18n.ts`** (+4) — added two new pluralized keys:
  - `agentAccess.revertSession.headerLabel_one` → `"{{count}} agent action"`
  - `agentAccess.revertSession.headerLabel_other` → `"{{count}} agent actions"`
  The existing `buttonAriaLabel_*` keys stay as-is (the button's aria-label still carries the full verb for screen readers).
- **`src/components/AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx`** (+6 / −3) — the visible `<span>` on the session-header row now uses the new `headerLabel` key. A comment block explains the deliberate split: visible text = short count, button aria-label + tooltip = full verb. No change to the button's appearance or semantics.

**Polish fix P3-2 — confirm-description copy precision:**

- **`src/lib/i18n.ts`** (+0 / −0, 2 words changed) — `"{{count}} agent action(s) from this session"` → `"{{count}} agent action(s) in this session"` in both `confirmDescription_one` and `confirmDescription_other`. Locative "in" reads more naturally than extractive "from".

**Test updates:**

- **`src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx`** (+4 / −1) — (1) extended the "renders a session header" test to assert the header's visible text is `"3 agent actions"` AND explicitly NOT `"Revert this agent session"` (pins the P2-1 fix); (2) updated the "clicking Revert session opens the confirm dialog with the correct count" test to match the new "in this session" copy.

**New REVIEW-LATER item:**

- **UX-253 — Per-entry activity-feed Undo button is icon-only on touch.** Files the P2 the UX reviewer surfaced about sighted touch-only users seeing a lone `Undo2` icon (Radix Tooltip does not fire on touch; only the `aria-label` is present for screen readers; button is still tappable but discoverability is poor). Entry captures four fix options (responsive visible text via `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:inline` — recommended; always-visible text; click-to-toggle touch tooltip; do-nothing); test plan; do-not list (don't remove the icon, don't double-label aria, don't touch the slice-4 session-header button); and a note that it can pair with UX-252 in one polish session. Cost: S (~45 min).

### Verification

- `npx vitest run src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx`: 47/47 passed (same count; P2-1 + P3-2 fixes cleanly absorbed without net test count change).
- `prek run --all-files`: all 26 hooks green on the final run.
- No Rust, backend, sqlx, or specta change.

### Design decisions

- **Apply only the clear wins; file the rest.** The UX reviewer raised three P2s. P2-1 (label redundancy) is a clear semantic win with a well-scoped fix. P3-2 (copy "from" → "in") is a two-word literal change. Both applied. P2-2 (touch discoverability) requires design judgement on four distinct fix shapes and is best-addressed with dedicated thinking — filed as UX-253. P2-3 (tooltip == aria-label) is the established codebase pattern and not worth churning.
- **Keep `buttonAriaLabel_*` keys separate from `headerLabel_*` keys.** The button's aria-label preserves the full verb ("Revert this agent session (N actions)") for screen-reader users who need the complete context at the action site. The header's visible label drops the verb because the button directly below it already carries it as visible text ("Revert session"). Two keys, two audiences, zero redundancy.
- **Test both directions of the P2-1 fix.** The test now asserts BOTH `toHaveTextContent('3 agent actions')` AND `not.toHaveTextContent('Revert this agent session')` — a future regression that reverted to the redundant copy would fail the second assertion even if the first still passed (superset match).

### Notes for the next session

- **UX-252 + UX-253 pair naturally** — both touch `AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx`, both are S-cost, both are strictly additive polish. A single follow-up session can close both, bringing the MCP activity-feed UX to a tight state (no stale buttons, clear touch discoverability). Total cost: ~2 h.
- **No open FEAT-4h work remains.** The umbrella stays IN PROGRESS only because v3 (FEAT-4i — Mobile) is DEFERRED; v2 is fully shipped and polished.

## Session 464 — FEAT-4h slice 4 per-session bulk-revert (closes FEAT-4h) + UX-252 filed (2026-04-23)

**1 REVIEW-LATER item resolved (FEAT-4h), 1 new filed (UX-252).** Open-items count unchanged at 18. FEAT-4h (MCP v2 umbrella) fully closed — its table row + detail section removed, and the FEAT-4 umbrella Status line updated to note v2 complete. A second commit was NOT needed for docs — the slice 4 implementation was found already staged at session start (from a prior agent pass that never committed); orchestrator reviewed, ran prek, committed code as `0157192`, then landed bookkeeping separately.

Flow this session was telescoped:

1. `git status` at session start surfaced unstaged edits across `AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx` + its test file + `i18n.ts` matching the FEAT-4h slice 4 spec from the REVIEW-LATER entry. Implementation was well-structured (derived-data memos, confirm-dialog snapshot semantics, per-session in-flight tracking, proper i18next `_one`/`_other` pluralization).
2. `npx vitest run src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx`: 47/47 passed (was 37 at session 463 close; +10 new tests).
3. `prek run --all-files`: one biome formatter round (auto-fixed in 2 files) then all 26 hooks green.
4. Fresh-eyes subagent review (`subagent_explore` — READ-ONLY, per PROMPT.md §"No self-reviews"): APPROVE WITH NOTES, zero P1. One P2 flagged (double-revert UX — user can re-click Undo / Revert-session after success and hit unexpected backend toggle behaviour). P2 filed as UX-252 (inherits from slice 3's identical path, affects both slices, fix scope is purely-frontend).
5. Commit `0157192 feat(mcp): FEAT-4h slice 4` with full prek pass.
6. Docs session (this entry + REVIEW-LATER updates) as a follow-up commit.

### What changed

**Frontend — FEAT-4h slice 4 session-grouped bulk revert (commit `0157192`):**

- **`src/components/AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx` (+268 / −54)** — two new `useMemo`s derive per-session data from the existing `entries` state without mutating it: `undoableBySession: Map<sessionId, OpRef[]>` filters to `actorKind === 'agent' && result.kind === 'ok' && opRef != null` entries, and `firstSeenIdxBySession: Map<sessionId, number>` tracks which activity row should carry the session header (in newest-first iteration order, so the header always lands at the top of the group as the user scrolls). Two new state slots — `pendingSessionRevert: { sessionId, ops } | null` for the confirm-dialog payload and `revertingSessions: Set<string>` keyed by `sessionId` for per-session in-flight tracking — drive the interaction flow. Three new callbacks: `handleRevertSessionClick(sessionId)` takes a snapshot of that session's opRefs at click time and opens the confirm dialog with the exact count the user saw; `confirmRevertSession()` fires `revertOps({ ops })` with the snapshot, mirrors `handleUndo`'s success / `isNonReversibleError` / generic error paths via pluralized toasts; the confirm dialog reuses the existing `ConfirmDialog` primitive (`actionVariant="destructive"` — undo-can't-be-undone is the whole point). Session header renders as a `<li>` inside the existing `<ul>` visible **only** on the first-seen (most-recent) entry of each session **and only** when `sessionOps.length >= 2` (one-op sessions fall through to slice 3's per-entry Undo — no redundant session control). Button shows `Undo2` icon + "Revert session" text on desktop, collapses to a spinner while in-flight with `aria-busy` + `disabled` set in concert.
- **`src/lib/i18n.ts` (+17)** — 11 new keys under `agentAccess.revertSession.*`: `button`, `confirmTitle`, `confirmAction`, `failed`, `nonReversible` (single-form); `buttonAriaLabel_one`/`_other`, `confirmDescription_one`/`_other`, `success_one`/`_other` (i18next pluralized). Namespace matches slice 3's `agentAccess.undoAgentOp.*`.
- **`src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx` (+489 / −6)** — 10 new tests under a new `describe('... revert session')` block. Coverage:
  1. Header renders on first-seen when session has ≥ 2 undoable ops.
  2. Header HIDDEN when session has only 1 undoable op (slice 3's per-entry Undo still applies).
  3. Header HIDDEN on non-first-seen entries of the same session.
  4. Separate headers for two interleaved sessions (two different sessionIds in the same feed).
  5. User-authored and failed entries are excluded from the undoable count.
  6. Click opens confirm dialog with the correct `count`.
  7. Confirming invokes `revert_ops` with every undoable `opRef` of that session, in newest-first iteration order.
  8. Cancelling the confirm dialog does NOT invoke `revert_ops`.
  9. `NonReversible`-specific toast fires when `revert_ops` rejects with `{ kind: 'non_reversible' }` (wire-shape from `src-tauri/src/error.rs:153`).
  10. Axe clean on a session-grouped feed (2 sessions with headers + mixed agent/user/ok/err entries).

**Bookkeeping — REVIEW-LATER.md:**

- Removed FEAT-4h table-row entry + the full FEAT-4h detail section (28 lines).
- Updated FEAT-4 umbrella Status line to note all three v2 slices (1-2, 3, 4) shipped.
- Added UX-252 table-row + a detailed entry under the UX section documenting the double-revert UX issue that slice 3 + slice 4 inherit, with three fix-shape options (preferred: local `revertedOpKeys: Set<string>` state; fallback: refresh-from-backend; accept-as-is: backend-idempotent), a test plan, and a do-not list (no cross-session sync, no reuse of the existing in-flight markers, don't hide the reverted agent entry).

### Verification

- `npx vitest run src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx`: **47/47 passed** (+10 new).
- `prek run --all-files`: all 26 hooks green on the final run.
- `cargo nextest run`: not re-run this session — no Rust change.
- `cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests`: not re-run this session — no SQL change.
- `cargo test -- specta_tests --ignored`: not re-run this session — no IPC-surface change.

### Design decisions

- **No `OpRecord.origin` surfacing and no `reverse_agent_session_inner` backend command.** Both were listed in the compacted FEAT-4h entry as "optional polish". The shipped v1 is strictly frontend — reuses the existing `revert_ops` IPC (which already wraps `revert_ops_inner` with atomic multi-op transactions). Adding a backend session-based query would require either embedding a session ULID into the `origin` column text (a format change that needs its own decision) or adding a new column (schema change requiring user sign-off per AGENTS.md §"Architectural Stability"). Neither is needed for v1.
- **Snapshot-at-click for the confirm dialog.** `handleRevertSessionClick` copies `undoableBySession.get(sessionId)` into `pendingSessionRevert.ops` at click time; `confirmRevertSession` reads from `target.ops`, NOT re-queries the memo. If the activity ring rolls over between click and confirmation — or if new tool calls arrive — the user still reverts exactly what the count in the dialog promised.
- **Header gate at `sessionOps.length >= 2`.** Single-op sessions show no header; the per-entry Undo button from slice 3 is sufficient. Keeps the UI minimal chrome-wise and avoids the degenerate case of a "Revert session (1 action)" affordance that's semantically identical to the per-entry button.
- **Session header rendered as a `<li>` inside the existing `<ul>`.** Semantically a mild stretch (a `listitem` acting as a section header for subsequent `listitem`s) but axe-clean on the v1 scope. Alternative — a nested `<ul>` per session or a `<section>` / `<div>` wrapper — would require structural refactor of the feed. Deferred until a concrete a11y complaint lands.
- **i18next pluralization via `_one` / `_other` suffixes, `count` parameter.** Matches the standard i18next convention; future locales can override with their own pluralization rules (e.g. Polish needs `_few` / `_many`) without a component-level change.

### Architectural invariants respected (AGENTS.md)

- Op log append-only + CQRS split untouched — frontend-only change; reuses the existing `revert_ops` command + `revert_ops_inner` backend handler shipped in F-08.
- No new tables, op types, Zustand stores, materializer queues, or sync message types.
- No `#[allow]`, `biome-ignore`, `@ts-ignore`, non-null assertions, or weakened strict settings.
- `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` / `noImplicitReturns` / `useExplicitLengthCheck` / `useAwait` all satisfied.
- Radix UI + `cn()` + semantic tokens (`bg-muted/30`, `text-muted-foreground`, `border-b`) — no hardcoded Tailwind colors.

### Review summary (REVIEW-C, fresh-eyes `subagent_explore`)

APPROVE WITH NOTES. Zero P1/P2 blockers in the code — all critical correctness checks passed (memo dependencies, iteration order, snapshot semantics, in-flight state cleanup in `finally`, error-handling parity with slice 3, `isNonReversibleError` reuse, i18next pluralization). The **one** P2 raised was a UX inheritance from slice 3 (double-revert after success) which I chose to file as UX-252 rather than bolt onto slice 4's diff — the fix shape is cross-slice and deserves its own scoped session. P3s: explicit in-flight test for the session-level button (the equivalent exists for the per-entry button; parity test is nice-to-have), and the 100-entry ring cap edge case is already covered by an existing test.

### Notes for the next session

- **FEAT-4 umbrella is 100% v2 complete** — no open slices. v3 (FEAT-4i — Mobile) stays DEFERRED per its own entry. Future MCP work lands as separate REVIEW-LATER items, not under FEAT-4h.
- **UX-252 is the natural pickup** if the double-revert UX annoys you in practice — it's an S-cost pure-frontend change (one new `Set<string>` state + two memo filters + two new tests). Alternatively it can sit on the backlog; the backend handles double-undo safely for every reversible op type, so this is a UX-polish issue, not a correctness bug.
- **Per-tool privacy-safe activity summaries** still carry a TODO at `handle_tools_call` in `src-tauri/src/mcp/server.rs` — slice 3's follow-up. Separate from UX-252 and from FEAT-4h (which is closed).

## Session 463 — FEAT-4c emission wiring + FEAT-4h slice 3 activity-feed Undo (2026-04-23)

**FEAT-4h partially progressed (slice 3 shipped, slice 4 remains), 0 new REVIEW-LATER items filed.** Open-items count unchanged at 18; FEAT-4h's summary-table row recast (`Cost M → S`) and detail section compacted to describe only slice 4. Four parallel subagents (2 build + 2 review) pipelined; two rounds of orchestrator fixes (one frontend a11y polish + one round of `prek` auto-fixes for clippy/fmt/biome formatting). Single commit `3551423`.

A scope discovery up-front resized the session: grep showed `emit_tool_completion` had **zero production callers** — the activity-emission seam (FEAT-4c) was built in session 449 but never wired into the `handle_tools_call` dispatch, so slice 3's "Undo button on activity entries" premise was operating on a dead feed in production. Surfaced via `ask_user_question`; user chose "Wire emission + slice 3" (largest single-session scope that delivers an end-to-end feature), deferring slice 4 (per-session bulk revert) to a follow-up.

### What changed

**Backend — FEAT-4c tool-side emission + FEAT-4h slice 3 op-ref propagation:**

- **`src-tauri/src/mcp/last_append.rs` (NEW, 135 lines)** — task-local `Cell<Option<OpRef>>` sibling to `mcp::actor::ACTOR`. `record_append(op_ref)` calls `LAST_APPEND.try_with(|c| c.set(Some(op_ref)))`; `try_with` is the silent-no-op path for frontend-user writes that are outside any MCP dispatch scope. Avoids threading `OpRef` back through 840+ `*_inner` call sites (pre-existing signatures on `create_block_inner`/`edit_block_inner`/`set_property_inner`/`add_tag_inner`/`delete_block_inner` are untouched). 4 unit tests cover outside-scope no-op, inside-scope set, last-write-wins, and strict per-task isolation (two sibling `tokio::spawn` tasks with independent scopes).
- **`src-tauri/src/op_log.rs`** — `append_local_op_in_tx` pokes `crate::mcp::last_append::record_append(OpRef { device_id, seq })` immediately after the INSERT succeeds and before returning `OpRecord`. `OpRecord` struct and the two existing `query_as!(OpRecord, ...)` SELECTs are **deliberately unchanged** — FEAT-4h's text suggested adding `origin: String` to `OpRecord`, but the activity-feed-based Undo design makes `origin` unnecessary on the Rust type (the frontend sees `actorKind` + `agentName` directly in `ActivityEntry`). One new integration test pins `append_local_op_in_tx_populates_last_append_inside_scope`. Origin stamping from slice 1 is unaffected.
- **`src-tauri/src/mcp/activity.rs`** — `ActivityEntry` gains `session_id: String` + `op_ref: Option<OpRef>` at the end of the struct. `op_ref` carries `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` so the wire payload omits the key when absent (matches the frontend's `opRef?: OpRef | undefined` shape under `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). `emit_tool_completion` signature extended with the two new args. `Debug` impl renders `session_id` straight (opaque ULID, not PII) and `op_ref` via its own `Debug`. 3 new tests pin the wire shape, the skip-when-none behaviour, and the `emit_tool_completion` propagation.
- **`src-tauri/src/mcp/server.rs`** — `ConnectionState` gains `session_id: String` with a hand-written `Default` that calls `Ulid::new().to_string()` (not an empty string) so every connection opens with a fresh, stable session ULID that persists across every request on that socket. `handle_tools_call` now wraps `registry.call_tool(...)` in **two** nested task-local scopes — `ACTOR.scope(ctx, ...)` outer (unchanged, feeds `current_actor()` for origin stamping) and `LAST_APPEND.scope(Cell::new(None), ...)` inner (new, harvests the op_ref). `op_ref` is captured with `LAST_APPEND.with(std::cell::Cell::take)` **before the scope exits** so the Cell is still alive. The FEAT-4c emission block emits exactly one `emit_tool_completion` call per `tools/call` on both success and failure when `state.activity_ctx.is_some()`; error messages are Unicode-safely clipped to 200 chars via `err.to_string().chars().take(200).collect::<String>()`. Summary for MVP is the tool name; a `TODO(FEAT-4h-followup)` comment marks the spot for privacy-safe per-tool summaries (counts, block_ids — never content). Pre-existing test `handle_connection_accepts_activity_ctx_without_emitting_on_placeholder_tools_call` was renamed to `handle_connection_emits_activity_on_placeholder_error_tools_call` and its assertion inverted (empty ring → one `ActivityResult::Err` entry) — the only behaviour-flip this session. 6 new dispatch tests cover success emission, error emission, op_ref propagation for RW tools, op_ref absence for `activity_ctx=None`, session-id stability across two sequential requests on the same state, and the ULID length + uppercase invariant.
- **`src-tauri/src/mcp/tools_rw.rs`** — added `append_block_populates_last_append_inside_scope`: runs `ReadWriteTools::call_tool("append_block", ...)` inside an explicit `LAST_APPEND.scope(Cell::new(None), ...)` and asserts the captured `OpRef.device_id` matches the tools' configured device and `seq > 0`. No production code change in this file.
- **`src-tauri/src/mcp/tools_ro.rs`** — added `list_pages_does_not_populate_last_append`: complementary test pinning that RO tools leave the cell `None`.
- **`src-tauri/src/mcp/mod.rs`** — one-line `pub mod last_append;` declaration.

**Frontend — slice 3 per-entry Undo button + a11y fix:**

- **`src/components/AgentAccessSettingsTab.tsx` (+188 / −48)** — `ActivityEntry` interface extended with `sessionId: string` (required) + `opRef?: { device_id: string; seq: number } | undefined` (the nested OpRef stays snake_case to mirror the Rust-side `OpRef` type already exported by `src/lib/bindings.ts`). New `isNonReversibleError(err)` helper matches the `AppError::NonReversible` wire shape from `src-tauri/src/error.rs:153` — `{ kind: 'non_reversible', message: 'Non-reversible operation: ... cannot be undone' }`. New `undoingKeys: Set<string>` state keyed by `${device_id}:${seq}` tracks per-button in-flight state — the `try/catch/finally` pattern guarantees the key is always cleared. New `handleUndo(opRef)` callback fires `revertOps({ ops: [opRef] })` (reusing the existing `src/lib/tauri.ts:415` wrapper that already calls the backend's `revert_ops_inner`), fires `toast.success(t('agentAccess.undoAgentOp.success'))` on resolve, `toast.error(t('agentAccess.undoAgentOp.nonReversible'))` when the rejection matches `isNonReversibleError`, and `toast.error(t('agentAccess.undoAgentOp.failed'))` + `logger.error(...)` for every other rejection. Inline `Undo2` (lucide) / `Spinner` button rendered iff `actorKind === 'agent' && result.kind === 'ok' && opRef != null`; wrapped in a single `TooltipProvider` at the list level (perf-minded pattern from `HistoryListItem`). `disabled={isUndoing}` + `aria-busy={isUndoing}` set in concert.
- **a11y fix uncovered by the new axe test**: the pre-existing `<ul role="log" aria-label=...>` was axe-flagged because `<ul>`'s implicit `list` role conflicts with `<li>`-children under `log`. Moved `role="log"` + `aria-label` onto a wrapping `<div>` (also added explicit `aria-live="polite"` for clarity, even though `role="log"` implies it per WAI-ARIA spec). `<ul>` now keeps its implicit `list` role; `<li>` children satisfy `listitem` parent-role checks.
- **`src/lib/i18n.ts`** — 5 new keys under `agentAccess.undoAgentOp.*` (`ariaLabel`, `tooltip`, `success`, `failed`, `nonReversible`). English-only (existing fallback pattern).
- **`src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx` (+357 / −8)** — 28 pre-existing tests → 37 (+9 new). New `describe('... undo agent op')` block covers button visibility across agent+ok+opRef / user / error / opRef-missing, click invokes `revert_ops` with the correct OpRef, spinner + aria-busy during in-flight, generic error logs + toast, `non_reversible`-specific toast, axe zero-violation scan on a 5-entry mixed feed. New `makeActivityEntry(overrides)` fixture factory reuses the new required fields across every existing fixture via spread defaults.

### Verification

- `cargo nextest run` (full backend): **2614 passed, 2 skipped**. +22 new tests across `mcp::*` and `op_log::tests`. Zero regressions.
- `cargo nextest run mcp:: op_log::tests`: 220/220 passed.
- `cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests`: clean. Zero new `.sqlx/` cache entries — no new SQL queries introduced.
- `cargo test -- specta_tests --ignored`: not needed (no IPC surface change; `ActivityEntry` flows over the event bus, not the command surface, so `src/lib/bindings.ts` is unchanged).
- `npx vitest run src/components/__tests__/AgentAccessSettingsTab.test.tsx`: 37/37 passed.
- `prek run --all-files`: all 26 hooks green after one round of auto-fixes (biome formatter pulled a few multi-line constructs back to single-line; `cargo fmt` did the same on the Rust side; `cargo clippy::redundant_closure_for_method_calls` flagged `|c| c.take()` which I fixed to `std::cell::Cell::take` directly; `biome/useThrowOnlyError` flagged two tests throwing plain objects which I converted to `Object.assign(new Error(msg), { kind })` to preserve the wire shape assertions).

### Design decisions

- **Skip adding `origin: String` to `OpRecord`.** FEAT-4h's text prescribed this "so the frontend can distinguish agent rows", but the activity-feed-based Undo design doesn't need it — each `ActivityEntry` already carries `actorKind: 'agent' | 'user'` + `agentName` directly. Adding `origin` would have touched 2 `query_as!(OpRecord, ...)` sites + every test fixture that constructs `OpRecord` (16+ files) for no functional gain. File a follow-up only if a future surface (op history view, etc.) needs origin for filtering.
- **Task-local `LAST_APPEND` instead of signature changes.** 841 call sites for the 5 write `*_inner` functions — threading `OpRef` back through them would have been a sprawling diff. The task-local is analogous to the shipped `ACTOR` and invisible outside the MCP dispatch path. Pinned by the 4-test unit suite in `last_append.rs` (including the per-task isolation test with multi-threaded tokio).
- **No backend `reverse_agent_session_inner` in slice 3.** Slice 4 can ship as a pure-frontend change (group activity entries by `sessionId`, collect `opRef`s, call the existing `revertOps`) — the backend's `revert_ops_inner` already handles atomic multi-op transactions. A backend session-based query variant is only needed for the stale-ring edge case and is now noted as "optional polish" in the FEAT-4h detail. This avoids either changing the `origin` column format (text convention) or adding a new column (schema change requiring user approval).
- **Emission fires on both Ok and Err `tools/call` results.** The activity feed renders errors as first-class entries — hiding them would mask tool failures from the user. Pre-existing test that asserted "placeholder `tools/call` does NOT emit" was inverted to "placeholder `tools/call` emits one Err entry" — matches the FEAT-4h spec for both branches. The rename is the only behaviour flip in this session.
- **Summary is tool name for MVP.** Activity.rs test fixtures hint at richer strings like `"searched for '…' (0 results)"`; per-tool counts / ULID inclusion is a follow-up filed under FEAT-4h. The TODO comment sits directly above the `let summary = name.clone();` line so future sessions can't miss it. For MVP the `toolName` field (separate from `summary`) already carries the distinguishing info in the UI.
- **`role="log"` move + explicit `aria-live="polite"`.** The move was axe-driven (unblocks the axe test); the explicit `aria-live` is a best-practice polish (the reviewer flagged it as a P3). Both land together because they touch the same three lines.

### Architectural invariants respected (AGENTS.md)

- **Op log strictly append-only:** no DELETE/UPDATE on `op_log`. `LAST_APPEND` is an observer pattern — it only reads the `(device_id, seq)` that `append_local_op_in_tx` just committed.
- **CQRS split preserved:** no direct writes to materialized tables outside the materializer path. Slice 3 only adds an observer + dispatch-layer emission; materializer signatures are untouched.
- **sqlx compile-time queries:** no new queries; `.sqlx/` cache unchanged.
- **PRAGMA foreign_keys:** no new FKs introduced.
- **ULID uppercase normalization:** `Ulid::new().to_string()` produces Crockford base32 (uppercase) — the 26-char session ULID satisfies the hash-determinism invariant.
- **Recursive CTE invariant #9:** no new recursive CTEs.
- **Architectural stability (AGENTS.md §"Architectural Stability"):** no new tables, no new op types, no new Zustand stores, no new materializer queues, no new sync message types. Only new abstraction is the `LAST_APPEND` task-local (sibling to `ACTOR`, scope-local, invisible outside MCP dispatch).
- **No weakening of strict settings:** zero new `#[allow]`, `#[cfg_attr(..., allow(...))]`, `biome-ignore`, `@ts-ignore`, or non-null assertions. Existing `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` / `noImplicitReturns` / `unsafe_code = "deny"` all preserved.

### Review summary

- **REVIEW-A (backend technical):** APPROVE WITH NOTES. Zero P1/P2 blockers. All critical correctness checks passed — scope ordering (`ACTOR` outer / `LAST_APPEND` inner), UTF-8-safe error clipping (`.chars().take(200)` not `.bytes()`), op_ref capture inside the scope before it exits, session-ID stability across requests, `skip_serializing_if` on `op_ref`. Test coverage rated comprehensive (~65 new tests including edge cases). Task-local abstraction rated "defensible design choice — minimum-viable alternative to touching 840+ call sites".
- **REVIEW-B (frontend technical + UX):** APPROVE WITH NOTES. Zero P1/P2 blockers. Verified `revertOps({ ops: [opRef] })` against `src/lib/tauri.ts:415`, the `useThrowOnlyError` conversions, the `role="log"` move preserves live-region semantics, 44px touch-target via `size="sm"`'s `[@media(pointer:coarse)]:h-11` variant. P3 notes filed as future polish: explicit `aria-live` (I applied it this session), unmount-cleanup for in-flight undo promises, test coverage for double-undo of already-undone ops, and icon-only button discoverability on touch-only devices.

### Notes for the next session

- **Slice 4 is the natural next step** — per the REVIEW-LATER entry, it's a pure-frontend ~2h task (group activity entries by `sessionId`, add a "Revert session" affordance above each group, collect `opRef`s of every `agent+ok+opRef` entry, call `revertOps({ ops })`). No backend work required for v1.
- **Per-tool privacy-safe summaries** are a free-standing follow-up: replace `let summary = name.clone();` in `handle_tools_call` with a per-tool helper that pulls counts / ULIDs from the `Value` the tool returned. Summary strings like `"searched (12 results)"` or `"appended block 01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV"` are the target. Still no backend schema or trait change needed.
- **Emission now live** — any future `tools_ro.rs` / `tools_rw.rs` handler automatically emits on success + failure once dispatched through `handle_tools_call`. No additional per-tool work needed unless you want a richer summary.

## Session 462 — UX-251 resolved: preserve tabs + pageStack across view changes (2026-04-23)

**1 REVIEW-LATER item resolved (UX-251), 0 new filed.** Open-items count 19 → 18. Surgical Zustand-store behavioural fix that aligns `useNavigationStore.setView` with its own JSDoc contract. No structural change: no new tables, no new op types, no new stores, no new materializer queue, no new sync message type. Frontend-only, single commit (`d24d9d5`).

### What changed

- **`src/stores/navigation.ts` (−26, +6 net)** — `setView` collapsed to a single non-destructive `set({ currentView: view })`. `navigateToPage`'s date-routed branch now only flips `currentView` + `selectedBlockId`; the inline `resetPageEditorState` computation (which used to rebuild `tabs` to a single empty entry and zero `activeTabIndex` when leaving `page-editor`) is gone, along with the mirroring-comment block. `goBack`'s last-tab reset is untouched — semantically different (an explicit user action, not a side-effect of view change) per the UX-251 entry's "Do not" list.
- **`src/stores/__tests__/navigation.test.ts` (+138 net)** — 3 pre-existing destructive-behaviour assertions flipped (they used to pin `tabs.length === 1` after `setView('journal')`; they now pin `tabs.length === 3`, `activeTabIndex` unchanged, `pageStack` of every tab intact). 3 new tests added: a cross-view matrix asserting tab preservation across all 12 non-editor `setView` destinations (`journal`, `pages`, `agenda`, `tags`, `search`, `graph`, `sync`, `settings`, `conflicts`, `trash`, `history`, plus `page-editor` round-trip for the identity case), a `navigateToPage`-to-a-date-titled-page test that pins the journal-routed branch preserves `tabs.length === 3` + `activeTabIndex`, and an explicit JSDoc-contract alignment test named `setView_preserves_tabs_when_leaving_page_editor_matching_jsdoc_contract`.
- **`REVIEW-LATER.md`** (this session) — UX-251 removed (table row + detail section). `## Summary` count updated 19 → 18. Navigation-cluster sequencing preamble re-cast without UX-251 (heading, intro paragraph, recommended sequence renumbered 1-4, "What does NOT get redone" bullet rewritten to describe the invariant generically, cross-cluster hard-dependencies list with the `FEAT-7 → UX-251` edge removed). FEAT-7 detail pruned: user-visible-symptom parenthetical stripped of the UX-251 qualifier, `**Hard dependency:**` paragraph removed entirely, `Land this without UX-251` item removed from the `**Do not:**` list, `**Cost:**` line no longer mentions the UX-251 store delta, `**Status:**` no longer lists UX-251 as a blocker (sign-off on the inactive-in-non-editor-view visual direction is still required).

### Verification

- `npx vitest run src/stores/__tests__/navigation.test.ts`: green (assertion-flip + new cross-view matrix) — run at commit time by the prek vitest hook.
- `prek run --all-files`: all hooks green on the final push.
- No Rust, SQLx, specta, or sync-protocol surface touched — no `cargo nextest run`, no `cargo sqlx prepare`, no `cargo test -- specta_tests --ignored` required. Frontend-only.

### Design decisions

- **`goBack`'s last-tab reset left alone.** When the user explicitly navigates back from the root of their only tab, the existing behaviour (rebuild `tabs: [{ id, pageStack: [], label: '' }]` and flip to `pages` view) is an intentional user-initiated action, not a side-effect of `setView`. Touching it would widen the diff and risk changing navigation behaviour the UX-251 entry explicitly scoped out.
- **JSDoc left verbatim.** Line 56's `/** Switch sidebar view. DON'T clear tabs when leaving page-editor (preserve them). */` was aspirational; it is now the truth. No text change needed.
- **Precondition for FEAT-7 unlocked.** FEAT-7 (hoist `<TabBar />` to the app shell) is the natural follow-up — per the navigation-cluster sequencing plan in `REVIEW-LATER.md`, it requires user sign-off on the inactive-in-non-editor-view visual direction before implementation starts. No structural change in that item either.

### Architectural invariants respected (AGENTS.md)

- **Architectural stability:** no new tables, no new op types, no new Zustand store, no new materializer queue, no new sync message type. The `persist` middleware already serialises `tabs` / `activeTabIndex` / per-tab `pageStack`, so after this fix multi-tab state also outlives app restarts across view changes — which is what UX-117 originally promised.
- **No weakening of strict settings:** no new `biome-ignore`, no new `@ts-ignore`, no non-null assertions, no `as any`. Existing `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` / `noImplicitReturns` / `useExplicitLengthCheck` constraints are preserved.
- **`useBlockStore` / `usePageBlockStore` / `useJournalStore` untouched.** The bug is confined to the two `set(...)` calls inside `useNavigationStore.setView` + `navigateToPage`'s date-routed branch; no other store was involved, no selector was weakened.

### Notes for the next session

- **FEAT-7 is the natural next step** once the user signs off on the inactive-in-non-editor-view visual direction (per the FEAT-7 `**Status:**` line in `REVIEW-LATER.md`). All hard dependencies from outside the cluster are clear: tabs now survive view changes, so a hoisted TabBar will have real data to reflect in every view.
- **Mobile policy reminder (session-461 decisions, restated in FEAT-7 scope #7):** no TabBar on mobile at all, `openInNewTab` redirects to `navigateToPage` on mobile, `tabs: Tab[]` state persists across a desktop → mobile → desktop resize, `useIsMobile()` is the single detector.

## Session 461 — File UX-249 + UX-250 + UX-251 + FEAT-3 (re-file) + FEAT-7 + FEAT-8 + FEAT-9 + navigation-cluster plan (2026-04-22)

**7 new / re-filed REVIEW-LATER items + 1 cross-item sequencing preamble, 0 resolved.** Open-items count 12 → 19. All entries are user-surfaced bugs / feature requests fleshed out from repository evidence; none were implemented. No code changes. No tests run. No dependency bumps. Documentation-only session.

Two distinct topics in one session:

1. **Tags (UX-249 + UX-250).** User asked to flesh out two complaints — (1) tags are not clickable, (2) the tags view does not count blocks (only pages). Confirmed both claims against the code before writing the entries.
2. **Workspaces / tabs / spaces / recent-pages (UX-251 + FEAT-7 + FEAT-8 + FEAT-9 + FEAT-3 re-file + navigation-cluster sequencing plan).** User asked "how do I change between workspaces in Agaric?". First response wrongly claimed the concept didn't exist and offered to file a multi-vault FEAT from scratch — after the user pointed back to SESSION-LOG, a grep found F-36 (shipped session 318, browser-style editor tabs). After further user prompting ("there were a lot of discussions about having spaces"), a widened git-log grep found **FEAT-3 "Spaces"** — a full L-cost design filed in commit `13eb736` (Apr 18 2026) and silently dropped the same day in commit `83e44ce`, a MAINT refactor whose commit message never mentioned FEAT-3 and whose diff quietly removed the whole detail section. FEAT-3 has been re-filed verbatim in this session. User then asked for a Recent-pages strip ("breadcrumbs that work across views and modes, last three visited pages below the header"), clarified mobile ("in mobile no tab bar nor tabs"), and directed filing the overall plan. All of this is now in REVIEW-LATER.md under a new `### Navigation cluster — sequencing plan` preamble at the top of the FEAT section, plus per-item entries. Session-log lesson: grep SESSION-LOG and the git log before claiming a feature doesn't exist.

Seven items filed:

- **UX-249 — Inline tag chips in block content are not clickable (Cost: S, Status: READY).** Root cause is in the `renderTagRef` function of `src/components/RichContentRenderer.tsx:232-249` and in the editor-side `TagRef` NodeView at `src/editor/extensions/tag-ref.ts:74-114` — both render a `.tag-ref-chip` span with no `onClick` / `onKeyDown` and no `role="link"`, while their two sibling inline-token renderers (`renderBlockLink`, `renderBlockRef` in the same file) _do_ wire clicks through `ctx.onNavigate`. The shared CSS in `src/index.css:925-951` already ships hover + focus-visible affordances identical to the clickable siblings, so the visual contract promises interactivity that was never wired. Filed with a recommended fix (extend `RenderContext` with `onTagClick`, mirror `blockLinkProps` from `RichContentRenderer.tsx:251-273`, propagate to all caller sites, add a matching `addEventListener('click', …)` to the NodeView in `tag-ref.ts`), a test plan (render+click tests in `RichContentRenderer.test.tsx` and `tag-ref.test.ts`, axe pass with `role="link"`, deleted-tag case), an explicit do-not list (do **not** silently call `add_tag` from chip-click — that would be a semantic mutation from a purely-display surface and is UX-250's problem, not UX-249's), and a note that UX-249 + UX-250 should ship together because a clickable chip navigating to an empty filter panel is a worse UX than either bug in isolation.
- **UX-250 — Tags view `usage_count` excludes blocks with inline tag references (Cost: M, Status: READY, awaits design decision).** Traces the data-source divergence: `tags_cache.usage_count` is populated by `rebuild_tags_cache_impl` in `src-tauri/src/cache/tags.rs:34-63` purely from the `block_tags` join table; `block_tags` is only written by the `add_tag_inner` op path in `src-tauri/src/commands/tags.rs:33-121`; the only frontend call site of `add_tag` is `PageHeader.tsx:353` via `useBlockTags(pageId)`, so in practice only page-level tagging contributes. Meanwhile the `@` / `#` autocomplete in `src/editor/extensions/at-tag-picker.ts` creates `tag_ref` inline nodes stored as `#[ULID]` in `blocks.content` and writes nothing to `block_tags` — and the sibling content-parser `reindex_block_links` (`src-tauri/src/cache/block_links.rs:34-38`, regex at `cache/mod.rs:44-45`) intentionally matches only `[[ULID]]` / `((ULID))`, never `#[ULID]`. Downstream fallout: `query_by_tags` (`src-tauri/src/tag_query/query.rs:13-84`) returns zero results for inline-only tags; `TagFilterPanel` therefore shows no matches; the MCP `list_tags` + `query_by_tags` tools inherit the same blind spot. Filed with two fix shapes — Option A (new `block_tag_refs` derived-state table parsed from content, unioned into `usage_count` and `resolve_expr`, with migration backfill; mirrors `block_links`) and Option B (cosmetic second count column, storage unchanged, filter panel stays broken) — plus open questions (explicit + inline co-existence counting rule, whether `list_tags_for_block` broadens, deleted-tag cascade behaviour, 100K-block migration cost), a do-not list (no silent `add_tag` from the editor autocomplete, preserve explicit-vs-inline distinction across separate tables, don't skip migration backfill), and a note that Option A adds a new table and therefore needs explicit user approval per AGENTS.md §"Architectural Stability" before any implementation session.
- **UX-251 — `setView` destroys all tabs + `pageStack` on exit from page-editor (Cost: S, Status: READY).** `src/stores/navigation.ts:113-126` unconditionally resets `tabs: [{ id: '0', pageStack: [], label: '' }]` and `activeTabIndex: 0` whenever `setView` moves from `'page-editor'` to any other view; the same reset is duplicated in `navigateToPage`'s date-routed branch at lines 147-158. The JSDoc at line 56 literally says `DON'T clear tabs when leaving page-editor (preserve them)` — the implementation does the opposite. Result: opening 3 tabs, clicking "Journal" in the sidebar, and clicking back loses all three tabs. Fix is surgical — drop the destructive branch from `setView`, drop the `resetPageEditorState` computation from `navigateToPage`'s date-routed branch, leave everything else untouched. `goBack`'s last-tab reset stays (semantically different — an explicit user action, not a view-change side effect). Filed with a cross-view test matrix (every sidebar destination preserves tabs), a `goBack` do-not-touch guardrail, and an architectural note confirming no structural change (pure Zustand-store behavioural fix; `persist` middleware already serialises `tabs`, so after the fix multi-tab state also outlives app restarts across view changes, which is what UX-117 originally promised).
- **FEAT-7 — App-shell TabBar (tabs visible across every sidebar view) (Cost: M, Status: READY, depends on UX-251).** F-36 landed the full tabs model (session 318) but `<TabBar />` renders only inside `ViewRouter`'s `case 'page-editor'` branch at `src/App.tsx:466-482`. Other sidebar views (Journal, Pages, Agenda, Tags, Search, Graph, Sync, Settings, Conflicts, Trash, History) show no tab strip — users never see how many tabs they have open, which one is active, or have any visual switcher to jump between them. Scope: hoist `<TabBar />` out of the view router into the app shell (render it between the top `<header>` at `App.tsx:917-930` and the `ViewHeaderOutletSlot` at `App.tsx:939`, full-width sticky row), wire `switchTab` to flip `currentView` back to `'page-editor'` when clicked from a non-editor view, add an inactive visual state when `currentView !== 'page-editor'`, keep the `tabs.length <= 1` autohide guard (explicit user decision in this session — filed with a comment placeholder so future cleanup passes don't remove the guard). Explicitly does NOT add a "+" button (paired decision with keeping autohide — a "+" on a hidden bar is invisible). Expand `condition: 'keyboard.condition.inEditor'` on `Ctrl+T` / `Ctrl+W` / `Ctrl+Tab` / `Ctrl+Shift+Tab` to be shell-wide. Hard dependency on UX-251 — hoisting without the tab-preservation fix would just show an always-empty bar after any view change.
- **FEAT-8 — Active-tab dropdown switcher, desktop-only (Cost: S, Status: READY, depends on FEAT-7).** Polish for users at 8+ tabs **on desktop**: clicking the active-tab label opens a Radix `DropdownMenu` listing every open tab (title + optional breadcrumb showing the next page in the tab's `pageStack`), active tab checkmarked, per-row close X. Pure UI extension in `TabBar.tsx` using the existing `ui/dropdown-menu.tsx` primitive — no store changes, no new primitives, no new tests beyond the component-level dropdown behaviours. **Scoped desktop-only after the session-461 mobile decision;** the earlier narrow-viewport-dropdown-fallback shape is obsolete because mobile no longer renders any tab UI.
- **FEAT-9 — Recent-pages strip (MRU row below the TabBar, desktop-only, Cost: S, Status: READY after FEAT-7).** New `src/stores/recent-pages.ts` Zustand store with `recordVisit` (MRU dedup, max 10 retained, `persist`-backed); hook into `useNavigationStore.navigateToPage` so every page navigation records a visit; new `src/components/RecentPagesStrip.tsx` renders a responsive grid (`repeat(auto-fit, minmax(120px, 180px))`) of chips between the hoisted TabBar and `ViewHeaderOutletSlot`. Exclude the currently-open page from the strip; click navigates, Ctrl/Cmd+click opens in new tab. Mobile (`useIsMobile() === true`) renders nothing — same minimal-chrome rule as tabs. Name deliberately not "breadcrumbs" — that's reserved for the two existing hierarchical features (namespace-title breadcrumb in `PageHeader.tsx:459-484` + block-zoom breadcrumb in `BlockZoomBar.tsx`). Global v1; will become `recentPagesBySpace: Record<SpaceId, string[]>` under FEAT-3 phase 3.
- **FEAT-3 — Spaces: partition pages into user-defined contexts, re-filed (Cost: L, Status: DESIGN).** Originally filed in commit `13eb736` on 2026-04-18 and **silently dropped the same day** in commit `83e44ce` — a MAINT refactor ("lower cognitive complexity in 6 frontend sites") whose commit message never mentioned FEAT-3 and whose diff quietly removed both the summary-table row and the full detail section. No SESSION-LOG entry documented either the filing or the removal. Process breach per AGENTS.md "Subagent Workflow" step 7 ("LOG — Update SESSION-LOG.md"). Re-filed **verbatim** from `git show 13eb736:REVIEW-LATER.md` in this session at user direction ("there were a lot of discussions about having spaces and i think we should have implemented it"). Design is unchanged from the original: space = page block with `is_space = true` marker property + `space` ref property on every non-space page, uses existing `property_definitions` / `block_properties` (AGENTS.md "primary extension point") — no new tables, no new op types, no sync-protocol change. User decisions locked in: per-space journal, per-space tabs (`tabsBySpace: Record<SpaceId, Tab[]>`), no "All" pseudo-space. 6-phase rollout, 8 open questions (default-space bootstrap strategy, space deletion semantics, cross-space link picker, graph rendering, export, first-boot UI, default-space count, search operator) must be resolved before phase 1 starts. Phase 3 of FEAT-3's rollout now also covers the FEAT-9 `recentPages` → `recentPagesBySpace` refactor in the same session. SpaceSwitcher replaces the static branding block at `src/App.tsx:798-805`; visible on both desktop and mobile (spaces are a higher-level scope than tabs). A provenance note at the top of the detail entry records the prior silent drop for future audit.
- **Navigation-cluster sequencing plan — new section preamble at the top of `## FEAT`** (not a numbered item; lives at `### Navigation cluster — sequencing plan (FEAT-3 + FEAT-7/8/9 + UX-251)`). Captures the recommended delivery order (UX-251 → FEAT-7 → FEAT-8/FEAT-9 → FEAT-3), what happens to FEAT-7/8/9 when FEAT-3 phase 3 lands (one-line data-source swaps — their DOM / styling / mobile handling stays), what does NOT get redone (MRU dedup, visual treatments, `setView` fix), the cross-cluster mobile policy (no tabs on mobile, no dropdown, no recent strip; space switcher yes), and hard dependencies. Single source of truth for "which of these items can ship independently vs together" so a future session doesn't serialise wrong.

Items **not** filed this session despite the analysis identifying them:

- **Removing the `tabs.length <= 1` autohide.** User explicitly opted out: "add all these three tasks except removing the autohide". FEAT-7's entry pins this choice with a code-comment requirement so it doesn't regress in future cleanup sweeps.
- **PROC-style item for the silent FEAT-3 drop in `83e44ce`.** The prior drop is recorded in FEAT-3's provenance note + in this session log, which is enough audit trail. A dedicated PROC-* entry would just restate what the provenance note already says. Not filing to avoid noise.

Mid-session refinements after initial filing:

- **Mobile decision on tabs — "in mobile no tab bar nor tabs".** User explicitly opted out of every tab affordance on mobile. FEAT-7 and FEAT-8 updated in-session to reflect this. Concretely: when `useIsMobile()` from `src/hooks/use-mobile.ts` (`viewport < 768px`) returns true, the TabBar does not render at all (no autohide, no dropdown collapse — just absent), `openInNewTab` is wrapper-redirected to `navigateToPage` (replace instead of parallel-open), `PageHeaderMenu`'s "Open in new tab" item is hidden, and `Ctrl+T/W/Tab` shortcuts are n/a (no keyboard). Underlying `tabs: Tab[]` state persists across desktop ↔ mobile resize so a user who resizes from 1440 px → 600 px → 1440 px gets their multi-tab state back intact. FEAT-7 scope item #7 carries the full spec; FEAT-8 tagged explicitly as "desktop-only" in its title and scope. The same `useIsMobile()` hook already drives `ui/sidebar.tsx` and `useBlockDnD.ts` — no second mobile detector. FEAT-9 inherits the same mobile-hidden policy; FEAT-3 space switcher deliberately does NOT (spaces are a scope higher than tabs).

New `### Navigation cluster — sequencing plan` preamble at the top of `## FEAT` (between the section header and FEAT-3) threading UX-251 → FEAT-7 → FEAT-8/FEAT-9 → FEAT-3 and documenting mobile policy + cross-cluster dependencies. No drift. No `BUG-*` / `TEST-*` / `MAINT-*` items currently open. `FEAT-6` deliberately skipped (already used for snapshot-driven catch-up in a prior session).

### Verification

- `grep -oE "^\| (FEAT\|PERF\|PUB\|UX)-…" REVIEW-LATER.md | sort` vs `grep -oE "^### (FEAT\|PERF\|PUB\|UX)-…" REVIEW-LATER.md | sort` — identical 19-line output; summary ↔ detail parity holds.
- `prek run markdownlint --files REVIEW-LATER.md SESSION-LOG.md`: **Passed**.
- No Rust / TS / SQL changes — `cargo nextest run`, `vitest`, `cargo sqlx prepare`, bindings regen all intentionally skipped (nothing they cover was touched).
- `git status` clean except for the two intended files (`REVIEW-LATER.md`, `SESSION-LOG.md`).
- FEAT-3 re-file cross-check: `diff <(git show 13eb736:REVIEW-LATER.md | sed -n '/^### FEAT-3 —/,/^---$/p' | head -95) <(sed -n '/^### FEAT-3 —/,/^### FEAT-4 —/p' REVIEW-LATER.md | head -100)` — substantive content preserved; only the provenance note + the FEAT-9 cross-reference + the SpaceSwitcher-replaces-branding-block detail are additions on top of the verbatim restore.

### Investigation notes (anchor to repo evidence, not user wording)

Because every user prompt in this session was compact, the entries are written from direct code-reading rather than paraphrased from the prompt.

**Tags topic (UX-249 + UX-250):**

- **Confirmed UX-249 scope** by reading both render paths (`RichContentRenderer.renderTagRef` for the static view, `TagRef.addNodeView` for the focused editor) and the shared CSS. Also confirmed the clickable siblings' wiring (`renderBlockLink` / `renderBlockRef` both delegate to `ctx.onNavigate`) so the entry can point to a concrete "do it like these two" template rather than abstract hand-waving. The fact that the `PageTagSection` tag badges in the page header are _also_ non-clickable (they only expose a remove-X button) is noted as a secondary surface but isn't the primary claim — the primary claim is about inline chips in block content, which is the surface that looks like it should navigate.
- **Confirmed UX-250 scope** by following the write path (`PageHeader.tsx:353` → `useBlockTags` → `addTag` IPC → `add_tag_inner` → `INSERT INTO block_tags`) and the read path (`TagList.tsx` → `listTagsByPrefix` → `list_tags_by_prefix_inner` → `SELECT … FROM tags_cache` populated by `rebuild_tags_cache_impl` which `COUNT(*) FROM block_tags`). Also verified the inline-tag path lands in content only (`at-tag-picker.ts` → `insertTagRef` → `tag_ref` ProseMirror node → markdown `#[ULID]` in `blocks.content`) with no call to `add_tag`. And verified `reindex_block_links` explicitly excludes tag tokens from its regex, so the "just mirror what block_links does" recommendation is structurally sound.
- **Tangential "tags are not clickable" surface identified but scoped out of UX-249.** Clicking a tag in the `/tags` view (the `TagList` component) _is_ wired — `src/App.tsx:389` calls `navigateToPage(tagId, tagName)` — but it routes the tag block through the page-editor as if the tag were a page. That is almost certainly a separate UX problem (a tag block is not a page; the destination view semantics are unclear), but the user's complaint was specifically about inline chips, so it wasn't filed in this session. Mentioning here so it doesn't get re-discovered later as "UX-249 didn't cover it" — it was deliberate scope discipline, not an oversight. File a sibling entry when/if it comes up (likely `UX-252`, since `UX-251` now belongs to the tabs-destroy bug).

**Tabs topic (UX-251 + FEAT-7 + FEAT-8) — initial mistake + correction:**

- **First response to "how do I change between workspaces" was wrong.** Replied "Agaric doesn't have workspaces" and proposed filing a multi-vault FEAT from scratch — without grepping SESSION-LOG first. The user explicitly pointed back with "check session log … you will find references to workspaces or something else". That led to F-36 (session 318: "Multiple editor tabs" — `navigation.ts` + `TabBar.tsx` + `Ctrl+T/W/Tab` keyboard surface + `Open in New Tab` kebab action + single-TipTap-instance invariant preserved) and UX-117 (persistence wiring). Tabs are the workspace-like concept and have been in the app since session 318.
- **Lesson recorded for future sessions:** *grep SESSION-LOG (and the codebase) before claiming a feature doesn't exist.* The codebase grep for "workspace" found nothing because the feature is called "tabs" — a purely terminological mismatch. The session log grep for "tab" / "Tab" / F-36 finds it immediately.
- **Confirmed UX-251 scope** by reading `src/stores/navigation.ts:113-126` (`setView`) and lines 147-158 (`navigateToPage` date-routed branch) directly. Both branches unconditionally reset `tabs: [{ id: '0', pageStack: [], label: '' }]` and `activeTabIndex: 0` whenever leaving page-editor. Line 56's JSDoc (`DON'T clear tabs when leaving page-editor (preserve them)`) actively contradicts the implementation. `goBack`'s last-tab reset at lines 206-214 is structurally similar but semantically distinct (an explicit user action, not a side effect of view change) — excluded from the UX-251 fix on purpose.
- **Confirmed FEAT-7 scope** by reading `src/App.tsx:466-482` (`<TabBar />` only inside the `case 'page-editor'` branch of `ViewRouter`), `src/App.tsx:915-939` (shell header + `ViewHeaderOutletSlot` as the natural hoist target), and `src/components/TabBar.tsx:50` (the `tabs.length <= 1` autohide guard the user explicitly wants preserved). The shell-level insertion point between the top `<header>` and the `ViewHeaderOutletSlot` is compatible with the existing UX-198 sticky-header pattern.
- **Confirmed FEAT-8 scope** by reading the existing `ui/dropdown-menu.tsx` primitive which covers keyboard nav + focus trap + escape-dismiss out of the box. Polish item; no new primitives required.
- **No work filed on the third "tags are not clickable" surface (TagList navigation target) even though it resurfaced indirectly during the tabs investigation.** Keeping UX-252 as a candidate future-session entry, not filed preemptively.

### Architectural invariants respected (AGENTS.md)

- Documentation-only session — no op log writes, no schema migrations, no Zustand stores added, no new materializer queues. None of the 9 key architectural invariants apply.
- **AGENTS.md §"Architectural Stability"** is flagged _within_ two of the items:
  - **UX-250**'s Option A adds a derived-state table (`block_tag_refs`), so the item's Status line explicitly requires a user decision on Option A vs Option B before implementation.
  - **FEAT-7**'s navigation-store semantic shift (tabs survive view change) + the hoist from view-router to app shell is flagged in the entry as needing user sign-off even though it adds no tables. The "click-a-tab-from-non-editor restores page-editor" behaviour is a cross-view navigation change; even though it's additive it is a deliberate user-experience policy.
- The entries themselves are additive documentation; the architectural changes they describe are not yet approved. UX-251 is a pure bug fix (restores the JSDoc contract) and does not require sign-off.
- AGENTS.md's top invariant — "No changes to this file (AGENTS.md) without explicit user approval. Ever." — respected; AGENTS.md untouched.

### Notes for the next session

- **UX-249 + UX-250 batchable.** UX-249 alone lands a clickable chip whose navigation target is empty under UX-250 — the user-visible improvement only materialises once both are fixed. Expected effort: S + M in one session, with UX-250 needing the user's Option A vs B decision up-front. Recommend asking the user which option before launching subagents.
- **UX-251 + FEAT-7 batchable (and recommended to pair).** UX-251 lands the tab-preservation fix (surgical, ~30 min of edits + ~1 h of tests). FEAT-7 hoists `<TabBar />` to the shell so the preserved tabs become visible from every view. Landing them together is the single user-observable delivery — UX-251 alone is silent from the user's perspective (you only see tabs survive when you have the shell-level bar to watch), FEAT-7 alone is ineffective (the bar hoists to an empty state because tabs get destroyed). Recommend one M session for UX-251 + FEAT-7 combined, followed by a separate S session for FEAT-8 after the combined landing is live.
- **FEAT-8 is polish.** Can wait; don't block the UX-251 + FEAT-7 landing on it.
- **`UX-252` and `FEAT-9` are the next free IDs** in each sequence (UX-251 and FEAT-8 are the session-461 ceilings; FEAT-6 was used prior to this session for the snapshot-driven catch-up feature, so the sequence jumps from FEAT-5g → FEAT-7).
- **A "tags are not clickable" third surface** — clicking a tag in `TagList` routes through `navigateToPage` to a page-editor on a tag block, which is semantically odd — was deliberately not filed this session per the user's wording. If that becomes user-visible friction, file as `UX-252` rather than folding into UX-249.
- **If a future session decides to unify the tag-chip and tag-list navigation targets**, `TagFilterPanel` is the natural shared destination. `src/App.tsx:389` would change from `navigateToPage(tagId, tagName)` to whatever opens the filter panel pre-populated.
- **Keyboard-condition expansion work for FEAT-7.** `src/lib/keyboard-config.ts:365-400` has `condition: 'keyboard.condition.inEditor'` on all four tab shortcuts. Under FEAT-7 they become shell-wide. Audit the current condition-resolver to confirm no other shortcuts collide when the condition relaxes (e.g., a `Ctrl+T` shortcut in a non-editor view that conflicts — unlikely but verify).
